feed.title: Does Freeport-McMoRan’s (FCX) Grasberg Extension Quietly Redefine Its Long-Term Asset Strategy?
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan recently reported earnings while managing the operational consequences of the 2025 Grasberg mudslide. A new Memorandum of Understanding with the Indonesian government has extended mining rights at Grasberg, allowing for a phased underground restart and securing the company's long-term access to this crucial asset. This development potentially redefines Freeport-McMoRan's asset strategy by reducing long-term regulatory uncertainty, though operational and political risks in Indonesia remain.

feed.title: Is Nabors Industries (NBR) Still Attractively Priced After Its 90% One Year Rally
feed.summary: Nabors Industries (NBR) has seen a 90% rally over the past year, prompting an assessment of its current valuation. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is significantly undervalued by 66.7%, with an intrinsic value of $236.66 per share compared to its current price of US$78.73. However, its P/E ratio of 4.7x is higher than Simply Wall St's proprietary "Fair Ratio" of 3.3x, indicating it might be overvalued by this metric.

feed.title: ONEOK (OKE) Is Up 5.0% After Dividend Hike And Debt Reduction Progress Has The Bull Case Changed?
feed.summary: ONEOK (OKE) recently announced strong financial results for Q4 and full-year 2025, including increased revenue and net income, alongside a 4% dividend hike and significant debt reduction. The company's 2026 earnings guidance and progress on integrating acquisitions strengthen its investment narrative, though earnings remain sensitive to commodity spread volatility. Investors should weigh this positive momentum against potential risks from commodity market fluctuations.

feed.title: New hybrid system brings electric efficiency to regional aircraft
feed.summary: RTX is developing a new combined thermal/electric propulsion system for regional turboprop aircraft, aiming to significantly boost efficiency. This hybrid system, which can be retrofitted into existing aircraft, integrates a Pratt & Whitney turboprop engine with a Collins Aerospace electric motor, allowing both to simultaneously drive the propeller shaft. The goal is to reduce fuel consumption by 30% and maintenance costs by 20%, while also enabling the use of 100% Sustainable Aviation Fuel.

feed.title: Skycorp Solar Revamps Board with New Independent Director After March Resignation
feed.summary: Skycorp Solar Group Ltd. announced changes to its board of directors in early March 2026. Following the resignation of director Shibo Feng, Kwok Hung Bell Wong was appointed as an independent director and chair of the audit committee, bringing the board to five directors, three of whom are independent. The company's stock, (PN), currently holds a "Hold" rating with a $0.43 price target, though TipRanks' AI Analyst shows a "Neutral" sentiment due to weakening financials and a bearish technical trend.

feed.title: Regional supply chain disruption escalates, Expeditors International cautions
feed.summary: Expeditors International has issued a warning about escalating regional supply chain disruptions due to ongoing operational challenges in the Middle East. The company advises stakeholders to seek updated information and contact local representatives for guidance amidst heightened pressure on transportation networks. This situation reflects broader patterns of disruption impacting air and sea routes, as previously assessed by Expeditors International regarding persistent shipping delays.

feed.title: Did ONE Gas' (OGS) New Equity Programs Quietly Redefine Its Capital Markets Playbook?
feed.summary: ONE Gas (OGS) recently filed for a $225 million at-the-market common stock offering and a $41.21 million dividend reinvestment plan shelf registration, signaling a focus on funding and capital market engagement. These equity moves aim to support significant infrastructure investments and capital expenditures, which are crucial for the regulated gas utility's growth and system integrity commitments. While these funding tools could alleviate balance sheet pressure, investors are cautioned that high capital spending without timely regulatory cost recovery could impact free cash flow and long-term profitability.

feed.title: 3 Dividend Stocks With Monster Yields Are Already Up 50% in 2026
feed.summary: This article highlights three energy-related dividend stocks—Nordic American Tankers (NAT), Frontline PLC (FRO), and Noble Corporation (NE)—that have seen significant share price appreciation (over 50%) in 2026 while offering substantial dividend yields. The strong performance is attributed to tightened tanker supply due to geopolitical events and aging fleets, as well as increasing offshore drilling contracts. Each company's specific catalysts and financial fundamentals are detailed, emphasizing their current market positions and future outlook.

feed.title: What Li Auto (LI)'s Delivery Stabilization and AI Robot Push Means For Shareholders
feed.summary: Li Auto (LI) reported stabilized vehicle deliveries in February 2026 and advanced its AI and robotics initiatives, including the OTA 8.3 software upgrade and extensive supercharging network. While delivery stabilization and new product launches like the Li L9 aim to boost volume and justify R&D spending, investors should be aware of increasing price competition and rising capital intensity. The company's future hinges on its ability to balance significant investments in AI and BEV technology with sustained sales growth in China.

feed.title: Ford recalls 1.74 million of its cars over rearview camera display issues
feed.summary: Ford has recalled nearly 1.74 million vehicles in the U.S. due to software issues affecting rearview camera displays. The recalls include 2021-2026 Ford Broncos and 2021-2024 Ford Edges facing infotainment system overheating, and 2020-2022 Ford Escapes, Lincoln Corsairs, 2020-2024 Lincoln Aviators, and Explorers which may display inverted rearview images. While no injuries or accidents have been reported, the NHTSA warns that these issues could increase crash risks, and Ford is working on software updates and remedies for the affected models.

feed.title: Ford recalls 1.74 million of its cars over rearview camera display issues
feed.summary: Ford is recalling nearly 1.74 million vehicles in the U.S. due to two separate rearview camera display issues that could increase crash risks. One recall affects certain 2021-2026 Ford Broncos and 2021-2024 Ford Edges where the infotainment system may overheat, preventing the rearview image from displaying. The second recall impacts 2020-2022 Ford Escapes and Lincoln Corsairs, and 2020-2024 Lincoln Aviators and Explorers, where the rearview image may appear flipped or inverted.

feed.title: Edison International targets 5% to 7% EPS growth through 2030 while reaffirming 2028 outlook
feed.summary: Edison International (EIX) announced its long-term financial targets, aiming for a 5% to 7% annual adjusted EPS growth rate through 2030 from a 2023 base. The company also reaffirmed its existing 2024 and 2028 adjusted EPS guidance ranges, projecting capital investments of $43 billion through 2028 and $63 billion through 2030 to support decarbonization and grid modernization. These targets will be discussed further during the company's investor call today.

feed.title: Waste Management Broadens Services With Stericycle Deal And Energy Investments
feed.summary: Waste Management (NYSE: WM) is expanding its services by acquiring Stericycle, adding regulated medical waste to its portfolio, and investing in renewable energy and recycling automation. These strategic moves aim to diversify its business, enhance resource recovery, and align executive compensation with long-term goals. Investors will monitor execution risk, integration of Stericycle, and the impact of these capital expenditures on future profitability and cash flow.

feed.title: Former U.S. trade chief Lighthizer resigns from Trump Media board
feed.summary: Former U.S. trade representative Robert Lighthizer has resigned from the board of directors of Trump Media & Technology, effective Friday. The Securities and Exchange Commission filing stated his departure was not due to disagreements with the company's management or board. Trump Media, known for its Truth Social platform, has recently shifted its strategy towards fusion energy through a merger with TAE Technologies.

feed.title: Alliant Energy Corp. Hits New 52-Week High at $72.65
feed.summary: Alliant Energy Corp. has reached a new 52-week high of $72.65, demonstrating strong performance in the power industry with a 44.03% increase over the past year. The company boasts a market capitalization of $18,280 million, a P/E ratio of 21.00, and an attractive dividend yield of 141.78%. Its financial health is further supported by a debt-to-equity ratio of 1.58 and a return on equity of 11.04%.

feed.title: Enphase Energy Broadens Revenue Mix With Commercial And Grid Opportunities
feed.summary: Enphase Energy is expanding its revenue streams beyond residential solar to include commercial and grid opportunities. The company has partnered with Capital Good Fund for mission-driven solar projects, launched the IQ9 Commercial Microinverter, and is contributing IQ Batteries to virtual power plant initiatives in Texas. While the stock has seen a significant decline over the past three years, these new ventures aim to diversify its business mix and strengthen its position in the broader clean energy market.

feed.title: Cleveland Steel Showdown: Ohio Targets Cliffs And Duck Creek Over Radioactive Mess
feed.summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost has filed a lawsuit against Cleveland-Cliffs Inc. and Duck Creek Energy, accusing them of mishandling radioactive waste at Cleveland-Cliffs' Cleveland Works steel mill. The complaint alleges improper disposal of brine, oil, and radioactive materials, leading to contamination and potential environmental violations. This state action escalates a prior legal dispute where Duck Creek sued Cleveland-Cliffs, and seeks to hold both companies accountable for cleanup and compliance.

feed.title: Genesis Energy launches $500M senior notes offering due 2034
feed.summary: Genesis Energy announced a private placement offering of $500 million in senior unsecured notes due 2034. The company plans to use the net proceeds from this offering, along with cash on hand, to repurchase all of its outstanding 8.000% senior unsecured notes due 2027 through a tender offer. This move aims to refinance existing debt and manage its maturity profile.

feed.title: Pony AI Expands Reach with Strategic Collaborations
feed.summary: Pony AI Inc. is expanding its market presence through strategic collaborations, including the acquisition of FreeNow for its European rideshare influence and a partnership with a major automotive manufacturer for autonomous vehicle development. Despite these efforts and investments in AI, the company's stock has been trading down due to regulatory concerns and its financial fundamentals show a challenging market position with weak profitability ratios. The outlook remains negative without clear catalysts or improved fundamentals, advising investors to be cautious.

feed.title: Is Canadian Solar (CSIQ) Quietly Recasting Its Identity Around Capital‑Recycled Storage Projects?
feed.summary: Canadian Solar (CSIQ) is shifting its focus towards higher-value energy storage projects through capital recycling, as evidenced by its subsidiary Recurrent Energy selling the Fort Duncan Battery Storage facility and its e-STORAGE arm delivering a battery system in Japan. This strategy aims to monetize completed storage assets and reinvest in an 80 GWh development pipeline, supporting the transition to integrated solar-plus-storage systems. While this move aligns with some optimistic analyst forecasts for future growth, the company still faces challenges from cost pressures and policy uncertainties that could impact profitability.

feed.title: Is Baytex’s 2025 Loss and Asset Shift Altering The Investment Case For Baytex Energy (TSX:BTE)?
feed.summary: Baytex Energy Corp. reported a full-year 2025 net loss of C$603.78 million, reversing from a previous profit, and announced plans to sell its U.S. Eagle Ford assets to focus on Canadian operations. Despite the loss and leadership changes, the company maintains a strong balance sheet with C$857 million in cash and an undrawn C$750 million credit facility, supporting its continued investment in Duvernay growth, heavy oil drilling, dividends, and buybacks. The article suggests investors consider multiple viewpoints due to the company's dependency on supportive oil prices and the wide range of fair value estimates.

feed.title: Barclays Ups CF Industries Price Target Amid Geopolitical Tensions

Despite a consensus "Hold" rating from other analysts, CF Industries exhibits strong financial health with robust profitability ratios and a bullish technical pattern. The company's strategic position in North American nitrogen production and clean energy efforts are key factors contributing to its positive outlook, tempered by some insider sales that warrant caution.

feed.title: ArcBest filing notes ‘ongoing softness’ in the U.S. freight sector

The company expects a less-than-typical increase in its first-quarter operating ratio, projecting lower net income and revenue compared to the previous year.

feed.title: Does Phillips 66’s (PSX) Higher Dividend Reveal a Deeper Shift in Its Capital Allocation Priorities?

Investors should consider this against the backdrop of refining margins, asset reliability, and the broader macro environment impacting its revenue and earnings forecasts.

feed.title: Southwest flight diverts to Atlanta airport after ‘security matter,’ airline says
feed.summary: A Southwest Airlines flight from Nashville to Fort Lauderdale was diverted to Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport due to a "possible security matter." A passenger was detained by Atlanta police, but the FBI determined there was no credible threat, and no charges were filed. Travelers were later moved to another plane to continue to their destination.

feed.title: Entergy Arkansas rate filing outlines investments, rate hikes and decreases
feed.summary: Entergy Arkansas has filed documents with the Arkansas Public Service Commission outlining both rate increases and decreases for customers. A new state law, the Generating Arkansas Jobs Act, will allow for phased-in rate increases to cover investments in new generation facilities totaling $5.77 per month for typical residential customers. However, a federal nuclear production tax credit will provide an estimated $1.55 monthly decrease, leading to a net increase of $4.22 per month.

feed.title: March 25: Coal plant extensions in Tennessee: What’s going on at TVA?
feed.summary: Appalachian Voices, Tennessee Citizens for Wilderness Planning, and Sierra Club are organizing a webinar on March 25 to discuss the Tennessee Valley Authority's recent decision to scrap plans for retiring its Kingston and Cumberland coal plants. The webinar will delve into the environmental and economic impacts of these extensions, including potential rate hikes, and offer community perspectives. Experts will also cover how Appalachian Voices is responding and how individuals can take action.

feed.title: State regulators give tentative approval to Aquarion Water Co. sale amid concerns over rates, oversight
feed.summary: Connecticut's Public Utilities Regulatory Authority (PURA) has given preliminary approval to the acquisition of Aquarion Water Co. by the South Central Connecticut Regional Water Authority, despite concerns over governance, rate oversight, and potential rate increases. The decision comes after a previous rejection by PURA and a subsequent court review, which sent the case back for further consideration. Opponents, including Attorney General William Tong, criticize the ruling as an "economic disaster" for Connecticut, citing potential transaction costs and rate hikes, while proponents argue it's a constructive step benefiting customers and communities.

feed.title: NIO's Firefly officially enters Thailand as its 2nd right-hand drive market
feed.summary: NIO's Firefly sub-brand has officially launched in Thailand, marking its second right-hand drive market after Singapore, as part of NIO's global expansion into tariff-free territories. The launch was in partnership with Thonburi BlueSky Co., Ltd., with specific Thai pricing and variants expected to be announced later in March. The Firefly is a five-door subcompact hatchback that has accumulated over 40,000 global deliveries since its Chinese launch in April 2025 and is set to expand to over 40 markets worldwide.

feed.title: Wabtec Settlement With Progress Rail Clears Path For Rail Growth

feed.title: Microchip Technology Introduces LX4580 Mixed-Signal IC for Aviation and Defense Actuation Systems

feed.title: Ford Recalls 1.74 Million SUVs Due to Rear Camera Issues
feed.summary: Ford Motor Co. has issued two separate recalls affecting 1.74 million late-model Ford and Lincoln SUVs due to rear camera malfunctions. One recall impacts 849,310 Ford Broncos (2021-2016) and Ford Edge (2021-2024) vehicles, where the rearview camera may temporarily shut down, leading to a blank screen. The second recall covers 889,950 Ford Escape (2020-2022), Lincoln Corsair (2020-2022), and Lincoln Aviator (2020-2024) models, facing issues with inverted or flipped camera images on the touchscreen. Both issues are expected to be resolved with software updates, though the fixes are not yet available.

Success hinges on stable mine production, disciplined capital deployment, and commercial traction in its VRFB business, making it a high-risk, high-upside play for global investors.
