feed.title: Insider Sell: Samuel Rubio Sells 22,461 Shares of Tidewater Inc (TDW)
feed.summary: Samuel Rubio, Executive Vice President, Chief Financial Officer, and Chief Accounting Officer of Tidewater Inc's subsidiary, recently sold 22,461 shares of Tidewater Inc (TDW). This transaction brings his total ownership to 60,066 shares, and over the past year, he has only sold shares, with no purchases. Despite this insider sell, the stock is considered "fairly valued" based on its GuruFocus Value, trading at $80.05 with a market cap of $3.855 billion and a P/E ratio of 26.19.

feed.title: Southwest’s Starlink Rollout Puts In Flight Wi Fi And Valuation In Focus
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines plans to equip over 300 aircraft with SpaceX's Starlink in-flight connectivity by the end of 2026, aiming to offer faster and more reliable Wi-Fi across its network in 11 countries. This strategic move is expected to enhance customer experience, potentially influencing competition for high-value passengers and impacting ancillary revenue. Investors are advised to monitor the pace of installation, passenger adoption, and the project's effect on capital spending, earnings per share, and dividend sustainability.

feed.title: State regulators give tentative approval to Aquarion Water Co. sale amid concerns over rates, oversight
feed.summary: Connecticut's Public Utilities Regulatory Authority (PURA) has given tentative approval to the acquisition of Aquarion Water Co. by the South Central Connecticut Regional Water Authority, despite concerns over governance, rate-setting, and rate impact raised by opponents. The decision follows a previous rejection by PURA, which was overturned by a Superior Court judge. Opponents, including Attorney General William Tong, argue the deal will lead to significant transaction costs and future rate hikes for consumers.

feed.title: Duke Energy launches $6 billion at-the-market equity program
feed.summary: Duke Energy Corporation has initiated an at-the-market equity distribution program to sell up to $6 billion of its common stock through various financial institutions. This move comes as the electric utility manages a significant debt burden and its stock trades near a 52-week high, despite InvestingPro analysis suggesting it is overvalued. The program aims to raise capital, with proceeds used by the company after commissions and expenses.

feed.title: Ex-KEPCO Worker Steals 12km Cables Amid Copper Surge
feed.summary: A former KEPCO employee has been accused of stealing 12 kilometers of copper cables. This theft occurred amid a surge in copper prices, indicating a potential motive for the crime.

feed.title: The Woman Behind a Trouble Call
feed.summary: Alissa Osburn, a troubleman field supervisor at Southern California Edison, shares her 17-year journey in the electric industry, moving from meter reader to substation operator and system operator before her current field role. Despite the male-dominated environment, Osburn advocates for curiosity and determination, encouraging women to explore challenging careers. Her experiences reinforce the value of continuous learning and building trust in specialized fields.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Ralph Larossa Sells Shares of Public Service Enter
feed.summary: Ralph Larossa, Chair, President, and CEO of Public Service Electric and Gas Company, a subsidiary of Public Service Enterprise Group Inc (PEG), sold 2,083 shares of the company on March 5, 2026. This transaction increased his total holdings to 292,889.0003 shares. Over the past year, Larossa has sold 7,604 shares with no purchases, and the company has seen seven insider sells and no buys.

feed.title: Helmerich & Payne shareholders approve incentive plan and elect directors
feed.summary: Helmerich & Payne shareholders approved the Amended & Restated 2024 Omnibus Incentive Plan and elected ten directors at the company's annual meeting. They also ratified the appointment of Ernst & Young LLP as independent auditors and passed an advisory vote on executive compensation. These approvals and elections signal a clear direction for the company's governance and compensation strategies.

feed.title: Sempra’s Record US$65b Plan Tests Dividend And Earnings Outlook
feed.summary: Sempra has announced a record US$65 billion capital plan for 2026-2030, focusing on its regulated utilities in Texas and California, alongside an increased annualized dividend of US$2.63 per share. This ambitious plan raises questions about dividend sustainability and financial flexibility, especially as recent net income was lower, and interest and dividend payments are not fully covered by current earnings or free cash flows. Despite these concerns, analysts forecast significant earnings growth, which could support the capital plan and dividend, while shares are also perceived to be trading at a discount to fair value.

feed.title: Sempra Energy Drops 1.39% Amid Mixed Results and Regulatory Uncertainty; $0.33 Billion Volume Places It at 417th as $65B Investment Strategy Targets Future Expansion
feed.summary: Sempra Energy (SRE) saw its stock drop by 1.39% despite beating Q4 adjusted earnings per share expectations, largely due to revenue missing forecasts and concerns over regulatory changes and LNG price volatility. The company is pursuing an ambitious $65 billion capital investment plan and has strong institutional backing, yet its high P/E ratio and short-term volatility remain factors for investors. The article concludes that future performance depends on Sempra's effective navigation of these challenges while advancing its infrastructure and clean energy initiatives.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Public Service Enterprise Group (NYSE:PEG) CEO Sells 2,083 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Public Service Enterprise Group (NYSE:PEG) CEO Ralph Larossa sold 2,083 shares of the company's stock for approximately $174,264, reducing his total stake by 0.71%. This transaction follows the company's recent strong performance, including a slight quarterly EPS beat, 18.3% revenue increase, raised FY2026 guidance, and an increased quarterly dividend. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with a consensus price target of $92.73.

feed.title: CSX ‘addressing’ Atlanta bridge where driver reports rocks are falling into traffic
feed.summary: A driver in Atlanta reported that rocks fell from a CSX railroad bridge, shattering his windshield, an incident he believes was caused by a moving train. After nearly a month without a response from CSX, the driver contacted the media, leading to a quick resolution and reimbursement for the damages. CSX confirmed they are addressing the issue, emphasizing safety as their top priority.

feed.title: Port Arthur LNG Announces Full Tuition Scholarship Program for Southeast Texas Students
feed.summary: Port Arthur LNG has launched the Pathways to Port Arthur LNG Scholarship Fund in collaboration with the Foundation for Southeast Texas. This program will provide ten graduating high school seniors from Jefferson and Orange Counties with up to four years of full tuition, books, and fees for STEM degrees at eligible Southeast Texas institutions annually. The initiative aims to remove financial barriers, strengthen the future workforce, and increase economic opportunity in the region.

feed.title: Robert Muraro Sells 24,589 Shares of Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP) Stock
feed.summary: Robert Muraro, an insider at Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP), sold 24,589 shares of the company's stock on March 5th, 2026, for approximately $5.93 million, reducing his holding by over 11%. This transaction valued his remaining shares at around $47.64 million. Targa Resources recently exceeded Q4 EPS expectations but had slightly lower revenue, and currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average price target of $240.79.

feed.title: CSX (NASDAQ:CSX) CEO Stephen Angel Buys 25,000 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: CSX CEO Stephen Angel purchased 25,000 shares of company stock for approximately $1.01 million, increasing his stake by over 20%. The company recently raised its quarterly dividend to $0.14 per share, but its latest earnings report missed analyst estimates. Despite the missed earnings, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for CSX.

feed.title: Boeing reportedly close to 500-jet order at US-China summit
feed.summary: Boeing is expected to secure an order for 500 737 Max jets during President Trump's upcoming visit to China. Additionally, the United States and China are in discussions for orders of approximately 100 787 Dreamliners and 777X planes. This potential deal highlights significant aerospace trade between the two nations.

feed.title: Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) CFO Sells $815,908.32 in Stock
feed.summary: Phillips 66 CFO Kevin Mitchell recently sold 4,944 shares of company stock for over $815,000, reducing his position by 4.83%. Despite this insider selling, the company increased its quarterly dividend and reported strong quarterly earnings that beat consensus estimates, although revenue slightly missed expectations. Phillips 66 shares are trading near their 52-week high, and analysts hold a mixed view with a consensus "Hold" rating.

feed.title: Assessing Hawaiian Electric (HE) Valuation After Earnings Turnaround And Wildfire Settlement Progress
feed.summary: Hawaiian Electric (HE) has seen a significant turnaround with a strong share price rebound following its 2025 earnings being profitable, along with progress in wildfire settlements and regulatory approvals. Despite a 90-day share price return of 42.91% and a 1-year total shareholder return of 54.11%, the stock is considered "Overvalued" by one narrative at a fair value of $12.00 compared to its closing price of $16.12, while a DCF model suggests an even lower value of $1.26, indicating significant valuation risk. The article highlights ongoing risks from wildfire litigation and funding needs, contrasting with legislative efforts to cap liability and support infrastructure.

feed.title: Toyota Recalls 550,000 Highlander, Highlander Hybrid SUVs for Seat Issue
feed.summary: Toyota is recalling approximately 550,000 Highlander and Highlander Hybrid SUVs from model years 2021-2024 due to an issue with the second-row seat recliner assemblies. These assemblies may not fully lock after adjustment, posing an injury risk in a high-speed crash. Toyota will replace the return springs in the recliner assemblies for free, and owners will be notified by early May.

feed.title: EOSE DEADLINE ALERT: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Reminds Eos Energy Enterprises (EOSE) Investors of Securities Class Action Deadline on May 5, 2026
feed.summary: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is reminding investors of Eos Energy Enterprises (NASDAQ: EOSE) about the May 5, 2026 deadline to apply for lead plaintiff in a federal securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that the company made misleading statements and failed to disclose production issues, including high battery line downtime and delays in automated bipolar production, which led to a significant drop in its stock price after reporting lower-than-expected full-year 2025 revenues. Investors who suffered losses are encouraged to contact the firm.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Patrick Craine Sells Shares of Gulfport Energy Cor
feed.summary: Patrick Craine, Corporate Secretary of CLAO, sold 2,000 shares of Gulfport Energy Corp (GPOR) on March 5, 2026, reducing his holdings to 11,060 shares. Over the past year, Craine has sold 18,929 shares with no purchases, and GPOR has seen 24 insider sells and no insider buys. The stock is currently trading at $209.09, which is considered fairly valued according to its GuruFocus Value.

feed.title: Citi Keeps Their Hold Rating on Woodside Energy Group (WDS)
feed.summary: Citi analyst Tom Wallington maintained a Hold rating for Woodside Energy Group (WDS) with a price target of A$28.00. This follows similar Hold ratings from UBS, although RBC Capital reaffirmed a Buy rating earlier. Woodside Energy Group recently reported a quarterly revenue of A$6.5 billion and a net profit of A$1.43 billion.

feed.title: Weekly Market Update: Stocks Fall as Energy Rises and Basic Materials Falter
feed.summary: The Morningstar US Market Index fell 2.18% this week, with the energy sector rising while basic materials and industrials were the worst performers. Several large-cap and growth stocks declined, and key economic reports are expected next week. Sabre, Wix, and Venture Global were top stock gainers, while Celsius Holdings and Norwegian Cruise Line experienced significant losses.

feed.title: Is JinkoSolar Holding (NYSE:JKS) Share Price Misaligned With Recent Valuation Signals
feed.summary: This article analyzes JinkoSolar Holding's (NYSE:JKS) share price using several valuation methods, including a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis and Price-to-Sales (P/S) ratio. Both methods suggest that JKS is currently undervalued, with the DCF model indicating a 72.8% discount to intrinsic value and the P/S ratio being significantly lower than industry and peer averages. The article also presents contrasting bullish and bearish "Narratives" from investors, offering different perspectives on the company's future revenue growth, margins, and policy risks.

feed.title: Economic implications of war: Middle East war and the automotive industry
feed.summary: This article analyzes the potential economic impact of the ongoing war in the Middle East on the automotive industry, categorizing the effects based on the conflict's duration: short-term, midterm, and longer-term scenarios. It details how oil prices, shipping costs, vehicle production, and consumer demand for different vehicle types would be affected. S&P Global Mobility currently maintains a base-case presumption of a short-term conflict for its vehicle production and sales forecasts, but acknowledges the significant uncertainties.

feed.title: Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) CFO Sells $2,835,516.32 in Stock
feed.summary: Phillips 66 CFO Kevin Mitchell recently sold 16,856 shares of company stock for over $2.8 million, reducing his direct ownership by almost 15%. This sale occurred as the company announced a dividend increase and reported quarterly earnings that beat EPS estimates but missed revenue expectations. Despite the insider selling, analysts maintain a consensus "Hold" rating on PSX with a price target of $160.11.

feed.title: Aptiv Advances Spin-Off of Electrical Distribution Systems Unit
feed.summary: Aptiv (APTV) is moving forward with its plan to spin off its Electrical Distribution Systems business into a standalone, publicly traded company named Versigent Limited. The company filed an amended SEC registration statement on March 6, 2026, and expects to complete the tax-free separation by April 1, 2026. This strategic move aims to sharpen Aptiv's focus and create a distinct investment profile for the new entity.

feed.title: Former US trade chief Lighthizer resigns from Trump Media board
feed.summary: Robert Lighthizer, former U.S. trade representative under Donald Trump, has resigned from the board of Trump Media & Technology Group (TMTG) effective last Friday. An SEC filing indicated that his departure was not due to disagreements with the company's management or board. TMTG, known for Truth Social, recently announced a strategic pivot to fusion energy through a merger with TAE Technologies.

feed.title: Eversource Statement on Proposed Final Decision to Approve Aquarion Sale
feed.summary: Eversource Energy issued a statement regarding the Connecticut Public Utilities Regulatory Authority's (PURA) proposed final decision to approve the sale of Aquarion Water Company. The company is considering the conditions of the decision and noted that the sale is not included in their 2026 guidance. Eversource's 2026 earnings projection of $4.80 to $4.95 per share and long-term growth rate of 5 to 7 percent through 2030 remain unchanged, assuming Aquarion remains part of Eversource.

feed.title: Freeport-McMoRan's Rally Is Over—But the Bull Case Isn't
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) saw an 80% rally in four months, but technical indicators suggest a pullback to the $55-$57 range is likely. Despite giving back recent gains, the long-term bull case for FCX remains strong due to increasing copper demand from EVs, data centers, and electrification, alongside its significant gold exposure. The company's restructured Grasberg mine operations, though reducing economic ownership, provide stability through 2041 and contribute meaningful cash flow.

feed.title: Duke Energy Plans to Offer $6 Billion in Shares
feed.summary: Duke Energy announced a plan to offer up to $6 billion in shares to strengthen its capital structure and fund future investments, including renewable energy projects and infrastructure upgrades. This move is expected to enhance the company's competitiveness in the renewable energy sector and market financing capabilities. Investors are advised to monitor the market reaction and potential implications for the company's financial health.

feed.title: Honda Recalls 65,000 SUVs For Screens That May Go Black
feed.summary: Honda is recalling 65,135 electric crossovers, including the 2024 Honda Prologue and 2024 Acura ZDX, due to software defects that can cause instrument and infotainment displays to go blank. This issue prevents drivers from seeing critical information like speed and warning lights. The recall affects vehicles built on General Motors' Ultium battery platform, and Honda will instruct owners to take their vehicles to a service center for a software update.

feed.title: Delek Logistics Partners, LP 2025 K-1 Tax Packages Available on Website
feed.summary: Delek Logistics Partners, LP (DKL) announced that its 2025 K-1 tax packages are now accessible online through its third-party provider's website. Printing and mailing of these tax packages are currently in progress. Questions regarding the 2025 Tax Reporting Package can be directed to a dedicated helpline.

feed.title: 2025 tax forms for Delek Logistics unitholders now online
feed.summary: Delek Logistics Partners, LP announced that its 2025 K-1 tax packages are now available online via a third-party provider's website. Unitholders can access their tax forms electronically, and printed copies are also being mailed out. Questions regarding the tax reporting package can be directed to a dedicated support line.

feed.title: BKV 10-K: $1,008.8M Revenue, $1.98 EPS
feed.summary: BKV reported total revenues and other operating income of $1,008.8 million for the year, driven by higher commodity prices and volumes, and achieved a net income attributable to BKV of $173.1 million, with a basic and diluted net income per common share of $1.98. These strong results reflect increased commodity prices, derivative gains, and consolidation impacts from recent business transactions. The company also highlighted significant business developments including 75% year-over-year natural gas revenue growth, expansion through joint ventures and acquisitions, and operational milestones in CCUS sequestration.

feed.title: Tsakos Energy Navigation Names Kosmatos as New Chief Financial Officer
feed.summary: Tsakos Energy Navigation (TEN) has announced a leadership change in its finance department. Paul Durham resigned as Co-CFO and Chief Accounting Officer but will continue as an adviser, while Theoharrys E. Kosmatos was promoted to Chief Financial Officer and Chief Accounting Officer, ensuring continuity. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates TEN as "Outperform," citing solid financial performance, attractive valuation, and strong forward visibility despite some concerns about negative free cash flow and high leverage.

feed.title: Mammoth Energy Services 10-K: Revenue $44.3M, EPS (continuing) $(1.32)
feed.summary: Mammoth Energy Services reported full-year revenue of $44.3 million, a 3% decline from the prior year, alongside a net loss from continuing operations of $63.8 million, or $(1.32) per diluted share. Despite this, the company achieved overall net income of $4.6 million due to a $68.4 million gain from discontinued operations and saw improvements in operating loss compared to the previous year. Strategic actions included asset impairments and idling of certain operations to align capacity with demand, with management forecasting steady activity in the first half of 2026.

feed.title: Previewing Suncor Energy v. Boulder County
feed.summary: Jonathan H. Adler discusses a Federalist Society Forum he participated in, which previewed the major climate change case of OT 2026, Suncor Energy v. Boulder County. He was joined by West Virginia Solicitor General Michael Williams and O.H. Skinner, with Annie Donaldson Talley moderating. The article also provides a list of Adler's previous posts related to climate change litigation and preemption issues.

feed.title: SM Energy’s New US$1b Notes Recast Debt Profile After Civitas Merger
feed.summary: SM Energy recently launched and priced US$1 billion in senior notes, intending to use the proceeds for a US$750 million tender offer for its 2028 notes and to optimize its debt profile following the merger with Civitas Resources. This move aims to reshape the company's debt structure and manage risk while supporting capital spending and shareholder returns. The article highlights that SM Energy is trading below analyst targets and is flagged as undervalued by Simply Wall St's valuation, despite the refinancing news.

feed.title: Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacifico Reports a Passenger Traffic Decrease in February 2026 of 5.5% Compared to 2025
feed.summary: Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacífico (GAP) reported a 5.5% decrease in total terminal passenger traffic in February 2026 compared to February 2025. The decline was mainly observed in Mexican airports like Tijuana, Puerto Vallarta, and Guadalajara, with significant reductions in Montego Bay, Jamaica, due to Hurricane Melissa. Overall, domestic passenger traffic fell by 4.5% and international traffic by 6.6% for the month.

feed.title: T1 Energy Inc. launches SXSW events to promote American energy
feed.summary: T1 Energy Inc. is hosting and sponsoring events at SXSW in Austin to promote American energy solutions and engage with the innovation community. This initiative highlights the company's commitment to sustainable American energy and building partnerships, as it continues to advocate for clean energy expansion in Texas and combine solar and fossil power in the Permian Basin.

feed.title: SFL (NYSE: SFL) wins $170M Canadian drilling deal for Hercules semi-sub rig
feed.summary: SFL Corporation has secured a new long-term drilling contract for its harsh-environment semi-submersible rig Hercules in Canada, valued at an estimated $170 million for a minimum term of 400 days. The contract, with a multinational oil and gas company, is expected to commence in Q1 2027, with the rig mobilizing from Norway to Canada later in 2026. Odfjell Drilling will manage the rig, positioning Hercules for future deepwater campaigns.

feed.title: Global methanol producer Methanex hits emissions target 5 years early
feed.summary: Methanex Corporation announced the release of its 2025 Sustainability Report, highlighting significant progress in its sustainability efforts. The company achieved its greenhouse gas emissions intensity reduction target five years ahead of schedule and reported continued strong safety performance. The report also details Methanex's efforts to manage environmental impacts, advance low-carbon solutions, and contribute to workforce and community development.

feed.title: YPF Files 2025 Consolidated Financials in Form 6-K Submission
feed.summary: YPF Sociedad Anónima (YPF) has filed its Form 6-K with U.S. regulators, providing consolidated financial statements for the fiscal years ending December 31, 2023, 2024, and 2025 in both U.S. dollars and Argentine pesos. This extensive filing details the company's financial position, performance, cash flows, risk management, capital structure, and regulatory exposure, enhancing transparency for investors. Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, rates YPF as Neutral, acknowledging significant challenges in financial performance but noting strong operational achievements and strategic initiatives.

feed.title: SA Asks: Which stocks are likely to be impacted by the Iran war? (UAL:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Seeking Alpha analysts Michael Boyd and Damir Tokic discuss the potential impact of a U.S.-Iran conflict on various stocks. Oil producers like Chord Energy, APA Corporation, and Kosmos Energy are highlighted as potential beneficiaries, while U.S. refiners like Valero and HF Sinclair could also see benefits. Conversely, the transportation sector (e.g., United Airlines), consumer staples (e.g., Walmart), and high-growth stocks are identified as vulnerable to rising oil prices.

feed.title: Freeport-McMoRan's Rally Is Over—But the Bull Case Isn't
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) saw a significant rally early in 2026 due to its position as a leading copper miner, but the stock has since given back most of those gains. Despite the recent sell-off and near-term concerns about valuation and geopolitical complexity, the long-term bull case for FCX remains strong. This is primarily driven by the central role of the Grasberg mine, increasing global copper demand for electrification, and the added benefit of gold exposure from the mine.

feed.title: Insider Purchase: President & CEO of $CSX Buys 25,000 Shares
feed.summary: Stephen F. Angel, President & CEO of CSX, recently purchased 25,000 shares of the company stock for an estimated $1,006,750, increasing his holdings by 20.6%. This transaction is part of broader insider trading activity at CSX, which includes both purchases and sales by various executives over the last six months. The article also provides details on hedge fund activity, Q4 2025 revenue, Congressional stock trading involving CSX, and recent analyst ratings and price targets for the company.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Downgrades AES Corp. (AES) Amid Equity Concerns
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has downgraded AES Corp. (AES) from Overweight to Equal Weight, revising its price target to $15 due to concerns about potential equity needs following a recent acquisition agreement. Despite AES's strategic acquisitions and diverse energy portfolio across 15 countries, its financial health shows significant leverage and a high risk of bankruptcy according to the Altman Z-Score. The market is showing skepticism, with the stock trading at a discount to the deal price, and analysts maintaining a 'Hold' position.

feed.title: Ameren Positioned Well for Midwest Transmission Needs
feed.summary: Ameren is a regulated utility operating in Illinois and Missouri, with improving regulation in Missouri driving significant investment opportunities. Despite challenges in Illinois, management has successfully navigated regulatory hurdles. The company's strategic positioning in the Midwest, particularly with the increased capital investment plan, supports a higher fair value estimate.

feed.title: Marathon Petroleum Corp says CEO Maryann T. Mannen's 2025 total compensation was $19.0 million - SEC filing
feed.summary: Marathon Petroleum Corp announced that its CEO, Maryann T. Mannen, received a total compensation of $19.0 million in 2025, according to a recent SEC filing. The company specializes in oil refining, transportation, and distribution, reporting 3 million barrels of refined products sold per day in 2025 and owning 13 refineries and a network of 7,882 service stations in the United States.

feed.title: Class I Briefs: CSX, NS
feed.summary: CSX celebrated the groundbreaking of a new Chick-fil-A Supply distribution center in Winter Haven, Florida, a $150 million investment expected to create 180 jobs and begin operations in 2027. Meanwhile, Norfolk Southern introduced its newest Landmark Series unit, the Altoona, featuring Automated Track Geometry Measurement Systems (ATGMS) which uses lasers and sensors to measure track geometry in real-time, enhancing safety and efficiency across its network. The article highlights both companies' commitment to improving their logistics and operational capabilities.

feed.title: Boeing Secures Major Orders Amid Skyrocketing Growth in Aircraft Demand
feed.summary: Boeing has secured significant new orders and is experiencing optimism in the market, with its stock trading up 3.85%. Despite some operational struggles highlighted by its profitability ratios and high valuation metrics, the company's strong order books, increased deliveries, and strategic partnerships position it for future growth. Analysts maintain a "Buy" rating, anticipating continued recovery and market leadership in the aerospace industry.

feed.title: Stock Traders Purchase Large Volume of FedEx Call Options (NYSE:FDX)
feed.summary: FedEx (NYSE: FDX) experienced a significant increase in call option purchases, up 825% compared to its daily average, indicating strong bullish sentiment despite a 4.3% share price drop influenced by tariff-refund uncertainty and pre-earnings de-risking. The company recently reported strong quarterly earnings, topping estimates with an EPS of $4.82 and $23.47 billion in revenue, and declared a quarterly dividend of $1.45 per share. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average target price of $366.19.

feed.title: Investors Purchase High Volume of Call Options on Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY)
feed.summary: Investors showed unusually high bullish sentiment towards Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY), purchasing 120,397 call options, a 96% increase from typical daily volume, as the stock rallied to $53.98. This activity is supported by strong fundamentals including beating EPS estimates, an increased quarterly dividend, ongoing debt reduction efforts, and rising oil prices, alongside several analyst price target increases. The company also benefits from positive geopolitical sentiment, commodity-driven rallies, and strategic tailwinds including Warren Buffett's significant stake.

feed.title: Evercore ISI Downgrades Duke Energy (DUK) to In Line after Recent Share Rally
feed.summary: Evercore ISI downgraded Duke Energy (DUK) to "In Line" from "Outperform," lowering the price target to $139 from $143, following a 15% share rally since early December. Despite maintaining a positive long-term outlook due to the company's strong growth prospects and infrastructure investments, the firm felt it was appropriate to "take a pause" given the recent stock performance. An analyst from Ritholtz Wealth Management also commented on Duke Energy, highlighting its strong regulated earnings growth, dividend yield, and premium valuation, but cautioned traders to await a clear breakout supported by strong volume.

feed.title: CF Industries Stock Surges Amid Rising Nitrogen Prices and Geopolitical Factors
feed.summary: CF Industries Holdings Inc. (CF) stock has surged by 4.51% due to strong financials, rising nitrogen prices, and clean energy initiatives. The company reported robust profitability with a 33.9% EBIT margin and $7.08 billion in total revenue, driven by sustained demand for nitrogen products. Strategic focus on clean energy and carbon capture, along with positive cash flow, positions CF Industries for continued growth despite long-term pricing uncertainties.

feed.title: Benchmark Downgrades Diamondback Energy (FANG) Following “Unremarkable” Q4 Results
feed.summary: Benchmark recently downgraded Diamondback Energy (FANG) from Buy to Hold, citing valuation concerns and "unremarkable" Q4 results. Despite the downgrade, Diamondback Energy remains committed to returning at least 50% of quarterly free cash flow to shareholders through dividends and buybacks, maintains low operating costs, and focuses on Permian Basin operations. The article suggests focusing on AI stocks with higher potential returns.

feed.title: Halliburton Stock: Why Wall Street Is Suddenly Watching Again
feed.summary: Halliburton is attracting renewed attention from Wall Street due to crude oil price swings, shifts in shale spending, and advancements in AI-driven drilling technology. The company, a major oilfield service provider, is seen as a high-beta energy play, with significant exposure to US oilfield activity and the capital expenditure decisions of producers. Investors view it as either a cyclical opportunity driven by rising oil prices and efficient drilling tech or a potential value trap if the oil boom's late stage is approaching.

feed.title: RBC Capital raises Cheniere Energy Partners price target on expansion progress
feed.summary: RBC Capital has increased its price target for Cheniere Energy Partners (NYSE:CQP) to $62.00 from $58.00, while maintaining a Sector Perform rating, following the company's Q4 2025 earnings report and progress on the Sabine Pass Liquefaction expansion. Despite the stock already trading above the new target at $64.11, the firm now incorporates the expansion project, including the commercialization of Train 7 and anticipated FERC permit in late 2026, into its valuation. Additionally, Moody's Ratings recently upgraded Cheniere Energy Inc.'s notes to Baa2 and affirmed Cheniere Energy Partners' Baa2 rating, both with stable outlooks, indicating strong creditworthiness.

feed.title: DHT Holdings, Inc. announces delivery of VLCC newbuilding
feed.summary: DHT Holdings, Inc. has announced the delivery of its second VLCC newbuilding, named DHT Addax, which will enter the spot market. This vessel is the second of four newbuildings expected in the first half of 2026 and aims to enhance the company's customer offerings and earnings. The new additions are fully funded, with the next delivery scheduled for late March 2026.

feed.title: Indonesia Energy Stock Jumps Friday: What's Driving The Move?
feed.summary: Indonesia Energy Corp Ltd (INDO) stock experienced a sharp rise on Friday due to fresh escalation in the U.S.–Israel–Iran conflict, specifically Israeli strikes and Iranian retaliatory actions. Increased Middle East tensions are tightening global crude supplies, which benefits high-beta energy stocks like INDO as its revenue is directly tied to oil prices. The company's short-term moving averages have also moved above the 200-day average, signaling positive momentum.

feed.title: PG&E's Electricity Rates Are Dropping This Month. What Does That Mean for Your Monthly Bill?
feed.summary: PG&E customers will see a slight decrease in their electricity bills this month, with a residential drop of about $5.14 and a $10.37 decrease for CARE program enrollees, effective March 1. This marks the third consecutive electric price cut since last September, attributed to lower costs for completed safety work exceeding new investment costs. However, natural gas rates are slightly increasing, and PG&E is implementing a new Base Services Charge to restructure billing.

feed.title: Terrestrial Energy to Participate in Upcoming Investor Conferences
feed.summary: Terrestrial Energy Inc., a developer of Generation IV nuclear plants, announced its participation in two upcoming investor conferences: Cantor Fitzgerald’s Global Technology & Industrial Growth Conference on March 11, 2026, and Canaccord Genuity's 2nd Annual CG Virtual Sustainability Summit on March 12, 2026. Senior leadership, including CEO Simon Irish and CFO Brian Thrasher, will be presenting and hosting one-on-one meetings at these events to discuss their proprietary Integral Molten Salt Reactor (IMSR) technology.

feed.title: Imperial Petroleum (IMPP) Q4 Earnings Surge 250% YoY to $0.35 EPS on Strong Tanker Utilization
feed.summary: Imperial Petroleum Inc. (IMPP) reported a 250% year-over-year surge in Q4 2025 EPS to $0.35, with net income reaching $15.0 million on revenues of $51.1 million. This significant growth, nearly doubling revenue from the previous year, was attributed to strong utilization of its tanker and dry bulk fleet. Investors are now awaiting the Q1 2026 guidance from the earnings call to assess if this momentum will continue, especially given the stock is trading below its 52-week high.

feed.title: FreightCar America Inc expected to post earnings of 19 cents a share - Earnings Preview
feed.summary: FreightCar America Inc is projected to report earnings of 19 cents per share. This information comes from a Reuters earnings preview. The article details the expected financial performance for the company.

feed.title: Tsakos Energy Navigation Ltd On March 3, Paul Durham Stepped Down As Co-Chief Financial Officer And Chief Accounting Officer
feed.summary: Tsakos Energy Navigation Ltd announced that Paul Durham stepped down from his roles as Co-Chief Financial Officer and Chief Accounting Officer on March 3. The brief Refinitiv report confirms the personnel change without further details.

feed.title: New CFO appointed at Tsakos Energy Navigation (TEN) as Durham shifts to advisory role
feed.summary: Tsakos Energy Navigation Limited (TEN) announced a leadership change in its finance department. Paul Durham stepped down as Co-Chief Financial Officer and Chief Accounting Officer on March 3, 2026, transitioning to an advisory role within the company. The Board of Directors appointed Theoharrys E. Kosmatos, previously Co-Chief Financial Officer since July 1, 2024, as the new Chief Financial Officer and Chief Accounting Officer, effective the same date.

feed.title: Central North Airport Group reports 5.4% rise in February passenger traffic
feed.summary: Central North Airport Group (OMA) reported a 5.4% increase in terminal passenger traffic across its 13 airports in February 2026 compared to the previous year. Domestic traffic saw a 6.5% rise, while international traffic experienced a slight 0.3% decrease. The company noted that commercial traffic accounted for 99.4% of total passengers, with the remaining 0.6% from general aviation.

feed.title: Shareholders of NuScale Power Corporation Should Contact
feed.summary: The Gross Law Firm is notifying shareholders of NuScale Power Corporation (NYSE: SMR) about a class action lawsuit. Shareholders who bought SMR shares between May 13, 2025, and November 6, 2025, are encouraged to contact the firm regarding their rights, with a lead plaintiff deadline of April 20, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that NuScale made misleading statements concerning its partnership with ENTRA1 Energy LLC, highlighting ENTRA1's lack of experience in nuclear power generation.

feed.title: FuelCell Energy (FCEL) Poised for Q1 Earnings Announcement
feed.summary: FuelCell Energy (FCEL) is scheduled to release its first-quarter earnings on March 9, with analysts projecting a significant 122.3% year-over-year revenue increase despite a predicted EPS of -$0.67. The company, a small-cap clean energy technology provider, faces financial challenges including negative margins and declining revenue growth, alongside a high bankruptcy risk indicated by its Altman Z-Score. While it has a strong liquidity position, its valuation and market sentiment reflect a speculative outlook with a "hold" recommendation from analysts.

feed.title: XPO's Demand Backdrop Likely To Remain Muted Into First-Half 2026 but Margins Healthy
feed.summary: XPO's demand is expected to remain subdued into the first half of 2026, yet the company is maintaining healthy margins. The article explains XPO's business strategy, which evolved from asset-light brokerage to include asset-based trucking and last-mile delivery through acquisitions. Matthew Young provides insights into the company's financial health despite the muted demand outlook.

feed.title: Enterprise Products Partners L.P. 2025 Letter to Investors Now Available
feed.summary: Enterprise Products Partners L.P. (NYSE: EPD) has announced the publication of its 2025 Letter to Investors, which is now accessible on the company's website under the Investors tab. The company is a major North American provider of midstream energy services, with extensive assets including pipelines, storage capacity for various energy products, and marine transportation.

feed.title: Navigator Holdings Loan Fuels Ethylene Fleet Growth And Debt Questions
feed.summary: Navigator Holdings (NYSE:NVGS) recently secured a $133.77 million senior secured term loan to finance two new liquefied ethylene gas carriers, expanding its fleet and focusing on petrochemical transport. While this move supports fleet modernization and competitive operations, it also introduces risks such as increased debt sensitivity to interest rates and potential challenges if shipping demand weakens. Investors are advised to monitor charter structures, net debt, and financial statements for insights into the company's capital allocation and ability to manage this new debt.

feed.title: Methanex (MEOH) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Methanex Corporation (MEOH) reported its Q4 2025 results, revealing an adjusted net loss of $11 million despite sequentially higher methanol production, primarily due to lower realized prices and fixed costs from plant outages. The company focused on accelerated debt repayment, reducing its Term Loan A facility to $300 million, and is integrating newly acquired OCI assets with targeted synergies of $30 million by the end of 2026. Global methanol markets face significant uncertainty, with Middle East disruptions increasing spot prices and raising concerns about supply reliability and potential demand destruction.

feed.title: Pangaea Logistics Solutions Ltd expected to post earnings of 24 cents a share - Earnings Preview
feed.summary: Pangaea Logistics Solutions Ltd (PANL) is projected to report earnings of 24 cents per share. This earnings preview provides an outlook for the company's upcoming financial disclosure. The information comes from Reuters via Refinitiv.

feed.title: Paccar Inc (NASDAQ:PCAR) Q2 2025 earnings call transcript
feed.summary: The article is the transcript of Paccar Inc's Q2 2025 earnings call. It contains detailed discussions related to the company's financial performance, operational achievements, and future outlook presented during the call.

feed.title: American Electric Drives Growth Through Investments and Renewables
feed.summary: American Electric Power Company (AEP) is focusing on growth through infrastructure investments and expanding its renewable energy portfolio. The company plans to allocate $8 billion to regulated renewable projects and a broader $72 billion investment plan through 2030, aiming for a 10% rate base CAGR. Despite strong growth prospects and emission reduction goals, AEP faces risks from its reliance on a limited number of Retail Electric Providers and potential impacts from new environmental regulations on its coal-fired units.

feed.title: Boeing Co Stock (BA) Moved Up by 3.70% on Mar 6: Facts Behind the Movement
feed.summary: Boeing's stock rose 3.70% on March 6 due to news of a potential large order of 500 737 MAX jets from China, alongside positive analyst ratings and improved operational metrics. The company recently achieved positive annual operating cash flow and is increasing production rates, contributing to an optimistic outlook despite ongoing quality control concerns and regulatory scrutiny. Technical indicators show mixed signals, with a sell signal from MACD but neutral to oversold conditions from RSI and Williams %R.

feed.title: Daily 5 report for March 6: Ford’s Plan B for Kentucky battery plant
feed.summary: Ford is repurposing its Kentucky battery plant to focus on energy storage, aiming to mitigate EV write-downs and tap into the demand from AI data centers. This strategic shift, while potentially profitable, places Ford in competition with established players in the energy storage market. The report also highlights other automotive news including disruptions from the Iran war, dealership buy-sell updates, and GM's leadership changes.

feed.title: Wall Street's 'fear index' hits highest point since October
feed.summary: Wall Street's "fear index," the CBOE Volatility Index (VIX), surged to its highest point since October 2025 amidst ongoing conflict in West Asia. The VIX spike reflects investor concerns about potential oil supply disruptions and inflation, with crude oil benchmarks like WTI and Brent Crude rising significantly. Concurrently, crypto stocks such as Strategy, Coinbase Global, Robinhood Markets, and Circle Internet Group experienced declines, mirroring the increased market volatility.

feed.title: Storage, PPAs, data centers, thermal auction: Where Argentina's Central Puerto sees opportunities
feed.summary: Argentina's largest power generator, Central Puerto, has identified key growth opportunities in energy storage, corporate power-purchase agreements (PPAs), and a potential thermal supply auction. The company is also involved in an AI data center project with OpenAI and aims to leverage its existing assets and new capacity additions. Structural reforms in Argentina's power market are creating both challenges and new avenues for business development for Central Puerto.

feed.title: Oil Above $90 Pushes US Gasoline Prices To Highest Level Since 2024 — These Energy Stocks Could Benefit
feed.summary: Oil prices have surpassed $90 per barrel for the first time since October 2023, causing US gasoline prices to jump to their highest levels since May 2024. The national average for regular gasoline is $3.320 per gallon, with some West Coast states experiencing prices near $5. This surge is attributed to rising crude oil costs due to an escalating war in Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, presenting potential benefits for oil producers, refiners, and fuel retailers like Exxon Mobil, Chevron, and Occidental Petroleum.

feed.title: Precision Trading with Crossamerica Partners Lp Representing Limited Partner Interests (CAPL) Risk Zones
feed.summary: This article analyzes Crossamerica Partners Lp Representing Limited Partner Interests (CAPL) using AI models to provide precision trading strategies. It highlights divergent sentiment, a potential breakout, and elevated downside risk due to a lack of long-term support signals. The article offers distinct trading strategies (Position, Momentum Breakout, Risk Hedging) tailored to different risk profiles, along with multi-timeframe signal analysis for support and resistance levels.

feed.title: Denison Mines Corp Sees Volatility Amid Market Speculations
feed.summary: Denison Mines Corp (DNN) is experiencing stock volatility, trading down by -5.15% due to market concerns over mining setbacks and negative margins despite robust operational cash reserves. The company's strategy involves leveraging capital for exploration and relies on anticipated upticks in the uranium market, with institutional investors showing bullish sentiment. Analysts are closely watching how Denison's substantial capital positions will translate into strategic and operational efficacy amidst market shifts.

feed.title: Energizer Sell-Off And Undervaluation As Geopolitical And Oil Risks Rise
feed.summary: Energizer Holdings (NYSE:ENR) has experienced a significant stock sell-off due to escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and rising oil prices, with its shares declining over 32% in the past year. Despite this downturn, Simply Wall St identifies the stock as undervalued, trading about 19.6% below analyst targets and 81.3% below its estimated fair value. Investors are advised to monitor the company's cost management, balance sheet resilience, and debt coverage by operating cash flow, especially given the current market pressures.

feed.title: Eos Energy Insider Buying And Hedge Fund Entry After Guidance Miss
feed.summary: Eos Energy Enterprises' CEO recently purchased a significant amount of company shares following a miss in its 2025 revenue guidance, despite the company reporting a large net loss. Concurrently, hedge fund Cannell Capital initiated a new stake in EOSE, signaling institutional interest. These actions suggest that insiders and some institutions see value in Eos Energy's long-term commercial pipeline and operational strategy, even amidst risks like ongoing losses and execution challenges.

feed.title: A Look At Ormat Technologies (ORA) Valuation After Upbeat Clean Energy Outlook And 2026 Revenue Guidance
feed.summary: Ormat Technologies (ORA) is currently undervalued at $107.81 compared to a fair value of $128, driven by its clean energy positioning, recent dividend affirmation, and 2026 revenue guidance of US$1.11b to US$1.16b. Despite a recent share price decline, federal permitting reforms are expediting geothermal projects, though its high P/E ratio of 52.9x and significant net debt to EBITDA present some valuation risks. Investors are encouraged to review performance data and consider potential risks associated with high capital spending and Chinese battery exposure.

feed.title: Frontier Group Holdings, Inc. Experiences Revision in Stock Evaluation Amid Market Volatility
feed.summary: Frontier Group Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: ULCC) has seen its stock evaluation revised due to market volatility, closing at $3.90. The airline industry player has experienced significant declines of 46.38% over the past year and 68.05% over three years, contrasting with broader market gains. Technical indicators show a mixed outlook, with some suggesting bearish trends while daily moving averages indicate a mildly bullish stance.

feed.title: Sun Country Airlines Hits Day Low of $16.61 Amid Price Pressure
feed.summary: Sun Country Airlines Holdings, Inc. experienced a significant stock decline on March 5, 2026, dropping 7.83% and hitting an intraday low of $16.61. This recent poor performance contrasts with a 16.96% year-to-date increase, although the three-year performance shows an 18.18% decline. Concerns about operational efficiency arose from recent quarterly results, despite the company maintaining a reasonable return on capital employed.

feed.title: Ecopetrol Posts Solid 2025 Earnings, Boosts Reserves and Surpasses Energy Transition Targets
feed.summary: Ecopetrol S.A. reported strong full-year 2025 results despite lower Brent prices, maintaining high production, achieving record refining throughputs, and expanding its transport system. The company posted COP 46.7 trillion in EBITDA and COP 9.0 trillion in net income, strengthened its reserve base, and surpassed its 2030 energy transition self-generation target. Despite a 10.2% drop in sales revenue, Ecopetrol sustained a robust 39% EBITDA margin and proposed a COP 110 per share dividend for 2026.

feed.title: BP PLC Stock (BP) Moved Up by 3.23% on Mar 6: Drivers Behind the Movement
feed.summary: BP PLC's stock rose by 3.23% on March 6, 2026, driven primarily by a significant surge in global oil prices caused by escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Additionally, company-specific developments, such as announced changes to its board of directors, signaled a focus on corporate governance and energy transition strategy, further contributing to the stock's upward movement. While technical indicators showed mixed signals, the robust increase in oil prices was identified as the main catalyst for the intraday appreciation.

feed.title: Diana Shipping Increases Offer to Acquire Genco
feed.summary: Diana Shipping has increased its offer to acquire fellow dry bulk shipping company Genco Shipping & Trading to $23.50 a share, representing a 31% premium. The proposal includes $1.43 billion in committed financing, and Star Bulk Carriers has agreed to purchase 16 of Genco's vessels for $470.5 million if the acquisition closes. Diana Shipping currently owns approximately 14.8% of Genco's outstanding shares.

feed.title: Is W&T Offshore (WTI) Using Its New Dividend to Reframe Its Capital Allocation Story?
feed.summary: W&T Offshore (WTI) has declared a consistent quarterly cash dividend of US$0.01 per share since early 2024, including for Q1 2026. This dividend, despite the company's recurring net losses, signals management's commitment to returning cash to shareholders while balancing reinvestment in its capital-intensive offshore business. The article suggests that while the dividend itself is modest, it plays a role in the company's capital allocation narrative, though operational execution and commodity prices remain key factors for its financial sustainability.

feed.title: Matson, BNSF, War-Lok Team on Cargo Security
feed.summary: Matson Inc. has partnered with BNSF and War-Lok to implement an enhanced cargo security program aimed at combating rising intermodal cargo theft. The program, starting in Q2 2026, will utilize War-Lok security devices on international containers moving from Los Angeles to BNSF network destinations and position Matson containers in the lower well of intermodal railcars for added protection, all at no extra cost to customers. This initiative seeks to significantly reduce theft exposure for Matson's customers.

feed.title: Assessing Kosmos Energy (KOS) Valuation After 2026 Growth Plan And Ghana License Extensions
feed.summary: Kosmos Energy (KOS) has presented an ambitious 2026 growth plan aiming for a 15% production increase, a 20% cut in operating costs, and reduced net debt. This guidance, coupled with Ghana license extensions, has significantly boosted its share price, despite historical losses. While an analyst narrative model suggests the stock is 12.8% overvalued at $2.24, Simply Wall St's DCF model indicates it's undervalued by 15.1% at $2.98, leading to conflicting valuation signals.

feed.title: Sikorsky to boost production of its flagship variant
feed.summary: This article states that Sikorsky plans to increase production of its flagship variant. No further details are provided in the content.

feed.title: Tesla Sued by Texas Worker Claiming Assault by Austin Shooter
feed.summary: Tesla is being sued for over $1 million by Lillian Mendoza Brady, a former employee, who claims she was assaulted by Ndiaga Diagne, the individual police have identified as the Austin mass shooter. The lawsuit alleges Tesla failed to ensure workplace safety and negligently supervised Diagne, who reportedly had "known aggressive tendencies" and had assaulted Brady during a company-sanctioned prayer break in December.

feed.title: Ford Recalls 1.74 Million SUVs Due to Rear Camera Issues
feed.summary: Ford Motor Co. has issued two separate recalls affecting 1.74 million late-model Ford and Lincoln SUVs due to rear camera display issues. One recall concerns 849,310 Ford Bronco and Edge models where the rear-view camera image may temporarily shut down, while another recall for 889,950 Ford Escape, Lincoln Corsair, and Lincoln Aviator models addresses a software problem causing flipped or inverted images. Ford states it is unaware of any crashes or injuries related to these issues but is developing software updates to resolve them.

feed.title: EQT's Options: A Look at What the Big Money is Thinking
feed.summary: Deep-pocketed investors are showing a mixed but leaning bearish sentiment towards EQT (NYSE: EQT), with 14 unusual options activities observed. While some call options suggest bullish hopes, a significant portion of the options volume points to a bearish outlook, particularly with a price target range between $45.0 and $62.5. Analysts currently have an average target price of $67.6 for EQT, with ratings ranging from Neutral to Buy/Overweight.

feed.title: Crude Oil Surges Above $90, Investors Focus on Energy Sector
feed.summary: Crude oil prices have surged above $90 per barrel due to geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, specifically U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran disrupting global supply chains and the Strait of Hormuz. This has led investors to focus on the energy sector, particularly small-cap companies like KNOT Offshore Partners LP, Deep Yellow Limited, and FLEX LNG, which have demonstrated exceptional profitability and resilience. The article highlights FLEX LNG's financial performance, analyst ratings, and dividend consistency amidst the volatile market.

feed.title: Avista seeks rate increase in North Idaho to pay for energy efficiency programs
feed.summary: Avista Utilities is seeking a 7.7% rate increase for North Idaho customers to cover underfunded energy efficiency programs from 2025 and fund future programs in 2026. The increase, if approved, would raise the average residential electric bill by $8.90 per month. The Idaho Public Utilities Commission is accepting public comments on the proposal until March 27.

feed.title: Delek Logistics puts 2025 annual report and audited financials online
feed.summary: Delek Logistics Partners, LP (NYSE: DKL) announced that its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, was filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on February 27, 2026. The report, which includes audited financial statements, is available on the Partnership’s website. Additionally, limited partners can request a free printed copy of the report.

feed.title: Genco Shipping & Trading Ltd has announced that its Board of Directors, with the assistance of external advisors, plans to carefully evaluate the revised non-binding indication of interest submitted by Diana Shipping.
feed.summary: Genco Shipping & Trading Ltd's Board of Directors, with the help of external advisors, will evaluate a revised non-binding indication of interest from Diana Shipping. This development marks a new phase in the potential transaction between the two shipping companies. The article includes a disclaimer stating that the content reflects only the author's opinion and is not investment advice.

feed.title: DHT Holdings, inc: Strait of Hormuz Risk Amidst US-Israel vs Iran Tensions Spikes VLCC Rates.
feed.summary: The article discusses how escalating US-Israel vs. Iran tensions leading to disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz have significantly driven up VLCC (Very Large Crude Carrier) spot rates, benefiting DHT Holdings Inc. DHT Holdings, with its high spot market exposure, is well-positioned to capitalize on these elevated rates. The author provides a valuation, suggesting the stock is undervalued given the current geopolitical climate and potential for sustained high rates.

feed.title: Jeep Solves Crash-Test Tipping Problem on 2026 Wrangler
feed.summary: Jeep has successfully addressed a persistent crash-test tipping issue with its 2026 Wrangler by modifying the frame rail, resulting in an "acceptable" rating from the IIHS for the small overlap frontal crash test. While the improvements prevented rollovers, the Wrangler did not achieve the highest "good" rating due to a high risk of injury to the front passenger's right foot and leg, based on crash-test dummy measurements. This modification also applies to the Gladiator pickup, boosting its rating to acceptable.

feed.title: Carnival Cruise Line stock falls as Iran war causes oil prices to increase
feed.summary: Carnival Cruise Line's stock has fallen due to increasing oil prices caused by the ongoing conflict in Iran and surrounding countries, leading to turmoil in the Middle East. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for global oil shipments, has driven Brent crude prices to nearly $90 per barrel. Unlike its competitors, Carnival does not hedge its oil purchases, making it particularly vulnerable to these market fluctuations, while several cruise lines have already canceled Middle East sailings.

feed.title: How Nabors’ Debt Reduction and Portfolio Moves Could Impact Nabors Industries (NBR) Investors
feed.summary: Nabors Industries recently reported robust fourth-quarter 2025 results, featuring $798 million in revenue and $10 million in net income, alongside a significant debt reduction of $554 million since 2024. These financial improvements, complemented by strategic portfolio adjustments like the acquisition of Parker Wellbore and divestiture of Quail Tools, are bolstering analyst confidence in the company's operational strength and balance sheet recovery. The article suggests that while debt reduction positively impacts Nabors' near-term risk profile, investors should consider future cash generation and potential drilling demand weaknesses.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Luke Putman Sells 30,276 Shares of Black Stone Min
feed.summary: Luke Putman, SVP, General Counsel, and Secretary at Black Stone Minerals LP, sold 30,276 shares of the company on March 5, 2026. This transaction leaves him with 761,417 shares. The company's stock is currently trading at $15.25 and is considered modestly overvalued based on its GF Value of $13.07.

feed.title: Turbo Energy Restructures Financing Amid AI Storage Growth
feed.summary: Turbo Energy S.A. has restructured its long-term financing with major banks, securing €4.87M ($5.3M) to support global expansion, particularly in AI-driven industrial electrification and energy storage. The company's stock has risen by 11.26%, driven by positive sentiment around its strategic advancements in AI-based solar-plus-storage solutions. This move aims to provide stability and mitigate industrial margin pressures amidst volatile global energy markets.

feed.title: Cosan (CSAN)'s JV, Raizen Discussing $765 Million Injection
feed.summary: Cosan S.A.'s joint venture with Shell Plc, Raizen, is considering a 4 billion real ($765 million) capital injection, with Shell proposing a larger share than Cosan and its controlling shareholder. This imbalance could lead to Shell gaining control due to potential dilution of Cosan's stake. Raizen is also exploring an out-of-court restructuring as its net debt previously increased to 55.3 billion reais due to various challenges.

feed.title: CVR Energy Experiences Evaluation Revision Amidst Market Dynamics and Performance Fluctuations
feed.summary: CVR Energy, a small-cap oil company, has seen its stock valuation revised due to market dynamics and performance fluctuations. Despite current volatility, with its stock at $27.08, the company has delivered a 49.45% return over the past year, significantly outperforming the S&P 500. Technical indicators show mixed signals, but past performance highlights the company's resilience within the oil sector.

feed.title: Assessing CVR Energy (CVI) Valuation After Carl Icahn’s Additional US$16.4 Million Share Purchase
feed.summary: Carl Icahn recently invested an additional US$16.4 million in CVR Energy (CVI) shares, increasing his stake despite a suspended dividend and a preliminary net loss for the company. While CVR Energy's stock has seen recent gains and a strong long-term return, it trades at a discount to analyst targets and some intrinsic value estimates, suggesting it might be undervalued. However, the company's high P/E ratio compared to the industry and peers raises questions about its current valuation.

feed.title: FuelCell Energy Q1 2026 earnings preview
feed.summary: This article provides a Q1 2026 earnings preview for FuelCell Energy (FCEL). It is the full content of the article from MSN.

feed.title: Canadian Solar Extends Battery Storage Footprint From Texas To Japan
feed.summary: Canadian Solar, through its subsidiary Recurrent Energy, has sold its 200 MWh Fort Duncan Battery Storage facility in Texas and deployed its first grid-connected battery energy storage system in Japan. These moves highlight the company's efforts to expand its battery storage capabilities beyond traditional solar module sales, aiming to strengthen its position in the global renewable infrastructure market. While the company's stock has seen mixed performance, these projects signify a strategy of capital recycling and geographical diversification, offering new potential earnings drivers but also exposing the company to ongoing financial and policy risks.

feed.title: Algonquin Power & Utilities Corp. Faces Financial Challenges Amid Changing Market Conditions
feed.summary: Algonquin Power & Utilities Corp. (AQN) is experiencing financial challenges, with its stock trading down amid investor concerns. The company faces increased operational expenses, volatile energy markets, and regulatory shifts in Europe, despite maintaining a strong gross margin. AQN's strategic direction involves navigating significant long-term debt and pursuing partnerships while balancing immediate fiscal responsibilities, making disciplined strategic choices crucial for future growth.

feed.title: Why Is Phillips 66 (PSX) Up 7.8% Since Last Earnings Report?
feed.summary: Phillips 66 (PSX) has seen its shares rise 7.8% since its last earnings report, outperforming the S&P 500, due to strong Q4 2025 results. The company reported adjusted earnings of $2.47 per share, beating estimates, driven by higher realized refining margins worldwide and increased contributions from its midstream segment. Despite this positive trend, estimates have been broadly trending downward, resulting in a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) for the stock.

feed.title: PPL Electric Utilities Corporation (PPL) Reaches Settlement in D
feed.summary: PPL Electric Utilities Corporation (PPL) has reached a non-unanimous settlement in principle regarding its distribution rate case with the Pennsylvania Public Utility Commission (PUC). This settlement, communicated on March 6, 2026, aims to ensure continued investment in a reliable electric system while focusing on customer affordability. The details will be filed for approval on March 10, 2026, with a final ruling from the PUC anticipated by the end of Q2 2026.

feed.title: Ford makes big shift into energy storage to keep battery plants busy
feed.summary: Ford is entering the battery energy storage system business, called Ford Energy, to repurpose a Kentucky factory originally planned for EV battery production, due to slower-than-expected electric vehicle sales. The company will produce large-scale energy storage units for utility companies and data centers, facing competition from established players like Tesla. Ford believes its manufacturing expertise and licensing of CATL technology give it an advantage in this new venture.

feed.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Industrial Goods Companies: Southwest Airlines (LUV), XPO (XPO) and MTR (OtherMTRJF)
feed.summary: This article provides an overview of recent analyst ratings for three industrial goods companies: Southwest Airlines (LUV), XPO (XPO), and MTR (MTRJF). Goldman Sachs maintained a Sell rating on Southwest Airlines, while Wells Fargo maintained a Buy rating on XPO. DBS downgraded MTR to Hold.

feed.title: Petrobras CEO says there is currently no talk of entering Venezuela
feed.summary: Petrobras CEO Magda Chambriard stated on Friday that the Brazilian state-run oil company currently has no plans to enter Venezuela. This decision comes as the U.S. has controlled Venezuela's oil exports since early January, following the capture of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro. Trading houses Trafigura and Vitol, along with Chevron, are currently responsible for exporting most of Venezuela's oil under U.S. authorizations.

feed.title: AES Indiana cancels more public open houses over safety threats, host concerns
feed.summary: AES Indiana has canceled more public open houses following explicit safety threats and concerns from host organizations. This marks the second time in a week that the utility company has had to alter its public engagement schedule due to safety issues, following a prior cancellation linked to social media threats. These cancellations coincide with increased public scrutiny and legislative interest regarding AES Indiana's operations and proposed transactions.

feed.title: Oppenheimer Raises Caterpillar (CAT) Price Target to $817 | CAT Stock News
feed.summary: Oppenheimer has increased its price target for Caterpillar (CAT) from $729.00 to $817.00, maintaining an "Outperform" rating. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook, with other analysts like Wells Fargo and Evercore ISI Group also recently raising their price targets for the company. Caterpillar is a leading global manufacturer of construction and mining equipment, and operates with a robust market position.

feed.title: Logistics Stock Looks Solid Despite AI Disruption Buzz
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (NASDAQ:CHRW) stock is showing resilience despite concerns about AI disruption in the logistics sector. The stock is maintaining key technical support levels, including its 50-day moving average and 200-unit trendline on its 65-minute chart, and is trading above its previous earnings report closing price. This performance suggests potential for a rally towards late 2025 highs, with significant put support evident at the 175 strike.

feed.title: Canadian Natural pauses $8.25-billion oil sands expansion, citing carbon policy uncertainty
feed.summary: Canadian Natural Resources Ltd. (CNRL) has halted its $8.25-billion Jackpine oil sands expansion due to regulatory uncertainty surrounding carbon pricing and methane policies. The company's president, Scott Stauth, stated that CNRL awaits clarification on federal and Alberta government negotiations on new rules and advocates for scrapping the industrial carbon price for projects utilizing carbon capture and storage technology. Despite the project pause, CNRL reported a record-setting production year in 2025 and made significant portfolio acquisitions, including full ownership of the Albian oil sands mines and natural gas properties in Alberta.

feed.title: Suburban Propane Partners LP Experiences Notable Revision in Stock Evaluation Score
feed.summary: Suburban Propane Partners LP, a small-cap company in the Trading & Distributors sector, has seen a notable revision in its stock evaluation. Despite a 2.53% return over the past year contrasting with the S&P 500's 18.89%, the company has outperformed with an 11.54% year-to-date return against the S&P 500's 0.35% gain. Technical indicators mostly suggest a positive trend, with bullish signals from MACD, Bollinger Bands, and moving averages, while the KST shows a mixed but mildly bullish Dow Theory outlook.

feed.title: Mammoth Energy (TUSK) Q4 2025 Earnings Transcript
feed.summary: Mammoth Energy (TUSK) reported its Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings, highlighting a strategic pivot involving significant asset divestitures and a substantial expansion into aviation rentals. While the company achieved portfolio restructuring and over $65 million of capital deployed into aviation, overall Q4 revenue was down, and the company posted a net loss due to execution and cost control issues in segments like fiber, sand, and drilling. Management anticipates over 50% revenue growth in 2026, primarily driven by aviation, and aims for mid-teens EBITDA margins and positive free cash flow by 2027, supported by a strong, debt-free balance sheet.

feed.title: Ultrapar Participações (UGP) Reports Q4 2025 Earnings, Here’s What You Need to Know
feed.summary: Ultrapar Participações S.A. (UGP) reported its Q4 2025 earnings, with revenue surpassing estimates at $6.66 billion and EPS largely in line with expectations at $0.0713. The company achieved record operational cash flow and recurring adjusted EBITDA, although net income for the quarter significantly decreased year-over-year due to non-recurring expenses and higher depreciation. Ultrapar Participações expects EPS of $0.46 for fiscal 2026 and $0.42 for fiscal 2027, with revenues projected to reach $26.52 billion and $26.72 billion, respectively.

feed.title: Option Traders Show Caution Toward Uber (UBER) as Stock Dips
feed.summary: Uber (UBER) stock recently experienced a minor decline, with options trading showing a preference for call options and an increase in implied volatility. Despite strong financial health marked by robust revenue growth and profitability, the company's valuation metrics present a mixed picture. Analysts remain positive, but increased put-call skew suggests a heightened interest among traders in downside protection.

feed.title: Saia (NASDAQ:SAIA) Trading Down 8.6% - Here's What Happened

feed.title: PlusAI Shatters AI Speed Records With SuperDrive 6.0, Targets Full Driverless Launch In 2026

Concurrently, the STGAP2SA and STGAP2HSA isolated gate drivers target high-voltage switching applications in electric and hybrid vehicles, supporting IGBTs and silicon MOSFETs up to 1,200 V with features for efficiency and safety.

feed.title: Brazil superintendence of regulator CADE approves IG4-Novonor deal for Braskem
feed.summary: Brazil's antitrust watchdog CADE has approved the agreement for private equity firm IG4 Capital to acquire a controlling stake in petrochemical firm Braskem from conglomerate Novonor. The deal, announced in December, received unconditional approval from CADE's general superintendence. The decision will become final in 15 days if no appeals are filed, allowing IG4 to share control of Braskem with state-run oil giant Petrobras, while Novonor retains a 4% stake.

feed.title: Russ Meyer: A Legacy That Shaped General Aviation

feed.title: Aquifer rebounds after International Paper closure

The Georgia Environmental Protection Division expected this recovery, which is now stabilizing, and is working to protect the aquifer for future use.

feed.title: Intuit Leads on a New Type of Renewable Energy Transaction: VPPA 2.0

The VPPA 2.0 model, designed for aggregated buyers, offers cost advantages and enables companies to cause new renewable energy capacity to be built.

feed.title: PlusAI unveils new autonomous trucking software with night driving and construction zone capabilities

feed.title: SMR Cooperation Gains Full Momentum in Thailand

The seminar, attended by experts from both countries, focused on SMR deployment conditions in Thailand, technical characteristics of innovative SMRs (i-SMR), and supply chain considerations. This collaboration is expected to advance Thailand's energy transition and expand SMR business in the ASEAN region.

feed.title: Ring Energy: Evidence Piles Up That The Acquisition Strategy Works (NYSE:REI)

(NYSE: AAP) announced that its CEO, Shane O'Kelly, and CFO, Ryan Grimsland, will present at the 2026 UBS Global Consumer and Retail Conference on Wednesday, March 11, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. ET. The brokerage also foresees "potential for traveler anxiety" which might lead to increased cancellations and weaker close-in bookings for the second quarter, particularly affecting European, Australian, and Asian brands and Pacific coast Mexico itineraries. Despite these challenges, William Blair rates Carnival's stock (CCL) as "outperform" with a $28.61 price target.

feed.title: Western Midstream Partners LP Experiences Revision in Its Stock Evaluation Score

The company's stock is currently at $42.19, has returned 10.01% over the past year (lagging the S&P 500), but has outperformed the S&P 500 year-to-date with a 6.81% return. Technical indicators show a bullish weekly MACD and Bollinger Bands, though monthly trends are mixed.

feed.title: Reasons Why You Should Retain Waste Management Stock for Now
feed.summary: Waste Management (WM) stock has outperformed its industry, driven by robust growth scores and positive earnings and revenue projections for 2026 and 2027. feed.title: CN February grain movement surges to record

This follows an impressive end to 2025 and builds on previous records in 2020 and 2024 for grain moved annually across Canada. CN continues to proactively coordinate with customers and partners to efficiently transport Canadian grain to markets.

feed.title: NIO Q4 Preview: From Breakeven Doubts To Pre-Profit, The Margin Expansion Story Begins
feed.summary: NIO is expected to report its Q4-2025 earnings, with management forecasting an adjusted operating profit of RMB 700M–1.2B, marking its first structurally profitable quarter. The company aims for margin expansion through a premium mix from its NIO brand and higher-margin ES6 and EC6 models. Despite the positive outlook, the stock remains range-bound and may be undervalued by the market, which could be underpricing NIO's growing operating leverage and shift towards a margin-driven profitability phase.

feed.title: United Airlines Holdings, Inc. Experiences Revision in Stock Evaluation Amid Market Dynamics

feed.title: Will Willdan Benefit as U.S. Electricity Demand Rises Again?
feed.summary: Willdan Group, Inc. (WLDN) is poised to benefit from the increasing electricity demand in the U.S., driven by AI data centers, electrification, and grid modernization. The company, which specializes in energy-related services, reported strong fiscal 2025 results with significant revenue growth. While facing competition from larger firms like AECOM and Jacobs Solutions, Willdan's specialized energy efficiency capabilities and utility relationships give it a differentiated edge in the expanding energy transition services market.

feed.title: BP seeks award of at least $3.7 billion from Venture Global arbitration win, Venture Global filing says
feed.summary: BP is demanding at least $3.7 billion in damages, and potentially over $6.0 billion, plus interest and legal fees, from its arbitration victory against Venture Global concerning liquefied natural gas (LNG) cargoes. This claim stems from Venture Global's failure to deliver LNG under a long-term contract that was supposed to begin in late 2022. Venture Global, however, has stated that BP's requested damages are "unserious and not supported by evidence or controlling law."

feed.title: Southwest’s Reserved Seating Change Is a Powerful Lesson in Leadership and Adaptability
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines has implemented a significant change by moving from its iconic open seating policy to reserved seating. This shift is presented as a leadership lesson, highlighting how companies must adapt and evolve even when it means altering long-standing traditions. While some customers may feel nostalgic for the old system, the change reflects a necessary adaptation to potentially outdated practices, despite the previous system's unique competitive energy and efficiency benefits.

feed.title: Eversource Energy (ES) Target Lifted to $79 at BMO After Q4 Results
feed.summary: BMO Capital has raised its price target on Eversource Energy (ES) to $79 from $75, maintaining a Market Perform rating, following the company's Q4 results and updated guidance. UBS also increased its price target to $80. Eversource Energy reported Q4 2025 non-GAAP EPS of $1.12 and introduced 2026 EPS guidance of $4.80–$4.95, reaffirming its long-term EPS CAGR target of 5%-7%.

feed.title: News: #Jimma University, #Boeing sign partnership to strengthen aerospace education Boeing and Jimma University have announced a long-term strategic partnership aimed at strengthening aviation sciences and aerospace engineering education and prep
feed.summary: Boeing and Jimma University have established a long-term strategic partnership to enhance aviation sciences and aerospace engineering education in Ethiopia and the broader African market. This collaboration will support the university's Aviation Sciences and Aerospace Engineering Academy through infrastructure improvements, curriculum development, and community engagement. The initiative seeks to develop Jimma into a leading aerospace center of excellence, with initial plans focusing on classroom modernization, specialized faculty training, and industry workshops starting in 2026.
