feed.title: Star Bulk Carriers Corp. Enters Conditional Agreement to Acquire Sixteen Vessels from Diana Shipping Inc. for $470.5 Million
feed.summary: Star Bulk Carriers Corp. has entered into a conditional agreement to acquire sixteen vessels from Diana Shipping Inc. for $470.5 million. This acquisition is contingent on Diana Shipping successfully acquiring Genco Shipping & Trading Ltd. The deal is expected to increase Star Bulk's fleet to 157 ships, enhance its market position, and generate additional revenue and cash flow, which could support dividend growth.

feed.title: Star Bulk to Acquire 16 Vessels from Diana Shipping Inc. Conditional Upon the Success of Its Offer to Acquire Genco Shipping & Trading Ltd

This acquisition is contingent upon Diana Shipping successfully acquiring Genco Shipping & Trading Ltd. Despite its strong position, Fluor's stock is currently trading at a discount compared to industry peers, and analysts forecast a 23.3% EPS growth for 2026.

feed.title: Star Bulk To Acquire 16 Vessels From Diana Shipping Inc. Conditional Upon The Success Of Its Offer To Acquire Genco Shipping & Trading Ltd

feed.summary: Genco Shipping & Trading Limited has announced the delivery of the Genco Stars and Stripes, the first of two high-specification, scrubber-fitted Newcastlemax vessels. feed.title: Akin Advises Kennedy Lewis Investment Management on $1.315 Billion Secured Term Loan for FTAI Infrastructure

feed.title: BP asks shareholders to reject call for more disclosures, trims board (BP:NYSE)

Additionally, the company has reduced the size of its board.

feed.summary: Imperial Petroleum reported strong unaudited Q4 and full-year 2025 results, driven by significant fleet expansion which nearly doubled quarterly revenue to $51.1 million and tripled profit to $15.0 million. The company achieved 91.8% fleet utilization, generated $50.0 million in net income for the year, and initiated a $10 million share repurchase program, reinforcing its capital return strategy. feed.summary: Cheniere Energy Partners (CQP) reported strong fourth-quarter 2025 results, beating earnings and revenue estimates. feed.title: Is FuelCell Energy Capable of Supplying Power to Data Centers When the Grid Faces Constraints?

The company's systems provide steady baseload power, can withstand grid outages, and use various fuel sources with low emissions. This strategy positions data centers as a key growth market for FCEL, which has seen its stock surge significantly in the past six months and holds a "Hold" rating from Zacks.

feed.title: Global Ship Lease (GSL) Q4 Adjusted EPS Drops 9 % to $2.32, EBITDA Hits $124.7M

The company's revenue climbed to $190.9 million, and adjusted EBITDA reached $124.7 million, showing strong performance in the marine shipping sector. Investors are now looking to management commentary for guidance on charter rates and fleet utilization for 2026.

feed.title: Array Technologies, Inc. (ARRY) Expands Solar Tracker Business with Record Order Book
feed.summary: Array Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ:ARRY) recently announced strong financial results, with a record $2.2 billion order book and 35% annual volume growth, despite a decrease in gross margin. feed.title: Is WEC Energy Group’s (WEC) New Connectivity Push Quietly Reshaping Its Grid Modernization Strategy?

This move integrates advanced connectivity into WEC's grid modernization efforts and large capital expenditure plan. While the technology improves reliability, the core investment narrative for WEC remains tied to its US$28 billion capital plan through 2029, focusing on regulatory approvals for cost recovery and managing financing risks.

feed.title: U.S. Bancorp Impact Finance raises $5.7B in tax credit capital
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp Impact Finance successfully raised $5.7 billion in third-party capital during 2025, marking a historic achievement for the company. This capital was deployed across 109 transactions with 58 institutional investors, supporting affordable housing, renewable energy, and economic development projects. The company's robust financial health and its significant role in tax credit syndication were highlighted, alongside recent positive analyst ratings for its parent company, U.S. Bancorp.

feed.title: Nuvve Holding Corp. (NVVE) has officially announced, through a filing submitted to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), that the company has acquired the development option for a 50 MW battery energy storage project located in Malvik, Sweden.
feed.summary: Nuvve Holding Corp. (NVVE) has announced the acquisition of a development option for a 50 MW battery energy storage project in Malvik, Sweden, through a filing with the SEC. This move signifies a strategic expansion into the European energy storage market. The project aims to provide stable energy storage and support renewable energy integration and grid resilience.

feed.title: Nuvve Expands European Battery Storage Through OMNIA Partnership
feed.summary: Nuvve Holding Corp. has entered into a significant partnership with Oelion AB and OMNIA Group Holdings AG, securing options and exclusive rights for European battery storage projects. This collaboration includes ownership and management of a 50MW Swedish Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) from Q2 2026 and aims to develop a 1GW pipeline within 24 months. The deal positions Nuvve to capture substantial revenues in the Nordic and broader European BESS markets.

feed.title: According to the latest filing submitted to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), Nuvve Holding Corp. (NASDAQ: NVVE) has officially obtained the exclusive rights to provide energy aggregation and consulting services for the Omnia project in Europe.
feed.summary: Nuvve Holding Corp. (NASDAQ: NVVE) has secured exclusive rights for energy aggregation and consulting services for the Omnia project in Europe, as per an SEC filing. This partnership uses Nuvve's V2G technology to offer flexible balancing services and professional solutions across multiple European countries. The agreement is expected to strengthen Nuvve's role in the European virtual power plant sector and accelerate regional microgrid commercialization.

feed.title: Trump Shrugs Off Oil Prices At $88: 'If They Rise, They Rise'—What Do Prediction Markets Say?
feed.summary: Former President Donald Trump has expressed indifference to rising gas prices, stating, "If they rise, they rise," amidst an Iran war that has pushed crude oil to $88. Prediction markets on Polymarket suggest a significant probability of oil prices reaching $90, $100, or even higher, with potential recessionary implications if sustained above $100. The conflict has also disrupted shipping in the Persian Gulf, leading to a supply crunch and further upward pressure on oil prices.

feed.title: Marathon Petroleum Corp. Hits New 52-Week High at $228.55
feed.summary: Marathon Petroleum Corp. has achieved a new 52-week high of $228.55, reflecting its strong performance in the oil industry with a 24.68% return over the past year, outperforming the S&P 500. The company boasts a significant dividend yield of 86.62% and demonstrates resilience despite past profit declines, supported by steady growth in net sales and operating profit.

feed.title: Rocket Lab (RKLB) Introduces Silicon Solar Arrays for Space-Based Data Centers
feed.summary: Rocket Lab Corporation (RKLB) has introduced advanced silicon solar arrays designed to power gigawatt-scale space-based data centers, addressing terrestrial constraints on land, water, and power. This innovative solution aims to provide scalable, low-cost power by harnessing space's limitless solar energy and cold environment, reducing reliance on geopolitically sensitive materials. The company's expansion into flexible silicon modules, supported by a $23.9 million award from the US CHIPS and Science Act, underscores a move towards more resilient and domestically produced critical space infrastructure.

feed.title: NIO Inc. marks production milestone of 2 million e-drive units

This achievement highlights NIO's robust in-house capabilities in developing and manufacturing core electric powertrain components. The two-millionth unit is a rear electric drive system for the NIO ES8, a model that recently surpassed 70,000 cumulative deliveries.

feed.title: Coterra Energy (NYSE:CTRA) Downgraded to "Hold" Rating by Siebert Williams Shank
feed.summary: Siebert Williams Shank has downgraded Coterra Energy (NYSE:CTRA) from a "buy" to a "hold" rating, though the stock still maintains a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts with an average price target of $33.95. The energy company's stock opened at $31.54, near its 52-week high, with a market capitalization of $23.95 billion. Institutional investors and hedge funds hold a significant 87.92% of the company's stock.

feed.title: Ford’s new battery business seeks to spin profits out of costly EV investments
feed.summary: Ford is establishing a new battery business to leverage its investments in electric vehicle (EV) battery production. The company aims to generate profits from these ventures by producing battery cells for both residential energy storage solutions and EVs, starting with its Marshall, Michigan plant in 2026. This strategic move is intended to recoup the significant costs associated with EV development.

feed.title: Beam Global (BEEM) Advances Off-Grid EV Charging and Autonomous Vehicle Technology
feed.summary: Beam Global (BEEM) has partnered with HEVO Inc. to launch the first integrated autonomous wireless charging system, powered by EV ARC off-grid solar EV charging, targeting the autonomous vehicle sector. The company also secured an order for its patented battery products from a drone company for various robotic applications. Beam Global is recognized as a clean technology company focusing on sustainable, rapidly deployed solar-powered EV charging solutions.

feed.title: U.S. Bancorp Impact Finance raises $5.7B in tax credit capital
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp Impact Finance successfully raised $5.7 billion in third-party capital in 2025, marking a historic high for the company. This capital supported 109 transactions across affordable housing, renewable energy, and economic development projects, financing over 6,800 affordable housing units and 5.2 gigawatts of clean energy capacity. The achievement highlights the financial stability of U.S. Bancorp, along with recent positive analyst upgrades and strategic executive changes.

feed.title: Algonquin Power & Utilities Corp. (AQN) Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings: Revenue Hits $630.7M
feed.summary: Algonquin Power & Utilities Corp. (AQN) reported strong Q4 and full-year 2025 results, with Q4 adjusted EPS of $0.06 and revenue of $630.7M both beating analyst estimates. The company completed a transformational shift to a pure-play regulated utility, selling its renewable energy business and using the proceeds to reduce debt. CEO Rod West reaffirmed the 2026 adjusted net EPS outlook and outlined a $3.2 billion capital plan for 2026-2028, underscoring investor confidence in its "Back to Basics" strategy.

feed.title: Intercontinental Exchange Plans to Launch Four Container Freight Futures Indexed to Nyshex Freight Indices
feed.summary: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) announced its plan to launch four new container freight futures contracts. These futures will be indexed to the New York Shipping Exchange's (NYSHEX) Freight Indices, which are calculated by ICE Data Services and serve as benchmarks for containerized freight pricing. The contracts, covering key shipping routes between the U.S., Asia, and Europe, are slated for an April 7, 2026 launch, pending regulatory approval, and aim to help the market manage container freight risk.

feed.title: Connecticut Land-Clearing Contractor Adds Second Astec-Peterson Grinder
feed.summary: Connecticut Clearing, a land-clearing business owned by Dean Smith, has acquired a second Astec-Peterson horizontal grinder, a 5710E model, from Barry Equipment. This addition allows the company to handle a greater volume of grinding work and conduct full-scale land-clearing operations more efficiently across various project types in Connecticut. The new grinder is significantly more productive than the previous model, enhancing the company's capability to process wood debris, stumps, and root balls on-site.

feed.title: Tsakos: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Tsakos Energy Navigation Ltd. (TEN) reported fourth-quarter earnings of $58 million, or $1.70 per share. The oil and gas shipping company's revenue for the quarter was $222.1 million, with adjusted revenue at $195.3 million. For the full year, Tsakos reported a profit of $160.9 million, or $4.45 per share, on revenue of $676.7 million.

feed.title: Traffic Will Be A Mess As Xcel Energy Moves Wind Turbines
feed.summary: Xcel Energy will be transporting generators and turbines across Northern Colorado this weekend, starting Saturday morning around 7 AM. These slow-moving vehicles, traveling at about 5 MPH, will cause significant traffic delays on I-25, Highway 287, and Colorado Highway 66, particularly near Longmont and Lyons. This is the first of four planned moves as part of Colorado's 2024 Clean Energy Plan to add 6,100 megawatts of clean generation capacity to the St. Vrain Generating Station.

feed.title: U.S. Bank Raises $5.7 Billion Through Tax Credit Syndications in 2025
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp Impact Finance reported a record-breaking 2025, raising approximately $5.7 billion in third-party capital through tax credit syndications from 58 institutional investors across 109 transactions. These funds are designated for financing affordable housing, renewable energy, and economic development projects, marking the highest annual total in the company's history. The firm also surpassed $7 billion in tax credit transfers since the Inflation Reduction Act of 2023, indicating significant growth and market maturity in tax credit solutions.

feed.title: Cameco uranium agreement a highlight of Canada-India deals
feed.summary: Canada and India have strengthened their partnership with multiple long-term deals, most notably a significant agreement for Cameco to supply India with nearly 22 million pounds of uranium concentrate over the next decade. This deal, valued at approximately C$2.6 billion, is substantially larger than their previous 2015 agreement and supports India's ambitious plans to expand its nuclear power capacity. Officials from both countries, including the Prime Ministers and Cameco's CEO, highlighted the strategic importance of this collaboration for energy security and economic development.

feed.title: Star Bulk to Acquire 16 Vessels from Diana Shipping for $470.5 Million
feed.summary: Star Bulk Carriers Corp. has entered into a conditional Sale and Purchase Agreement with Diana Shipping Inc. to acquire 16 vessels for $470.5 million in cash. This acquisition, which includes various vessel types totaling 1.8 million dwt, is expected to enhance Star Bulk's fleet size, market competitiveness, and earnings power. The purchase will be funded through existing cash, reserved funds, and new debt financing, with the CEO emphasizing the strategic importance of consolidation in the dry bulk sector.

feed.title: Earnings Summary: Imperial Ptrl Q4
feed.summary: Imperial Ptrl (NASDAQ: IMPP) reported its Q4 earnings on March 6, 2026, beating estimated earnings per share (EPS) by 14.71% and showing a significant increase in revenue compared to the previous year. The company posted an EPS of $0.39 against an estimate of $0.34, with Q4 revenue up by $24.93 million. This performance follows a previous quarter where the company also beat EPS estimates.

feed.title: Equinor, Wellesley Tie Up for Norwegian Exploration
feed.summary: Equinor and Wellesley Petroleum have formed a joint exploration campaign to drill up to 15 wells in Norwegian waters between 2027 and 2030. This collaboration aims to counter the expected natural decline in production on the Norwegian continental shelf (NCS) by focusing on high-pressure, high-temperature (HPHT) opportunities in the Northern North Sea. The partnership combines Equinor's regional expertise with Wellesley's exploration model to maximize resource recovery and extend the life of offshore facilities.

feed.title: Mammoth Energy Services, Inc. Announces Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Mammoth Energy Services, Inc. reported its financial and operational results for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2025. The company experienced a slight revenue decrease in Q4 2025 but showed significant improvement in its full-year net loss compared to 2024, driven by strategic divestitures and investments in its aviation platform. Mammoth aims for improved results in 2026 through internal initiatives and favorable market conditions.

feed.title: CVR Energy Experiences Revision in Stock Evaluation Amid Market Dynamics
feed.summary: CVR Energy, Inc. has seen a revision in its stock evaluation due to changing market conditions. The company's stock price has increased slightly, and it has significantly outperformed the S&P 500 over the past year with a 26.82% return. Despite mixed technical indicators and volatility in the oil sector, CVR Energy's adaptation skills will be key to maintaining its competitive stance.

feed.title: United Capital Management of KS Inc. Purchases 4,621 Shares of Cheniere Energy, Inc. $LNG
feed.summary: United Capital Management of KS Inc. increased its stake in Cheniere Energy (NYSE:LNG) by 18.8% in the third quarter, acquiring an additional 4,621 shares to bring its total holdings to 29,257 shares valued at approximately $6.88 million. Cheniere Energy also recently announced a $10.00 billion share buyback program and a quarterly dividend of $0.555 per share, reflecting strong financial performance as the company reported Q3 EPS of $10.68 against an expected $3.90 and revenue of $5.45 billion. The stock currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average price target of $264.89.

feed.title: Cheniere Energy Announces New Senior Notes Offering
feed.summary: Cheniere Energy (LNG) announced a new senior notes offering totaling $1.75 billion, consisting of $1 billion at 5.200% due 2036 and $750 million at 6.000% due 2056. The proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, including refinancing existing debt and funding capital expenditures. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates LNG as "Outperform" due to strong financial performance, positive earnings outlook, and supportive valuation.

feed.title: White Falcon Picked Canadian Solar (CSIQ) on Valuation Grounds
feed.summary: White Falcon Capital Management, a fund manager, highlighted Canadian Solar Inc. (CSIQ) in its Q4 2025 investor letter, noting the company's valuation as a key reason for their investment. Despite initial skepticism about solar stocks, White Falcon found CSIQ to be trading at a significant discount to its sum-of-the-parts value, supported by solar's growing cost-effectiveness and the company's strong management. The firm believes CSIQ, with a cost base under $10, has strong re-rating potential, especially as solar plus storage is seen as a viable option for supplying clean power to data centers, making it an AI beneficiary.

feed.title: AECOM Wins Seattle Transit Contracts And Expands Long Term Project Visibility
feed.summary: AECOM (NYSE:ACM) has secured multi-year contracts with Sound Transit for major transit expansions in the Seattle region, providing design, engineering, planning, and construction management services. These contracts reinforce AECOM's role in long-term public transportation investments and offer extended visibility on project pipelines amidst ongoing mass transit funding across the U.S. While offering potential for steady revenue, investors should consider risks like reliance on government funding and execution challenges, balanced against the company's strengthened position against competitors.

feed.title: Is NACCO Industries, Inc. (NC) One of the Best Coal Mining Stocks to Buy Right Now?
feed.summary: NACCO Industries, Inc. (NYSE: NC) is highlighted as one of the best coal mining stocks after reporting strong fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results, with operating profit nearly doubling. Despite a revenue drop and a net loss due to a pension settlement charge, the company's underlying performance improved, and it declared a regular quarterly cash dividend. NACCO primarily operates surface coal mines for power generation and provides contract mining services for various minerals.

feed.title: American Superconductor Stock Performance & Valuation | 2026 Analysis - News and Statistics
feed.summary: American Superconductor (AMSC) has experienced a six-month stock price decline, now at $32.16, despite strong company fundamentals including consistent sales growth exceeding the industrials sector average and expanding free cash flow margin of 5.7%. The company, established in 1987, has successfully transitioned from superconductor research to power systems for energy grids and naval technology. This analysis also broadens into a comprehensive report on the United States static converter industry, detailing demand, supply, trade flows, pricing, and competitive landscapes with forecasts extending to 2035.

feed.title: SPAC attack: Einride bets $1.35B that their SPAC will be different
feed.summary: Swedish electric and autonomous trucking company Einride is pursuing a $1.35 billion SPAC deal with Legato Merger Corp. III, following a $113 million oversubscribed capital raise. This valuation is lower than previous projections but Einride executives view it as a win, distinguishing their approach by focusing on a digital freight and logistics platform rather than direct EV manufacturing. They aim to avoid the failures of past EV startup SPACs by sourcing trucks from partners and leveraging their established autonomous driving technology.

feed.title: RyanAir shares under pressure amid Middle East conflict 📉
feed.summary: Ryanair shares have fallen over 10% from recent highs due to market concerns about weaker demand for routes in the Middle East and surrounding areas amid ongoing conflict. Despite very strong recent results, including record passenger numbers and high load factors, the security situation, rising jet fuel prices, and a more cautious consumer are weighing on investor sentiment. The company's future passenger growth targets may be disrupted by the Iran conflict, despite a recent upgrade in its traffic outlook.

feed.title: UPDATE: Critical Materials Investments Are Kicking Off As Demand Rises - Sprott's SETM ETF Offers Wide Exposure
feed.summary: The article discusses the increasing global demand for critical materials, driven by technological advancements and geopolitical tensions, particularly China's dominance in rare earths. It highlights the efforts of the U.S. and its allies to secure critical mineral supply chains through agreements and investments. The Sprott Critical Materials ETF (SETM) is presented as an investment vehicle offering diversified exposure to companies involved in the exploration, refinement, and distribution of these essential materials.

feed.title: South Dakota Investment Council Makes New Investment in Oceaneering International, Inc. $OII
feed.summary: The South Dakota Investment Council has acquired a new stake in Oceaneering International (NYSE:OII) during Q3, purchasing 41,600 shares valued at approximately $1.03 million. Institutional investors collectively own 93.93% of the company, while insiders have sold a significant number of shares recently. Analysts currently rate OII as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus target price of $30.33, following recent price target upgrades from Barclays and TD Cowen.

feed.title: Can FuelCell Energy Power Data Centers Amid Grid Limits?
feed.summary: FuelCell Energy (FCEL) is promoting its carbonate fuel cell technology as a solution for data centers facing power grid limitations, particularly driven by the demand from AI and cloud computing. The company highlights its modular, behind-the-meter systems as offering quicker deployment, scalability, and independence from strained utility grids, along with operational benefits like continuous power and low emissions. Competitors like Bloom Energy (BE) and Enphase Energy (ENPH) are also addressing the growing power needs of data centers with their respective energy solutions.

feed.title: Granite Construction Weighs Nevada I-80 Contract Upside Against Impairment Risks
feed.summary: Granite Construction (NYSE:GVA) has secured a preliminary agreement for the Nevada I-80 expansion project, while also disclosing potential risks related to goodwill and intangible asset impairments. The company's stock has seen significant returns over the past year, indicating investor expectations. This news highlights both new infrastructure opportunities and the need for investors to closely monitor the company's financial statements regarding balance sheet strength and impairment disclosures.

feed.title: Proud to be American: American Airlines partners with America250 to celebrate nation’s 250th anniversary
feed.summary: American Airlines has announced a partnership with America250, the national organization leading the commemoration of the signing of the Declaration of Independence. This collaboration will see American Airlines, which is also celebrating its centennial in 2026, play a significant role in the nationwide celebration of the United States' 250th anniversary, including painting two aircraft in America250 livery and serving as the Official Airline of America Innovates. The partnership underscores American's deep ties to U.S. history and its commitment to connecting communities.

feed.title: XTI Aerospace to Participate in 2026 Wharton Aerospace Conference on Innovation and National Security
feed.summary: XTI Aerospace (Nasdaq: XTIA) is participating in the 2026 Wharton Aerospace Conference in Philadelphia, focusing on innovation and national security. Steve Zohrabian, Executive Vice President of XTI Aircraft, will join a panel discussing disruptive capacity and meeting defense demands with faster innovation and scalable manufacturing. The company emphasizes its commitment to advancing aerospace technology, particularly in unmanned systems for enterprise, government, and allied missions.

feed.title: Worksport is Building Infrastructure for Scale, Not Hype
feed.summary: Worksport Ltd. (NASDAQ:WKSP) is focusing on building robust production, distribution, and compliance infrastructure rather than relying on market hype. Recent developments, including record monthly revenue, strategic partnerships, and significant growth in quarterly revenue and gross profit, demonstrate the company's methodical approach to scaling its clean energy solutions and automotive accessories business. This strategy positions Worksport for long-term operational traction by ensuring product readiness, market access, and regulatory compliance.

feed.title: $16.2M clean energy push: how Worksport is wiring for demand
feed.summary: Worksport (NASDAQ: WKSP) is prioritizing infrastructure development over hype to support its clean energy initiatives, evidenced by strong financial performance in 2025 including 91% revenue growth and expanded gross margins. The company is advancing with manufacturing partnerships, comprehensive certification processes for its products like COR and Aetherlux, and strategic collaborations to access federal and commercial markets. These efforts build upon a stable automotive accessories business, positioning Worksport for scalable growth in the clean energy sector.

feed.title: Stifel raises Plains All American stock price target on capex cut
feed.summary: Stifel has increased its price target for Plains All American Pipeline LP (PAA) to $25.00 from $23.00, maintaining a Buy rating due to reduced capital expenditure forecasts and the company's ability to increase distributions in 2026. This comes despite Plains All American missing analyst expectations for Q4 2025 earnings and revenue. Other analysts like Goldman Sachs, UBS, and Scotiabank have offered varied perspectives, with price target adjustments ranging from $17.00 to $25.00, reflecting mixed views on the company's future performance.

feed.title: U.S. Bank Raises $5.7 Billion Through Tax Credit Syndications in 2025
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp Impact Finance reported a record-breaking 2025, raising approximately $5.7 billion in third-party capital through 109 tax credit syndication transactions from 58 institutional investors. These funds are designated for affordable housing, renewable energy, and economic development projects nationwide. The company highlighted increased investor interest in tax credit transfers and continued growth in the market.

feed.title: Quantum Leap Energy (NASDAQ: ASPI) inks U.S. nuclear fuel MOU
feed.summary: ASP Isotopes Inc., through its subsidiary Quantum Leap Energy (QLE), has signed a non-binding Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with a large U.S. energy company that operates nuclear power stations. This MOU aims to evaluate support for QLE's plans to build advanced nuclear fuel cycle facilities in the United States, producing high assay low enriched uranium (HALEU) and low enriched uranium plus (LEU+). This initiative seeks to strengthen the domestic nuclear fuel supply chain in anticipation of a federal ban on Russian uranium imports and increasing demand for next-generation reactors.

feed.title: Nuvve Launches European BESS Venture With Omnia and Oelion; Option on 50 MW Project, 20-Year Services Deal
feed.summary: Nuvve has entered into three coordinated agreements with Omnia Group Holdings and Oelion to advance its energy storage strategy in Europe. These agreements include an option for Nuvve on a 50 MW Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) project in Sweden, exclusive rights to provide energy aggregation services for Omnia's future European projects, and a 20-year managerial services agreement with first-year fees of approximately $1.35 million. The deal also involves consideration of common shares and potential Series B preferred shares for Nuvve.

feed.title: Nuvve Partners with OMNIA Global to Address 1GW Development Pipeline with First 50MW/75MWh Battery Energy Storage System in Sweden
feed.summary: Nuvve Holding Corp. has partnered with OMNIA Global to develop a 1 GW pipeline of battery energy storage systems (BESS) over the next 24 months, starting with a 50MW/75MWh BESS in Sweden expected to commence operations in Q2 2026. Nuvve will own and manage the facility, providing market access services to optimize revenue in Sweden, a strategic market for energy storage due to its climate goals and high renewable energy penetration. This collaboration expands Nuvve's European footprint and aims to generate significant recurring revenue through asset ownership and management.

feed.title: UBS raises Sempra Energy stock price target to $100 on growth outlook
feed.summary: UBS has increased its price target for Sempra Energy (NYSE:SRE) to $100 from $96, while maintaining a Neutral rating. The firm acknowledged the company's strong past year performance, its shift towards regulated operations, and consistent dividend increases. However, UBS also noted concerns from bears regarding flat earnings forecasts, potential liabilities from the Edison International lawsuit, and the need for a discount on California assets.

feed.title: Sunpointe LLC Has $643,000 Holdings in NextEra Energy, Inc. $NEE
feed.summary: Sunpointe LLC significantly reduced its stake in NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) by 61.1% in Q3, ending with holdings valued at approximately $643,000. Meanwhile, NextEra Energy's CEO and other insiders have sold a considerable number of shares recently. Despite insider selling, the company has increased its quarterly dividend and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with target price upgrades.

feed.title: GE Aerospace: Blue-Chip Performance, Red-Line Valuation
feed.summary: GE Aerospace is a high-quality company with strong growth and a clean balance sheet, but its current stock price reflects a "Very High" valuation. The stock has historically shown "Very Weak" resilience during market downturns, making the current entry point risky for value-conscious investors. The article suggests waiting for a tactical dip due to potential volatility and offers the High Quality (HQ) Portfolio as an alternative for those seeking superior returns with lower risk.

feed.title: Truist reiterates Buy rating on Albemarle stock on lithium momentum
feed.summary: Truist Securities has reiterated a Buy rating and a $210 price target for Albemarle (NYSE:ALB) due to sustained lithium pricing momentum, driven by strong demand from electric vehicles and grid storage. The firm believes the stock is undervalued and expects continued upside from Albemarle's competitive position and cost discipline. Recent company developments include a quarterly dividend declaration, board appointments, and a stock increase following Zimbabwe's lithium export suspension.

feed.title: Worksport is Building Infrastructure for Scale, Not Hype
feed.summary: Worksport Ltd. is strategically building its operational infrastructure for scale rather than focusing on hype, as evidenced by recent developments in late 2025 and early 2026. The company is prioritizing scalable production, market access through strategic partnerships, and obtaining crucial certifications for its clean energy products like Aetherlux heat pumps and COR portable energy systems. This methodical approach, coupled with significant revenue growth and margin expansion, indicates a strong foundation for future expansion beyond its core automotive accessories business.

feed.title: 50,000 Shares in APA Corporation $APA Bought by United Capital Management of KS Inc.
feed.summary: United Capital Management of KS Inc. recently acquired 50,000 shares of APA Corporation, valued at approximately $1.214 million, during the third quarter. This move is part of significant institutional activity, with other major investors also increasing their stakes in APA. The company reported strong quarterly earnings, beating estimates, and the stock is trading near its 12-month high with a market cap of $11.4 billion.

feed.title: 5 Oil Stocks with Highest Upside Potential
feed.summary: This article lists the top five oil stocks with the highest upside potential, focusing on National Energy Services Reunited Corp. (NASDAQ: NESR) as a prime example. It details analyst rating updates and financial performance for NESR, highlighting its strong fiscal Q4 and full-year 2025 results. The article also includes a promotional section for an AI investment report.

feed.title: Alto Ingredients, Inc. (NASDAQ:ALTO) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Alto Ingredients, Inc. (NASDAQ:ALTO) reported strong Q4 and full-year 2025 results, demonstrating significant improvements in earnings and adjusted EBITDA due to increased crush margins, 45Z credits, and renewable fuel export sales. The company achieved a net income of $21.5 million for Q4 2025 and $12.1 million for the full year, a substantial increase from the previous year. Alto Ingredients plans to continue focusing on operational excellence, cost discipline, and strategic investments in 2026, including increasing production capacity at its Pekin Dry Mill and expanding CO2 utilization.

feed.title: FedEx Momentum Soars On Possible Billion-Dollar Windfall As Federal Court Rejects Trump's Bid To Stall Tariff Refunds
feed.summary: FedEx is experiencing significant momentum due to a robust technical profile and a recent federal court decision. This ruling rejects a bid to delay tariff refunds, potentially leading to a substantial cash infusion for FedEx, which had sued for a "full refund" of duties paid. The company also has an ambitious strategy to increase operating income by $3 billion by 2029, and its stock has shown strong performance year-to-date, outperforming broader indices.

feed.title: Victory Capital Management Inc. Has $84.32 Million Stock Holdings in Cummins Inc. $CMI
feed.summary: Victory Capital Management Inc. reduced its stake in Cummins Inc. (NYSE:CMI) by 2.2% in the third quarter, now holding 199,636 shares valued at approximately $84.32 million. Cummins reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates with an EPS of $5.81 and revenue of $8.54 billion, and recently paid a $2.00 quarterly dividend. Despite notable insider selling, institutional ownership remains high at 83.46%, and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with a consensus target price of $588.93.

feed.title: Victory Capital Management Inc. Sells 108,602 Shares of Williams Companies, Inc. (The) $WMB
feed.summary: Victory Capital Management Inc. reduced its stake in Williams Companies (NYSE:WMB) by 8.8%, selling 108,602 shares and retaining 1,123,903 shares valued at approximately $71.2 million. Other hedge funds also adjusted their positions in WMB. Williams Companies recently increased its quarterly dividend to $0.525 per share and reported Q4 earnings, missing EPS estimates but exceeding revenue expectations.

feed.title: ICE to Launch NYSHEX Container Freight Futures
feed.summary: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) announced its plan to launch four container freight futures contracts, indexed to the New York Shipping Exchange’s (NYSHEX) Freight Indices (NYFI), on April 7, 2026, subject to regulatory approval. These contracts are designed to provide new tools for risk management in the volatile container shipping industry. The NYFI indices, for which ICE Data Services is the calculation agent, are based on actual shipped transactions, ensuring the futures contracts settle on real market prices.

feed.title: 200 years of gas know-how: Teledyne GMI and Southern Cross team up in U.S.
feed.summary: Teledyne GMI and Southern Cross have formed a strategic partnership to enhance gas and leak detection solutions for U.S. gas utilities. This collaboration combines 200 years of industry experience, aiming to improve safety, operational efficiency, and data accuracy through Teledyne GMI's advanced instruments and Southern Cross's distribution and service network. The partnership will provide localized expertise and support to the gas utility sector in the Southeast United States.

feed.title: Here Are Friday’s Top Wall Street Analyst Research Calls: BorgWarner, CoreWeave, Intuit, Marvell Technology, Netflix, Okta, Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Trade Desk, and More
feed.summary: This article summarizes top Wall Street analyst upgrades, downgrades, and initiations announced on Friday, March 6, 2026. It also reviews the market's performance, including stock futures, treasury bonds, oil and gas, gold, and cryptocurrency, ahead of the February non-farm payroll report. Key analyst actions include upgrades for BorgWarner, Intuit, Marvell Technology, Netflix, and Okta, and downgrades for AES Corp., Delek Logistics Partners, Tanger, Trade Desk, and Zoetis.

feed.title: JPMorgan Raises New Jersey Resources Corporation (NJR) Price Target to $56
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase raised its price target on New Jersey Resources Corporation (NJR) to $56 from $52, reiterating an Overweight rating. This follows NJR's strong fiscal Q1 2026 performance and a guidance raise, aligning with a 7%–9% long-term NFEPS growth target. The company also reaffirmed a substantial five-year CapEx plan focusing on natural gas and clean energy, supported by solid balance sheet metrics.

feed.title: FTC Solar, Inc. (NASDAQ:FTCI) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: FTC Solar, Inc. (NASDAQ:FTCI) reported its Q4 2025 earnings, revealing strong growth with revenue up 26% sequentially and 149% year-over-year, and achieving its best gross margin and adjusted EBITDA performance as a public company. The company also announced significant commercial momentum, including new multi-year Master Supply Agreements (MSAs) totaling over 9 gigawatts, and aims to become a top three tracker provider. Despite regulatory uncertainties in 2025 that impacted Q1 2026 forecasts, FTC Solar expresses optimism for continued accelerated growth in the latter half of 2026 due to increasing market demand for diversification and its competitive product offerings.

feed.title: Ecopetrol Posts Solid 2025 Earnings, Boosts Reserves and Surpasses Energy Transition Targets
feed.summary: Ecopetrol S.A. reported solid audited full-year 2025 results, maintaining production and achieving record refining throughputs despite lower Brent prices. The company strengthened its reserve base, accelerated energy transition efforts, and proposed a COP 110 per share dividend for 2026. While an analyst issued a Sell rating with a $12.00 price target, TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates EC as Outperform due to strong technical indicators and attractive valuation, despite financial challenges.

feed.title: Eni Discloses Share Sale by Internal Audit Director Under EU Rules
feed.summary: Eni S.p.A. announced that its Internal Audit Director, Gianfranco Cariola, sold 7,675 ordinary Eni shares for €20.40 each on March 6, 2026. This disclosure complies with EU market abuse regulations, providing transparency on insider dealings. Eni is an integrated energy company with a current market capitalization of €61.54 billion.

feed.title: Nio launches Firefly brand in Thailand, entering 2nd right-hand drive market
feed.summary: Nio has officially launched its Firefly brand in Bangkok, Thailand, marking its second right-hand drive market after Singapore, as part of its global expansion strategy. This move helps Nio bypass trade barriers and positions Southeast Asia as a crucial growth area. Firefly, which focuses on urban mobility with its compact EV, plans to expand into nearly 40 markets across Europe, the Americas, and Southeast Asia.

feed.title: Goldman Sees $100 Oil Coming, And One Income ETF Bets 47% They’re Right
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs predicts oil prices could surge to $100 a barrel due to escalating geopolitical tensions, particularly around the Strait of Hormuz. The Strive U.S. Energy ETF (DRLL), which focuses on maximizing oil and gas production, is heavily invested in Exxon Mobil and Chevron (47.19% of holdings), making it particularly sensitive to such price increases. While DRLL has seen significant year-to-date gains, critics point to its past underperformance compared to peers and higher expense ratio.

feed.title: Riley Exploration Permian, Inc. (AMEX:REPX) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Riley Exploration Permian (REPX) held its Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings call, highlighting a transformative year driven by strategic acquisitions, debt reduction, and a new share repurchase program. The company detailed plans for over 20% year-over-year oil volume growth in 2026, supported by increased activity levels and infrastructure improvements in New Mexico. Management emphasized flexibility in their capital plan based on oil price environments and discussed ongoing optimization efforts in drilling and completion to enhance efficiency and productivity.

feed.title: Baytex Energy Corp. (NYSE:BTE) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Baytex Energy Corp. held its Q4 2025 earnings call, marking a significant transition for the company following the sale of its Eagle Ford assets, repositioning itself as a focused Canadian oil producer with a strong balance sheet. CEO Eric Greager announced his succession by Chad Lundberg, effective after the May AGM, highlighting the company's clear strategy for disciplined, long-term value creation. The call detailed strong operational performance in 2025 with 6% organic growth in Canadian production, significant reserve growth, and a focus on Duvernay and heavy oil assets, alongside plans for exploration and enhanced recovery methods like waterfloods. The company also discussed its financial position, including achieving a net cash position of $857 million and commitment to returning a significant portion of the Eagle Ford proceeds to shareholders through share buybacks and dividends, while maintaining a flexible growth strategy for 2026.

feed.title: S&P 500 Futures Fall in Premarket Trading; Ingram Micro, Gap Lag
feed.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced its worst week in nearly a year, falling by 1% due to a surge in oil prices and an unexpected decline in the February jobs report. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite also saw significant drops, marking consecutive weekly declines for all three major indexes. Despite weak labor data, traders see a low probability of an interest rate cut in the first half of the year, further compounded by ongoing geopolitical tensions impacting investor sentiment.

feed.title: Board exits and new committee chair at BP (NYSE: BP) for 2026 AGM
feed.summary: BP p.l.c. has announced significant board changes slated for its 2026 Annual General Meeting on April 23, 2026. Non-executive directors Melody Meyer (after nine years), Karen Richardson, and Simon Henry will retire, while Satish Pai will assume the role of chair of the Safety and Sustainability committee. These changes were detailed in a Form 6-K SEC filing, providing transparency to investors about future leadership.

feed.title: LATAM Airlines Group Files 2025 Annual Report with SEC, Highlighting their Growth and Financial Performance in the Aviation Future of South America

feed.title: U.S.-Iran war exposes big market concentration risk. It isn't in S&P 500 stocks
feed.summary: The U.S.-Iran conflict has highlighted a significant concentration risk in emerging markets, particularly within Asia, where ETFs are heavily weighted towards tech-dependent economies like China and South Korea. This dependence on energy-intensive tech manufacturing has led to extreme volatility in these markets due to rising oil prices. While not a reason to abandon emerging markets, investors are advised to adopt a "barbell approach," balancing Asian exposure with investments in Latin America, which could benefit from commodity links and political reforms.

The gift was motivated by Farley's deep Catholic faith and a desire to connect the Pope with Ford's Chicago roots.

feed.title: INVESTOR NOTICE: Enphase Energy, Inc. Investors with Substantial Losses Have Opportunity to Lead the Class Action Lawsuit – Company Announcement - FT.com

feed.title: Tesla Cybertruck crashed after Autopilot was engaged, suit alleges
feed.summary: A lawsuit has been filed against Tesla Inc. alleging that a Cybertruck operating on Autopilot crashed into a concrete barrier on a Houston overpass, causing serious injuries to the driver. The lawsuit claims Tesla misrepresented Autopilot's capabilities and failed to incorporate crucial safety features like lidar. It also criticizes Elon Musk's decision to rely solely on cameras for the self-driving system, which the suit alleges made the accident inevitable.

feed.title: BP PLC Pulls Foreign Staff From Iraq’s Rumaila Field After Drones, Raising Fresh Supply Fears
feed.summary: BP PLC has evacuated foreign staff from Iraq's Rumaila oilfield following a drone incident, intensifying concerns over global oil supply. This event occurs amid ongoing Middle East conflict, which has already led to significant production cuts in Iraq and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The security scare is particularly impactful for BP as it adjusts its strategy and a new CEO prepares to take over, contributing to a surge in crude oil prices.

feed.title: Three Factors Shortening Commodity Cycles, Study
feed.summary: A McKinsey study reveals that traditional long commodity supercycles are giving way to shorter, more volatile cycles due to three key factors: geopolitics, non-linear energy transition, and AI acceleration. This shift is reshaping trading strategies and investment priorities within the industry. The study highlights how national strategies, the complexities of decarbonization, and rapid technological advancements like AI are increasing market volatility and accelerating deal timelines.

The collaboration highlights a growing interest from major technology companies and utilities in advanced battery solutions.

feed.title: Baker Hughes Weighs US$10b Bond Sale And Nuclear Data Center Push

These strategic moves highlight the company's commitment to expanding into industrial gas and energy infrastructure while also focusing on power solutions for the energy transition and AI data centers. Investors should consider the potential increase in leverage from the bond sale and the execution risks associated with integrating Chart and ramping up complex nuclear and gas-powered data center projects.

feed.title: Ameren Illinois Launches Goalby Substation To Improve Power Reliability In Belleville
feed.summary: Ameren Illinois has activated a new gas-insulated transmission substation, named Goalby Substation, near downtown Belleville to enhance power reliability. This advanced facility, the first of its kind in Ameren's transmission system, uses a compact design that significantly reduces the needed land, making it suitable for a site above historic mine shafts. The project is expected to strengthen the local grid, speed up power restoration, and support future economic growth in Belleville.

feed.title: First Solar (FSLR): The AI-Driven Evolution of a Renewable Giant
feed.summary: First Solar (FSLR) has transformed from a domestic manufacturing policy play into a critical infrastructure provider for the AI revolution, marked by a significant stock surge in mid-2024. The company, the largest solar manufacturer in the Western Hemisphere, leverages its unique Cadmium Telluride thin-film technology and vertically integrated business model, benefiting from robust demand driven by AI data centers and U.S. domestic content incentives. Despite its strengths, FSLR faces risks from policy dependency and grid interconnection delays, but its expansion into India and innovation in Perovskite tandem cells present significant growth opportunities.

Analysts have revised down earnings estimates for Q1, but the consensus remains constructive with an average 1-year price target of $137.98.

feed.title: 4 High Yield Refiners Built for Exactly These Spiking Oil Prices and Geopolitical Swings
feed.summary: This article identifies four U.S. refiners well-positioned to benefit from spiking oil prices due to geopolitical events, specifically the recent leadership crisis in Iran. Marathon Petroleum (MPC), PBF Energy (PBF), Valero Energy (VLO), and Phillips 66 (PSX) are analyzed based on their EPS performance, refining margins, throughput, dividend strength, and balance sheet resilience, with Marathon Petroleum emerging as the top performer. The article explains that higher crude costs, when refined product prices lag, can significantly expand crack spreads and refiner margins.

feed.title: NextEra Energy’s quiet move that could reshape your power bill
feed.summary: NextEra Energy is silently transforming the US power grid by focusing on clean, reliable, and affordable electricity through its vast portfolio of renewable-focused energy services. The company, through Florida Power & Light and NextEra Energy Resources, is building large-scale solar, wind, and battery storage projects, which will impact consumers' electricity bills, EV charging, and grid stability. While this makes NextEra a key player in the US clean energy transition, its success is tied to interest rates, regulatory decisions, and climate risks.

feed.title: Midstream Dividends From EPD, Williams, and ONEOK Are Built to Survive Oil Price Chaos
feed.summary: This article highlights how midstream energy companies like Enterprise Products Partners (EPD), Williams Companies (WMB), and ONEOK (OKE) are better positioned to maintain stable dividends amidst oil price fluctuations due to their fee-based models. In contrast, Chevron (CVX), as an integrated oil major, has earnings more directly tied to commodity prices, making its dividend more vulnerable in a low-price environment. The piece details the financial strength and dividend growth of the midstream companies, emphasizing their infrastructure-driven revenue stability.

feed.title: Natural Resource Partners L.P. (NRP) Achieves Robust Free Cash Flow Growth Despite Weak Coal Prices
feed.summary: Natural Resource Partners L.P. (NRP) reported robust free cash flow of $169 million in Q4 2025 and retired $109 million in debt, despite weak metallurgical and thermal coal prices. The company's net income was $30.99 million for the fourth quarter and $136.37 million for the full year 2025. NRP also declared a special cash distribution of $0.12, reflecting its continued ability to generate cash flow amidst challenging market conditions.

feed.title: Fusion Fuel Green PLC Announces Approximately $1.16 Million in New LPG Engineering Subcontracts Across Two Residential Development Projects in Dubai, United Arab Emirates
feed.summary: Fusion Fuel Green PLC announced that its indirectly held company, Al Shola Al Modea Gas Distribution LLC, has secured two new engineering subcontracts worth approximately $1.16 million for residential developments in Dubai, UAE. These subcontracts involve the design, supply, installation, testing, and commissioning of centralized LPG systems. Following the completion of one of the larger developments, Al Shola Gas is also expected to be awarded an LPG utility operations subcontract for about 2,900 apartments, and has acquired new delivery vehicles to support growth and expand service capabilities.

feed.title: Brookfield Renewable Partners L.P. (NYSE:BEP) Receives Consensus Rating of "Moderate Buy" from Analysts
feed.summary: Brookfield Renewable Partners L.P. (NYSE:BEP) has received a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with eleven out of fifteen firms recommending a buy and a 12-month average price target of $34.93. Brookfield Renewable also reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates with $0.54 EPS and $1.54 billion in revenue.

feed.title: Electricity and gas industries were hit the hardest amid a week of sharp declines in the domestic st..
feed.summary: The electricity and gas industries in Korea experienced sharp declines in stock performance, primarily due to rising international energy prices exacerbated by geopolitical tensions involving the U.S., Israel, and Iran. This has led to concerns about the profitability of companies like Korea Electric Power Corp. and Korea Gas Corporation, which are heavily reliant on imported energy. Analysts predict that sustained high oil and LNG prices could further impact their financial outlook, especially given the lack of alternatives for LNG supply through the Strait of Hormuz.

feed.title: DT Midstream (DTM) Reports $1.138B FY2025 Adjusted EBITDA and 17% YoY Growth
feed.summary: DT Midstream (DTM) reported strong financial results for FY2025, with adjusted EBITDA reaching $1.138 billion, representing a 17% year-over-year increase, primarily driven by its Pipeline segment. The company also expanded its five-year organic project backlog by 50% to $3.4 billion and raised its quarterly dividend. Management provided an adjusted EBITDA guidance range of $1.155 to $1.225 billion for 2026, highlighting significant growth initiatives and strategic investments in response to natural gas demand.

feed.title: Should Danaos’ Broad New Shelf Registration Shape How DAC Investors View Its Liquidity Story?
feed.summary: Danaos Corporation recently filed a broad shelf registration for various securities, which enhances its financial flexibility. This move reinforces the company's existing strategy of prioritizing high liquidity and a conservative balance sheet in the cyclical shipping industry, although it doesn't significantly alter the near-term investment narrative dominated by its substantial cash position and contracted cash flows. Investors should be aware that future charter renewals could occur in a weaker rate environment, posing a key risk to current earnings power.

feed.title: Borr Drilling Fleet Deal Expands Backlog But Raises Leverage Questions
feed.summary: Borr Drilling has acquired five jack-up rigs for US$360 million and secured 24 new contract commitments totaling US$649 million in backlog, significantly expanding its operational capacity and work pipeline. While this boosts the company's scale and revenue visibility, the financing structure, involving high-coupon debt and equity raise, raises concerns about increased leverage and interest costs. Investors will need to monitor how effectively the expanded fleet generates cash flow to service this debt amidst lower profit margins and increased share count.

feed.title: MPLX Weighs Corporate Shift While Balancing Growth Projects And Buybacks
feed.summary: MPLX is considering a significant corporate shift from a master limited partnership (MLP) to a traditional corporation, a move that would impact its tax treatment, income distribution, and investor base. This structural review is happening concurrently with continued investment in growth projects and unit buybacks, highlighting the company's strategy to balance capital returns with long-term asset growth. Investors are encouraged to monitor how management reconciles debt levels, dividend policies, and growth spending amid this potential transition, especially given current debt levels and dividend coverage concerns.

feed.title: Mesa Royalty Trust (MTR, US5906351052): What energy income investors should know now
feed.summary: This article analyzes Mesa Royalty Trust (MTR), a niche U.S. oil and gas royalty vehicle, for international income investors. It highlights MTR's variable distributions tied to commodity prices and production, emphasizing its transparent but unforgiving link to macro energy trends and its finite lifespan due to depleting reserves. The analysis covers the trust's structure, SEC filing considerations, distribution profile, and risks such as depletion and regulatory shifts, contrasting it with more diversified energy investments.

feed.title: Energy Stocks Are Flashing A Signal Markets Haven't Seen Since The 2022 Oil Crisis
feed.summary: Energy stocks are significantly outperforming the broader market, a trend not seen since the 2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine, following a surge in oil prices due to conflict near the Strait of Hormuz. This divergence highlights a split between energy producers, which are benefiting, and energy consumers and other sectors, which are experiencing declines. The article emphasizes that investors' exposure to energy, whether as a producer or consumer, is a critical variable in their portfolio performance during this period.

feed.title: SM ENERGY PRICES AN UPSIZED PRIVATE OFFERING OF $1.0 BILLION OF SENIOR NOTES DUE 2034

feed.title: Is BKV Corporation (BKV) One of the Best Up and Coming Energy Stocks to Buy?
feed.summary: BKV Corporation reported strong 2025 earnings, its first full year as a public company, with net income reaching $173.1 million. The company expanded its operations with increased production, a larger stake in its Power JV, and significant progress in its Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Sequestration business. While BKV shows potential as an energy stock, the author suggests that certain AI stocks offer greater upside and less risk.

feed.title: Range Resources Contract And Dividend Shift Cash Flow Outlook
feed.summary: Range Resources (NYSE: RRC) has secured a new 10-year natural gas supply agreement for a Midwest power plant at premium pricing and increased its quarterly cash dividend by 11%. These moves reflect the company's strong cash flow generation, highlighted by US$658.02 million in net income and over US$650 million in free cash flow in 2025. The long-term contract and increased dividend suggest management's confidence in sustainable cash returns, aligning with narratives of growing natural gas demand from LNG exports and data centers.

feed.title: Atlas Energy Solutions (AESI) Reports $1.1B FY2025 Revenue and $221.7M Adjusted EBITDA
feed.summary: Atlas Energy Solutions (AESI) reported $1.1 billion in revenue for FY2025, a 3.7% increase year-over-year, alongside a net loss of $50.3 million and adjusted EBITDA of $221.7 million. The company is expanding its market share and diversifying into power generation with plans for 500 MW capacity by 2027. For Q1 2026, AESI anticipates flat EBITDA due to lower sand pricing and severe weather impacts, offset by volume gains and new power contributions.

feed.title: Atlas Energy Solutions (AESI) Reports $1.1B FY2025 Revenue and $221.7M Adjusted EBITDA

feed.title: Ford to recall 1.74 million vehicles in US over rearview camera issue, NHTSA says
feed.summary: Ford is recalling 1.74 million vehicles in the U.S. due to a rearview camera defect that can prevent images from displaying, affecting driver visibility. The recall targets certain Ford Bronco and Edge models where the APIM may overheat, and other models like the Escape and Lincoln Corsair where the image may flip or invert. The U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) announced the recall, citing safety concerns.

feed.title: Nuvve Holding (NVVE) Signs $5M European Energy Storage Agreement with Capture Energy AB
feed.summary: Nuvve Holding Corp. (NASDAQ: NVVE) subsidiary Nuvve Denmark ApS has signed a $5 million framework agreement with Capture Energy AB to deploy Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) across Europe. This initial phase involves three projects in Denmark set to be operational by summer 2026, with Capture Energy providing turnkey solutions and a decade of maintenance. The partnership aims to streamline virtual power plant scaling using Capture Energy's modular PowerBox LC units, with negotiations underway for three additional 10MW projects.

feed.title: Nuvve Holding (NVVE) Signs $5M European Energy Storage Agreement with Capture Energy AB
feed.summary: Nuvve Holding Corp. (NVVE) subsidiary, Nuvve Denmark ApS, has signed a framework agreement with Capture Energy AB to deploy Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) across Europe, starting with an initial $5 million contract. This agreement includes Capture Energy providing turnkey solutions for three projects in Denmark, which are expected to be operational by summer 2026, and they are currently negotiating an additional three 10MW projects. Nuvve's V2G technology platform utilizes EV batteries to provide grid services and aims to standardize virtual power plant scaling.

feed.title: Ecopetrol Q4 Earnings Call Highlights
feed.summary: Ecopetrol met its 2025 operational and financial goals despite a 15% drop in crude prices, achieving its second-highest net profit in history, with a 121% reserves replacement ratio. The company reported a 2025 EBITDA of COP 46.7 trillion and net income of COP 9 trillion, proposing a dividend of COP 110/share. For 2026, Ecopetrol plans investments of $5.4–6.7 billion, targeting production of 730–740k boe/d and aiming for a breakeven near $47–60 per barrel.

feed.title: Himalaya Shipping Ltd. (HSHP) – Commercial Update and Key Information Relating to the Cash Distribution for February 2026

feed.title: 777X

feed.title: Middle East Conflict Causing Financial Hit' To Battered Airline Industry, Says Boeing Chief

He emphasized that this geopolitical situation is a "tragedy" with economic consequences for the interconnected aviation sector. Nelson also addressed the investigation into the 2025 Air India Flight 171 crash, expressing full confidence in the 787 aircraft and highlighting Boeing's internal transformation with a focus on safety and quality.

feed.title: Record 2025 output and profit surge at Petrobras (NYSE: PBR)
feed.summary: Petrobras (NYSE: PBR) reported a significant surge in net income in 2025, reaching US$19.4 billion, a 161% increase from the previous year, despite a 14% drop in Brent crude prices. This growth was driven by a record 11% increase in commercial oil and gas production in Brazil, reaching 2,990 Mboed, and substantial CAPEX investment of US$20.3 billion, predominantly in Exploration & Production. The company also achieved its highest proven reserves in a decade, a fully organic reserve replacement ratio of 175%, and distributed R$45.2 billion in dividends.

feed.title: Tight Truck Capacity Opens Window for US Railroads to Win Back Freight
feed.summary: U.S. railroads like CSX, Union Pacific, and BNSF are poised to regain freight from trucking companies due to shrinking trucking capacity and rising road-haul rates. This shift is driven by small carriers exiting the market and increased federal scrutiny on driver requirements, making rail more competitive, especially for intermodal freight. Analysts predict this trend could lead to significant growth for railroads, with potential beneficiaries including eastern railroads with high intermodal exposure.

feed.title: Corporation Commission rejects OG&E's charge plan for power plants
feed.summary: The Oklahoma Corporation Commission rejected OG&E's request to charge customers for new power plants before they are operational, blocking the use of a new state law (Senate Bill 998) that allows such cost recovery. This 2-1 decision sets the stage for a legal battle at the state's highest court, as OG&E plans to appeal immediately, arguing the project meets state law requirements. Commissioners disagreed on the interpretation of the new law, which authorizes Construction Work in Progress (CWIP) for power generation, with opponents warning it forces ratepayers to act as "involuntary investors."

feed.title: Did Genco’s New Newcastlemax Delivery Just Shift Genco Shipping & Trading's (GNK) Investment Narrative?
feed.summary: Genco Shipping & Trading (GNK) recently took delivery of a new scrubber-fitted Newcastlemax vessel, the Genco Stars and Stripes, which has been deployed into the drybulk spot market. This delivery, with a second similar vessel expected by month-end, increases Genco's large-vessel capacity at a time of strong drybulk freight conditions and an ongoing takeover dispute with Diana Shipping. The article examines how this new asset could influence Genco’s investment narrative, particularly concerning its spot market exposure, dividend sustainability, and risk profile.

feed.title: JinkoSolar Powers Tanzania’s First Solar PV Project to Full Grid Connection

The collaboration with PowerChina ensured timely equipment supply and technical support, setting a new benchmark for JinkoSolar in the African market.

feed.title: Kosmos Energy (NYSE:KOS) - Stock Analysis
feed.summary: This Simply Wall St analysis of Kosmos Energy (NYSE:KOS) highlights its current stock performance, market valuation, and future prospects. The company, a deepwater exploration and production firm, is trading below its estimated fair value but faces risks due to its highly volatile share price, unprofitability, and significant insider selling. Analysts present varied outlooks, with some forecasting growth due to increased LNG and oil production, while others warn of vulnerability to renewable energy transition and geopolitical risks.

feed.title: KLX Energy Services Announces 2025 Full Year and Fourth Quarter Earnings Release and Conference Call Schedule
feed.summary: KLX Energy Services Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: KLXE) announced its schedule for releasing its 2025 full year and fourth quarter financial results. The company will host a live conference call and webcast on Thursday, March 12, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. Eastern Time. A replay of the call and an archive of the webcast will be available for a limited time for those unable to attend the live event.

feed.title: NRES Stock Fund Price and Chart — NASDAQ:NRES
feed.summary: The NRES Xtrackers RREEF Global Natural Resources ETF is the first actively managed ETF by DWS, launched on February 27, 2024. It focuses on the global natural resources sector, including agriculture, energy, and metals, aiming for total return through a top-down and bottom-up research approach. With an AUM of $38.52 million and a 0.45% expense ratio, NRES currently shows strong buy signals based on technical analysis and has seen significant price and NAV increases since its inception.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Howard Mayson Sells 5,000 Shares of Ovintiv Inc (O
feed.summary: Howard Mayson, a Director at Ovintiv Inc (OVV), sold 5,000 shares of the company on March 3, 2026, bringing his total ownership to 45,780 shares. This transaction is part of a pattern of insider selling at Ovintiv, with five insider sells and no buys over the past year. Ovintiv's stock is currently trading at $52.39, which GuruFocus assesses as "significantly overvalued" compared to its intrinsic GF Value of $37.03.

feed.title: TAPinto Announces Johnson & Johnson, PSE&G as New Signature Marketing Partners
feed.summary: TAPinto has announced that Johnson & Johnson and PSE&G have joined as new signature marketing partners. This partnership will support TAPinto's local news coverage and community initiatives across its network. The collaboration aims to strengthen community ties and provide valuable resources through sponsored content and localized outreach efforts.

feed.title: SGF FANG Holdings, LP Sells 1,000,000 Shares of Diamondback Ener
feed.summary: SGF FANG Holdings, LP, a 10% owner of Diamondback Energy Inc (FANG), sold 1,000,000 shares of the company on March 4, 2026. Following this sale, the insider now holds 96,686,727 shares. The stock is currently trading at $176.71, and GuruFocus indicates it is modestly overvalued with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 1.12.

feed.title: Is Enphase Energy, Inc. (ENPH) One of the Best Solar Stocks to Buy Now?
feed.summary: Jefferies recently upgraded Enphase Energy, Inc. (ENPH) to a Buy with an increased price target, citing improved demand visibility, product launches, and expected margin expansion. The solar technology company is launching its fifth-generation storage product in late 2026 and has partnered with Capital Good Fund for solar projects in Georgia and Pennsylvania, utilizing its microinverters. While acknowledging ENPH's potential, the article suggests specific AI stocks might offer greater upside with less risk.

feed.title: Is Enphase Energy, Inc. (ENPH) One of the Best Solar Stocks to Buy Now?
feed.summary: Jefferies analysts upgraded Enphase Energy, Inc. (ENPH) to a Buy rating with a raised price target of $57, citing improved demand visibility, new product launches, and margin expansion. The solar technology company is poised for growth with its fifth-generation product launch in late 2026 and expansion into commercial and industrial applications. Enphase has also partnered with Capital Good Fund to deploy solar projects in Georgia and Pennsylvania, utilizing its microinverter systems for long-term reliability.

feed.title: Scotiabank Raises Dominion Energy (D) Price Target Following Solid Execution
feed.summary: Scotiabank has increased its price target for Dominion Energy (D) from $63 to $67, maintaining a Sector Perform rating, citing strong execution and delivered management commitments. Dominion Energy recently projected lower annual profit but boosted its five-year capital spending plan by nearly 30% to meet growing electricity demand, particularly from data centers for AI and cryptocurrency. The company, which serves major tech companies like Alphabet and Amazon, expects to invest $64.7 billion between 2026 and 2030, a significant increase from its previous plan.

feed.title: Caterpillar Built A Real Pickup Truck After AI Faked One First
feed.summary: Inspired by AI-generated images, Caterpillar has developed a one-off pickup truck based on a Ford Super Duty. Unveiled at an industry event, the "Cat Truck" features custom yellow-and-black styling, off-road accessories, and Caterpillar's VisionLink infotainment system. While the company has no plans for mass production, this custom build showcases Caterpillar's design vision in the automotive space.

feed.title: Southern Company supports president's ratepayer protection pledge
feed.summary: Southern Company, the parent company of Alabama Power, has publicly announced its strong support for the president's ratepayer protection pledge. This pledge focuses on ensuring that advancements in AI and technology benefit customers and communities broadly. The company emphasized its commitment to partnering with large customers who pay their fair share to support continued investment, manage costs for existing customers, and broadly distribute the benefits of economic growth.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Group Proposes Ship Repair Yard for Panama's Pacific Coast
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group has proposed building a ship repair yard with a 130,000-ton floating dry dock on Panama's Pacific coast, near Puerto Armuelles. This facility, capable of accommodating large cruise and container ships, aims to address the current lack of suitable repair options in the region, which often forces vessels to travel to Asia for maintenance. The Panamanian president, Jose Raul Mulino, has expressed support for the project, which is expected to create 500 to 800 jobs and enhance Panama's role as a maritime hub.

feed.title: Kuntal Kumar Verma Sells 728 Shares of First Solar (NASDAQ:FSLR) Stock
feed.summary: Kuntal Kumar Verma, an insider at First Solar (NASDAQ:FSLR), sold 728 shares of the company's stock on March 4th at an average price of $196.95, reducing his ownership by 8.74%. This follows a larger sale of 3,426 shares on March 3rd, part of a pattern of concentrated insider sales by top executives. The stock has seen downward pressure linked to missed Q4 earnings, weak 2026 guidance, analyst price target cuts, and an investor investigation by Pomerantz LLP.

feed.title: New $69 JetBlue flights link NYC and Boston to Florida’s Emerald Coast
feed.summary: JetBlue has launched new nonstop service to Destin–Fort Walton Beach (VPS) from New York's JFK and Boston Logan International Airports, marking its 11th Florida destination. To celebrate, the airline is offering introductory one-way fares starting at $69, aiming to connect the Northeast with Florida's Emerald Coast and enhance travel options for both leisure visitors and local residents. This expansion builds on JetBlue's continued growth in Florida, providing award-winning service including free Fly-Fi, seatback entertainment, and complimentary snacks.

feed.title: TVA names new board chair and successor, both Tennesseans
feed.summary: The Tennessee Valley Authority has appointed Mitch Graves of Memphis as its new board chair, serving until April 2027, with Jeff Hagood, a Knoxville attorney, succeeding him for a two-year term. Both appointees by President Donald Trump are Tennesseans and emphasize reliability, affordability, and job creation in the Valley. Their appointments resolve a previous board gridlock and align with the utility's recommitment to coal and natural gas generation.

feed.title: Stellantis Pro One, Stellantis’ Commercial Vehicles Business Unit, launches in Europe a commercial campaign offering BEVs at diesel price
feed.summary: Stellantis Pro One is launching a commercial campaign in Europe offering battery-electric versions of its compact and mid-size vans at the same price as their diesel equivalents. This initiative aims to remove the purchase price barrier for EV adoption among professional customers, ensuring access to sustainable mobility without compromising operational capabilities. The offer is valid until the end of June 2026 and covers a wide range of popular van models from Citroën, Fiat Professional, Opel, and Peugeot.

feed.title: Energy Recovery (NASDAQ:ERII) SVP Sells $218,226.48 in Stock
feed.summary: Energy Recovery's SVP Rodney Clemente sold 20,568 shares for $218,226.48 on March 4, reducing his holdings by over 15%. This sale occurred as the company missed Q4 earnings and revenue expectations and provided a FY2026 EPS guidance of $0.50-$0.70. Shares of ERII fell 1.1% on heavy volume following the news, trading below key moving averages, though analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with a $15.00 average price target.

feed.title: Venture Global (VG) Soars 10% Ahead of Dividend; Wins Battle vs Shell
feed.summary: Venture Global Inc. (NYSE:VG) saw its shares jump over 10% and announced a cash dividend ahead of a payout later this month. The company also secured a legal victory against Shell regarding LNG sales and reported strong financial results for the past year, with net income growing by 53.7% and revenues by 177%. Goldman Sachs issued a "buy" recommendation following these developments.

feed.title: Natural Resource Partners L.P. 2025 Tax Information
feed.summary: Natural Resource Partners L.P. announced that its 2025 tax packages for unitholders, including individual K-1 tax information, are now available online and will be mailed starting March 9, 2026. Unitholders needing assistance can access their information on the company's website or call a toll-free support line. The company, a diversified natural resource master limited partnership, holds a portfolio of properties in the United States and an equity investment in Sisecam Wyoming LLC.

feed.title: American Airlines' 'Premium Push' Now Sees it Serving Boxed Wine On Flights From its Biggest International Premium Destination
feed.summary: American Airlines is reportedly serving boxed wine on flights from London Heathrow, its largest international premium destination, due to an ongoing catering meltdown. This comes despite the airline's "premium push" and has led to speculation about hygiene issues with its third-party catering supplier. The carrier has resorted to "double catering" flights from the US and has temporarily replaced its usual Champagne with Cava to save space and manage the catering challenges.

feed.title: FuelCell Energy, Advanced Drainage, Northwest Pipe, Bel Fuse, and JELD-WEN Shares Plummet, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of FuelCell Energy, Advanced Drainage, Northwest Pipe, Bel Fuse, and JELD-WEN plummeted following exacerbated geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which drove oil prices higher and reignited inflation concerns. The Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped significantly due to fear of disrupted global energy markets and potential delays in Federal Reserve interest rate cuts. While the market saw a broad-based sell-off across multiple sectors, these five companies experienced notable declines in share price.

feed.title: Capital Clean Energy Carriers (CCEC) Margin Strength Challenges Cautious Narratives In FY 2025 Earnings
feed.summary: Capital Clean Energy Carriers (CCEC) recorded significant earnings growth of 115.6% year-over-year in FY 2025, with net profit margins rising to 28.3%. While analysts forecast continued growth, a premium valuation compared to DCF fair value and peer multiples, alongside interest coverage risks due to floating rate debt for newbuilds, present cautious narratives. The company's strong margins challenge consensus views, but potential investors are advised to weigh these against valuation and debt concerns.

feed.title: Citigroup Inc. Boosts Stake in JetBlue Airways Corporation $JBLU
feed.summary: Citigroup Inc. has significantly increased its stake in JetBlue Airways Corporation (NASDAQ:JBLU) by 91.0%, acquiring an additional 387,543 shares. This brings their total ownership to 813,375 shares, valued at approximately $4.00 million.
