Other institutional investors like Par Capital Management Inc. and Ameriprise Financial Inc. have also boosted their positions, indicating strong institutional interest despite JetBlue reporting a quarterly loss and having a "Reduce" average analyst rating.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Ovintiv (NYSE:OVV) Director Sells 5,000 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Ovintiv (NYSE:OVV) Director Howard John Mayson sold 5,000 shares of the company's stock on March 3rd, reducing his stake by 9.85%. The sale was valued at $261,950, with shares trading at an average price of $52.39. This transaction followed a strong quarterly earnings report where Ovintiv beat analyst estimates and announced a quarterly dividend.

feed.title: U.S. NRC Dockets Terra Innovatum’s SOLO™ Micro-Modular Reactor Topical Reports, Initiating the Formal Review and Approval Process
feed.summary: Terra Innovatum Global N.V. announced that the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) has formally docketed several key topical reports for its SOLO™ Micro-Modular Reactor, marking a significant regulatory milestone. This action initiates the NRC's detailed technical review, moving the company closer to obtaining approval for construction and operation. The company aims for commercial deployment of its carbon-free power solution by 2028, targeting energy-intensive infrastructure and other critical applications.

feed.title: Did PPL’s (PPL) US$1 Billion Composite Units Deal and Dividend Hike Just Redefine Its Equity Story?
feed.summary: PPL Corporation recently completed a US$1 billion composite corporate units offering and increased its quarterly dividend by 4.6% to US$0.2850 per share. The company also reported higher Q4 and full-year 2025 sales and issued 2026 earnings guidance above 2025 results. This financing supports PPL's capital plan, but the main investment narrative still hinges on timely regulatory approval for new investments, with a potential fair value upside of 7%.

feed.title: Wabtec (WAB): Should You Buy, Sell, or Hold After Q4 Results?
feed.summary: Wabtec (WAB) has shown impressive financial performance, delivering a 249% return since March 2021 and outperforming the S&P 500. The company boasts expanding operating margins and strong long-term EPS growth, though it faces a challenge with sluggish organic revenue growth. The article examines whether, despite these points, Wabtec remains a good investment at its current valuation.

feed.title: Sempra Energy's Legal Probe and 1.52% Slide Fuel $370M Trading Surge Ranking 384th in Market Activity
feed.summary: Sempra Energy (SRE) experienced a 1.52% decline and a $370 million trading surge, ranking 384th in market activity, amid a securities fraud investigation initiated by The Portnoy Law Firm. This probe follows a disappointing January 2025 earnings report, which missed revenue estimates and led to a 19% stock plunge in February 2025. The legal and operational challenges, including lower consumption due to mild weather and increased expenses, point to prolonged uncertainty for the company.

feed.title: Assessing SLB (NYSE:SLB) Valuation After Oman Contracts And Q4 Earnings Beat
feed.summary: SLB (NYSE:SLB) is gaining attention following two new five-year contracts with Petroleum Development Oman and a strong Q4 2025 earnings beat, leading to significant stock outperformance. Despite recent weaker daily and weekly moves, SLB's stock is up 26% over 90 days and analysts consider it undervalued with a fair value around $55, primarily due to expected revenue expansion, margin improvement, and synergies from the ChampionX integration. However, the valuation depends on continued global upstream spending and successful integration.

feed.title: Does Earnings Beat And EV Fleet Expansion Change The Bull Case For Republic Services (RSG)?
feed.summary: Republic Services (RSG) recently reported better-than-expected earnings with improved adjusted profit margins and expanded its electric vehicle fleet by over 150 vehicles, aiming to reduce emissions and operating costs. This performance could strengthen its investment narrative, especially given ongoing softness in construction and manufacturing volumes. The company's future projections include significant revenue and earnings growth, suggesting a potential upside to its current stock price.

feed.title: PSEG Ranked No. 1 Utility in 100 Best Corporate Citizens of 2022
feed.summary: PSEG has been named the No. 1 utility and eighth overall in 3BL Media's 100 Best Corporate Citizens of 2022 list, recognizing its strong performance and transparency in environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors. The company is committed to achieving net-zero carbon emissions by 2030 and has launched programs focused on clean energy and community impact. This recognition adds to PSEG's numerous awards for ethics, climate leadership, and commitment to its employees and communities.

feed.title: Kinder Morgan V.P. Schlosser sells $207k in stock
feed.summary: John W. Schlosser, V.P. (President, Terminals) at Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI), sold 6,166 shares of Class P Common Stock on March 5, 2026, for approximately $207,640. This sale occurred as the stock neared its 52-week high, following strong recent performance. The article also mentions positive analyst ratings and financial results for Kinder Morgan and ExxonMobil.

feed.title: TAPinto Announces Johnson & Johnson, PSE&G as New Signature Marketing Partners
feed.summary: TAPinto, a local news network, has announced Johnson & Johnson and PSE&G as its new Signature Marketing Partners. This collaboration aims to provide enhanced marketing opportunities for these corporations through TAPinto's extensive local news platform.

feed.title: FTC Solar, Inc. (FTCI) Inks Multi-Year Solar Tracker Supply Agreement in South Africa
feed.summary: FTC Solar Inc. has secured a three-year agreement with Lubanzi Inala, a South African procurement company, to supply solar tracker systems, software, and engineering services for multiple utility-scale PV projects totaling approximately 840 megawatts across South Africa. This deal follows FTC Solar's acquisition of a 55% stake in Alpha Steel, strengthening its capability to produce steel components for solar projects. The company aims to optimize energy production and reduce installation costs in the utility-scale solar market.

feed.title: First Solar (NASDAQ:FSLR) CTO Sells $716,898.00 in Stock
feed.summary: First Solar's CTO, Markus Gloeckler, sold 3,640 shares of the company's stock for $716,898.00 on March 4, reducing his direct ownership by 31.17%. This sale follows another recent transaction on March 3 and occurs amidst an investor probe, a Q4 earnings per share miss, weak 2026 guidance, and concentrated insider selling by other senior executives, contributing to negative sentiment and a decline in share price. Analysts have issued mixed ratings and price targets, with some maintaining a "buy" or "hold" while others downgraded the stock due to near-term pressures.

feed.title: Southern Company Stock Shocks Wall Street: Smart Buy or Slow Trap?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Southern Company (SO) stock, a major US utility holding company, to determine if it's a smart investment or a "boomer trap" for younger investors. It highlights the company's high dividend yield, its shift towards clean energy, and the regulatory/rate risks, positioning it as a defensive, income-oriented play rather than a growth stock. The article discusses why Gen Z and Millennials might consider utility stocks like SO for portfolio stability and dividends, despite their slow growth profile, and covers the pros and cons identified by experts.

feed.title: Goldman Says Oil Market Not Yet Confident in US Hormuz Measures
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs believes the oil market lacks confidence in US security measures to protect tankers in the Strait of Hormuz. Samantha Dart, co-head of global commodities research, cited concerns about the practicality of naval escorts for numerous tankers and their effectiveness against drone attacks. These doubts suggest ongoing market uncertainty despite the US efforts.

feed.title: Mirae Asset keeps Kepco neutral, urges tariff reform amid oil risk in South Korea - CHOSUNBIZ
feed.summary: Mirae Asset has maintained a neutral stance on Kepco, advising for tariff reform in South Korea. This recommendation comes amidst potential risks associated with oil prices.

feed.title: W&T Offshore declares $0.01 quarterly dividend for Q1 2026
feed.summary: W&T Offshore, Inc. (NYSE:WTI) has announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.01 per share for the first quarter of 2026, payable on March 26, 2026, to stockholders of record as of March 19, 2026. This marks the company's ninth consecutive quarterly dividend since its program began in November 2023. The independent oil and natural gas producer operates offshore in the Gulf of Mexico, boasting significant returns over the past year.

feed.title: TD Cowen lowers FTC Solar stock price target to $8 on guidance
feed.summary: TD Cowen significantly lowered its price target for FTC Solar Inc. (FTCI) from $12.50 to $8.00 while maintaining a Buy rating, citing a technical covenant default and weaker-than-expected Q1 2026 guidance. Despite record margins and approaching EBITDA breakeven, the company is burning through cash. The firm views the covenant issue as procedural, noting increasing master service agreements and backlog, and suggests the stock is currently undervalued.

feed.title: TVA names new board chair and successor, both Tennesseans
feed.summary: The Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) has announced new leadership for its board of directors, with Mitch Graves of Memphis becoming the new chair until April 2027, and Jeff Hagood of Knoxville designated as his successor. Both appointees by President Donald Trump are Tennesseans. This leadership change follows an period of gridlock on the board and signals a recommitment to coal and natural gas generation for the utility.

feed.title: Memphian Mitch Graves selected TVA board chair, will serve through 2027
feed.summary: Mitch Graves, CEO of West Cancer Center & Research Institute, has been appointed interim board chair of the Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) and will serve through April 2027. Graves was nominated to the TVA board by President Donald Trump in July and replaces Bill Renick, who resigned in February. Following Graves' term, Jeff Hagood will assume the role of chair for the federally owned utility company.

feed.title: James Small III Sells 20,000 Shares of International Seaways (NYSE:INSW) Stock
feed.summary: James Small III, CAO of International Seaways (NYSE:INSW), sold 20,000 shares for approximately $1.51 million, reducing his stake by 35.48%. This sale occurred after the company reported strong Q4 results, exceeding revenue and EPS estimates, and announced a quarterly dividend. Despite the insider selling and a stock price dip, analysts maintain a "Buy" consensus with some recent upgrades to price targets and ratings.

feed.title: Gogo Inc. Earnings Call Highlights Growth Amid Strain
feed.summary: Gogo Inc.'s recent earnings call highlighted growth in next-generation products, record equipment activity, and strong free cash flow, despite challenges such as declining legacy ATG metrics, negative equipment margins, and litigation costs. The company reported significant increases in service revenue and anticipates continued growth in its Galileo and 5G platforms, while actively managing its debt and transitioning away from older ATG systems.

feed.title: Horizon Aircraft's Unique VTOL Delivers Up to 75% Lower Operating Costs Than Helicopters
feed.summary: Horizon Aircraft announced that its Cavorite X7 hybrid-electric VTOL aircraft is projected to operate with up to 75% lower costs per available seat mile compared to traditional helicopters. This is due to its hybrid architecture and patented fan-in-wing design, allowing for longer range and faster speeds. The company aims to highlight the aircraft's economic performance and operational benefits, including all-weather capability, low maintenance, and high utilization, for various critical missions.

feed.title: Horizon Aircraft's Unique VTOL Delivers Up to 75% Lower Operating Costs Than Helicopters
feed.summary: Horizon Aircraft announced that its Cavorite X7 hybrid-electric VTOL aircraft is projected to operate up to 75% more cost-efficiently than traditional helicopters, validated by an independent audit. The aircraft offers enhanced redundancy, speed, and utilization through its hybrid architecture and fan-in-wing design, targeting regional distances up to 450 km/h. This efficiency and all-weather capability aim to revolutionize services in critical missions like medevac and disaster relief, promising significant operational cost reductions and higher fleet utilization for operators.

feed.title: Horizon Aircraft's Unique VTOL Delivers Up to 75% Lower Operating Costs Than Helicopters
feed.summary: Horizon Aircraft announced that its Cavorite X7 hybrid-electric VTOL aircraft is projected to have operational costs up to 75% lower than conventional helicopters on a cost-per-available-seat-mile basis, validated by an independent audit. The aircraft's design, which integrates a hybrid architecture and fan-in-wing technology, allows for higher speeds, greater redundancy, and increased utilization. These advancements aim to revolutionize regional air mobility for various applications like medevac, disaster relief, and defense, while also offering enhanced economic benefits to operators.

feed.title: Consumers Energy Warns 13 Michigan Dams Could Be Torn Down
feed.summary: Consumers Energy has offered to sell 13 hydroelectric dams in Michigan for $1 each, warning that if the state doesn't approve the sale, the company may demolish them. The dams are no longer profitable for Consumers Energy, costing nine times more than other power sources. State regulators are hesitant due to the buyer's history of reselling dams, but Consumers Energy stresses that the sale would save customers money and prevent financial devastation to communities built around the reservoirs.

feed.title: Hallador Energy Company Schedules Fourth Quarter & Full Year 2025 Conference Call for March 12, 2026 at 5:00 p.m. ET
feed.summary: Hallador Energy Company (Nasdaq: HNRG) has scheduled a conference call for Thursday, March 12, 2026, at 5:00 p.m. Eastern time to discuss its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025. The company will release its results in a press release prior to the call, which will also include a question-and-answer period. Interested parties can submit questions via email before the call and access the broadcast live or as a replay on the company's investor relations website.

feed.title: Navigating Crosscurrents: The Virtus Reaves Utilities ETF's Divergent Performance
feed.summary: The Virtus Reaves Utilities ETF (UTES) demonstrated strong annual performance in 2025, outperforming its benchmark with a 25.53% gain, but experienced a decline of 4.91% in Q4 2025. This divergence is attributed to the conflicting forces of rising electricity demand due to AI and data centers versus the impact of high interest rates and cooled investor sentiment towards AI-beneficiary stocks. The article highlights the fund's active management strategy and the sector's long-term growth potential tied to power demand.

feed.title: DHT Holdings Extends Charter Coverage As Bartnes Joins Board Oversight
feed.summary: DHT Holdings has secured two new one-year time charter contracts for its VLCCs, DHT Redwood and DHT Opal, providing clearer near-term cash flow visibility. The company also appointed Erik Bartnes, an experienced maritime executive, to its Board of Directors to bring additional boardroom experience. These strategic moves aim to enhance DHT's revenue visibility, operational stability, and board-level oversight while balancing short-term market exposure with long-term stability.

feed.title: According to the latest filing disclosed by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), some existing shareholders of electric aircraft manufacturer Archer Aviation Inc. plan to sell up to 5.3 million shares of the company's Class A common stock.
feed.summary: Existing shareholders of Archer Aviation Inc. plan to sell up to 5.3 million shares of Class A common stock, as disclosed in a recent SEC filing. This sale, which is solely initiated by shareholders and not the company, aims to provide liquidity and allow early investors to realize returns. Analysts note that such secondary market reductions are typical for growth technology companies, urging investors to monitor changes in shareholder structure and potential impacts on corporate governance.

feed.title: SandRidge Energy (SD) Reports Strong Q4 Revenue and Production Growth
feed.summary: SandRidge Energy (SD) announced strong Q4 revenue of $39.4 million and a significant increase in oil production, reaching a multi-year peak. The company plans to continue its development activities in the Cherokee region, focusing on full-cycle returns and operational flexibility while maintaining a strong safety record. Despite a slight revenue decline over three years, SandRidge exhibits robust financial health with strong margins, no debt, and notable institutional ownership.

feed.title: NCS Multistage Holdings (NCSM) Q4 EPS Surge Tests Bearish Margin Narratives
feed.summary: NCS Multistage Holdings (NCSM) reported strong Q4 2025 results with a significant EPS surge, challenging bearish narratives about its compressed margins and slower revenue growth. While the trailing net profit margin decreased to 6.9% from 28% a year prior, Q4 net income of US$15.0 million indicates a positive shift. The company's low P/E ratio of 9.2x and a DCF fair value of US$117.50 suggest potential undervaluation despite profitability concerns.

feed.title: Global Ship Lease (GSL) Margin Strength Reinforces Bullish Valuation Narratives
feed.summary: Global Ship Lease (GSL) reported strong Q3 2025 results with revenue of US$189.3 million and a net profit margin of 53.1%, converting a significant portion of revenue into profit. Despite this, its latest earnings growth of 24.6% is below its five-year average, and bullish forecasts anticipate a revenue decline and margin easing by 2028. The company trades at a low P/E of 3.5x compared to its DCF fair value, but investors also grapple with an unstable dividend history amidst strong current profitability, highlighting a debate between growth potential and shareholder returns.

feed.title: Can Carnival (CCL) Turn Geopolitical Travel Disruptions Into a Stronger Onboard Spending Strategy?
feed.summary: Carnival Corporation (CCL) is facing challenges from higher fuel costs due to Middle East conflict and disrupted travel operations. Despite these geopolitical risks and security concerns, Carnival is implementing new guest initiatives and luxury promotions to boost onboard spending and differentiate its offerings. The company's strategy focuses on strengthening pricing power and onboard spend, particularly in its higher-end brands, to drive earnings and cash flow amidst short-term headwinds.

feed.title: Littelfuse Appoints Holly B. Paeper to Board of Directors
feed.summary: Littelfuse, Inc. (NASDAQ: LFUS) has announced the appointment of Holly B. Paeper to its board of directors, effective March 4, 2026. Ms. Paeper will also serve on the Technology Committee. She is currently the President, Commercial HVAC Americas, for Trane Technologies, bringing extensive leadership experience in various end markets, M&A, strategy, product management, and marketing from previous roles at Corning, Eaton, and Intel.

feed.title: Assessing SLB (NYSE:SLB) Valuation After Oman Contracts And Q4 Earnings Beat
feed.summary: SLB (formerly Schlumberger) is gaining investor attention after securing two five-year contracts with Petroleum Development Oman and exceeding Q4 2025 earnings expectations. The company's stock has seen significant gains, and analysts estimate it to be about 13% undervalued. Integration with ChampionX is expected to boost EBITDA margins and earnings per share, particularly from 2026 onwards.

feed.title: Why the VW-Qualcomm-Rivian Alliance is the New Blueprint for the Western Software-Defined Vehicle
feed.summary: Volkswagen, Qualcomm, and Rivian have formed an alliance to develop a new "software-defined vehicle" architecture for Western markets. This partnership aims to overcome the software challenges faced by traditional automakers by integrating Qualcomm’s Snapdragon Digital Chassis with Rivian’s advanced electrical architecture. The collaboration signifies a crucial shift in the automotive industry towards centralized, software-driven vehicle control, creating a formidable competitor to other SDV initiatives.

feed.title: Devon Energy (DVN) Price Target Raised by Piper Sandler to $59
feed.summary: Piper Sandler has increased its price target for Devon Energy (DVN) from $55 to $59, maintaining an Overweight rating. This adjustment follows several other recent analyst rating changes for DVN, with various firms raising or lowering price targets and maintaining or changing ratings. Devon Energy is a significant oil and gas producer in top US shale plays, with an average target price of $48.87 from 27 analysts and a consensus "Outperform" recommendation.

feed.title: Brazil's Petrobras to pay $1.5 billion in interest on equity to shareholders
feed.summary: Brazilian state-run oil firm Petrobras announced that its board of directors has approved the payment of 8.1 billion reais ($1.54 billion) in interest on equity to shareholders. This equates to approximately 0.63 real per share. The company stated that these payments will be distributed in two installments, one in May and the other in June.

feed.title: CSX Corporation launches safety briefing workshops to reinforce workplace vigilance
feed.summary: CSX Corporation has initiated Job Safety Briefing workshops to further strengthen its commitment to workplace safety for its employees. These workshops aim to make employees more aware of changing conditions before, during, and after shifts, promoting a comprehensive safety culture. This move aligns with the company's broader leadership and community engagement initiatives, emphasizing holistic employee well-being.

feed.title: Phillips 66 (PSX) Increases Despite Market Slip: Here's What You Need to Know
feed.summary: Phillips 66 (PSX) saw a 1.04% increase in its stock price, closing at $166.44, outperforming the S&P 500, Dow, and Nasdaq, which all experienced declines. Analysts anticipate significant year-over-year growth in earnings per share for the upcoming release, despite a projected decrease in quarterly revenue. The company currently holds a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold) and is trading at a Forward P/E ratio that indicates a discount relative to its industry average.

feed.title: Mortimer Buckley Buys 2,230 Shares of Boeing (NYSE:BA) Stock
feed.summary: Mortimer Buckley, a director at Boeing (NYSE:BA), recently purchased 2,230 shares of the company's stock for approximately $499,966, increasing his total holdings to 4,430 shares. This transaction, disclosed in an SEC filing, occurred on March 3rd at an average price of $224.20 per share. Boeing demonstrated strong quarterly earnings, reporting $9.92 EPS and $23.95 billion in revenue, surpassing analyst estimates, although the stock faces a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with various positive and negative sentiments impacting its outlook.

feed.title: W&T Offshore Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.01 a Share, Payable March 26 to Holders of Record March 19
feed.summary: W&T Offshore, Inc. (NYSE: WTI) has announced that it will maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.01 per share. The dividend is payable on March 26, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 19, 2026. This announcement confirms the company's consistent dividend policy for the first quarter of 2026.

feed.title: Goodyear Tire & Rubber Receives $10.83 Consensus Target Price
feed.summary: Goodyear Tire & Rubber (NASDAQ:GT) has a consensus "Hold" rating from nine analysts, with an average 12-month price target of $10.83. This target suggests a potential upside of approximately 15% from its current trading price. The ratings include a mix of Sell, Hold, Buy, and Strong Buy recommendations.

feed.title: Chrysler CEO Feuell leaves company as Dodge chief takes over post
feed.summary: Chris Feuell, CEO of Chrysler, has left Stellantis NV for personal reasons. Her role will be taken over by Matt McAlear, the current Dodge CEO, who will also oversee Alfa Romeo operations in North America. This change is part of several leadership shifts under CEO Antonio Filosa, as the company focuses on improving its North American brands.

feed.title: Net Income Up 31% for Heartland Express
feed.summary: Heartland Express Inc. reported a 14.6% increase in gross revenues to $104.5 million for the quarter and a 30.9% increase in net income to $14.5 million. This marks their 10th consecutive quarter of year-over-year revenue and net income growth. The company maintained a debt-free balance sheet and was recognized by Forbes as one of the "200 Best Small Companies in America" for the 12th time.

feed.title: Heartland Express Gross Revenues Up 16.1%
feed.summary: Heartland Express Inc. reported a 16.1% increase in gross revenues for the third quarter of 2005, reaching $136.2 million. Net income also rose by 2.8% to $17.5 million. Despite record high diesel prices, the company maintained a strong financial position, ending the quarter with $276.5 million in cash and remaining debt-free.

feed.title: Honda to import US-made SUV, luxury sedan to Japan this year
feed.summary: Honda Motor will begin importing US-produced vehicles, including an SUV and a luxury sedan under the Acura brand, to Japan in the second half of 2026. This move follows a simplified certification process for U.S.-made cars due to a recent tariff agreement between the two countries. Toyota is also planning to import US-made models into Japan starting in 2026.

feed.title: Honda to launch China-focused e:NS2 in Thailand and Japan
feed.summary: Honda is expanding the availability of its China-focused electric vehicle, the e:NS2, to Thailand and Japan. In Thailand, it will be called the e:N2 and is set to launch at the Bangkok International Motor Show with a price of around 1.4 million baht, while in Japan, it will revive the Insight nameplate with pre-orders beginning in March. Both versions will be manufactured by Dongfeng-Honda in China and will offer similar specifications, including a 150 kW motor and a 68.8 kWh battery.

feed.title: Titan International, Astronics, Installed Building Products, SolarEdge, and SPX Technologies Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Several stocks, including Titan International, Astronics, Installed Building Products, SolarEdge, and SPX Technologies, experienced declines due to escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. These tensions led to a surge in oil prices, reigniting inflation concerns and impacting the broader market. The instability complicates monetary policy for the Federal Reserve and raises fears of increased fuel costs and reduced consumer spending.

feed.title: Green Plains, Inc. $GPRE Stock Position Lifted by Citigroup Inc.
feed.summary: Citigroup Inc. significantly increased its stake in Green Plains, Inc. (NASDAQ:GPRE) during the third quarter, acquiring an additional 331,326 shares to hold a total of 457,280 shares worth approximately $4.02 million. This move comes despite Green Plains reporting mixed financial results, including an EPS beat but a substantial year-over-year revenue decrease and negative net margin. Analyst ratings for GPRE are varied, with an average "Hold" rating, though some firms have recently upgraded the stock and raised price targets.

feed.title: FuelCell Energy, Advanced Drainage, Northwest Pipe, Bel Fuse, and JELD-WEN Shares Plummet, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of FuelCell Energy, Advanced Drainage, Northwest Pipe, Bel Fuse, and JELD-WEN plummeted following escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which led to a surge in oil prices and renewed inflation concerns. The broader market experienced a significant sell-off, impacting various sectors as rising energy costs complicate future monetary policy decisions. The article highlights the individual stock drops and briefly reviews JELD-WEN's recent financial performance, noting its operational strengths despite revenue decline and cautious outlook.

feed.title: Petróleo Brasileiro S.A. (PBR): Deep Value Vertically Integrated Energy Platform
feed.summary: Petrobras (PBR) is highlighted as a deeply undervalued, vertically integrated energy giant with dominant offshore oil production and refining assets. Despite strong cash flows and competitive production costs, it trades below international oil majors, largely due to concerns over government ownership and political influence. The article suggests PBR offers a compelling deep value opportunity for investors comfortable with emerging-market energy exposure.

feed.title: Allient, Shoals, Pangaea, United Parcel Service, and Ducommun Stocks Trade Down, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Stocks for Allient, Shoals, Pangaea, United Parcel Service, and Ducommun traded down following escalated geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which caused oil prices to soar and reignited inflation concerns. The market-wide sell-off led the Dow Jones Industrial Average to fall over 1,000 points. Ducommun, despite a quarterly revenue miss but beat on profit estimates, saw its shares drop, though its long-term performance remains strong.

feed.title: Honda to import China-made EVs to bolster Japanese lineup
feed.summary: Honda Motor is set to become the first Japanese automaker to sell China-made electric vehicles in its domestic market, with sales starting as early as this spring. The initiative aims to offer more EV options to Japanese consumers, beginning with models like the Honda e:NP2. This strategic move highlights increasing integration between Chinese manufacturing and the global automotive market.

feed.title: ETF Roundup: 3 New ETF Launches in February to Watch
feed.summary: February 2026 saw over 50 new ETF launches, with notable additions to income and nuclear ETF segments. This article highlights three specific funds: the VanEck Communication Services TruSector ETF (TRUC) for concentrated communications exposure, the ALPS Nautilus SMR, Nuclear & Technology ETF (SMRF) for nuclear tech and AI exposure, and the NEOS Boosted Nasdaq-100 High Income ETF (XQQI) for high-income generation from Nasdaq-100. These ETFs offer diverse investment opportunities, competitive fees, and tradability for various investor goals.

feed.title: Gran Tierra Energy (NYSEAM:GTE) Q4 Loss Surge Pressures Bullish Profitability Narrative
feed.summary: Gran Tierra Energy reported a significant Q4 2025 loss of US$3.99 EPS and US$141.1 million net loss, which puts pressure on the bullish narrative of future profitability through production growth and lower operating costs. Despite proponents highlighting operational efficiencies and increased production, the company's financial results demonstrate widening quarterly losses, reaching US$193.12 million by Q4 2025 on a trailing basis. The stock is currently priced below industry peers based on sales, with bullish investors anticipating future profitability and trading below DCF fair value, while critics remain concerned about volatility, funding costs, and regional risks impacting earnings.

feed.title: Gas and Liquid Handling Stocks Q4 Recap: Benchmarking Gorman-Rupp (NYSE:GRC)
feed.summary: This article provides a Q4 recap of the gas and liquid handling sector, highlighting Gorman-Rupp's performance. It details Gorman-Rupp's strong financial results, including record sales and adjusted EPS, and compares its performance with other key players like Atmus Filtration Technologies, Chart, Ingersoll Rand, and Flowserve. The sector generally saw satisfactory results, with revenues beating estimates and stable share prices.

feed.title: Trinity Industries Announces Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Trinity Industries, Inc. has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.31 per share, reflecting an annualized yield of 3.7%. The transportation industry player has consistently increased its dividend for 16 years, demonstrating financial stability and commitment to shareholder returns. The dividend will be paid on April 30, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 15, 2026, with an ex-dividend date of April 15, 2026.

feed.title: Valero Energy Corporation Announces Pricing of Notes Offering
feed.summary: Valero Energy Corporation announced the pricing of a public offering of $850 million aggregate principal amount of 5.150% Senior Notes due 2036. The offering is expected to close on March 10, 2026, with net proceeds primarily intended for general corporate purposes, including the repayment or redemption of existing debentures and senior notes. SMBC Nikko Securities America, Inc., Citigroup Global Markets Inc., MUFG Securities Americas Inc., and Wells Fargo Securities, LLC are acting as joint book-running managers for the offering.

feed.title: RAM Mounts Partners with Tyler Technologies to Support Smarter, More Durable Onboard Student Transportation Technology
feed.summary: RAM Mounts has partnered with Tyler Technologies to provide more durable and reliable onboard technology for student transportation. This collaboration combines Tyler Technologies' student transportation software with RAM Mounts' rugged mounting solutions, ensuring that devices like Samsung enterprise-grade tablets perform reliably in demanding school bus environments. The partnership aims to enhance safety, efficiency, and scalability for school districts by providing secure, adjustable, and vibration-damping mounting options for mobile devices.

feed.title: Nio's EV motor production tops 2 million on ES8 demand
feed.summary: Nio has reached a significant manufacturing milestone, producing its 2 millionth electric drive system at its Hefei plant, with the latest unit destined for an ES8 SUV. This achievement, coming just 23 months after hitting the 1 million mark, underscores Nio's accelerated industrial manufacturing capabilities. The strong performance of the ES8, which accounts for over 50% of Nio's monthly deliveries, has been crucial in improving the company's financial outlook, with a forecast for its first-ever quarterly profit in Q4 2025.

feed.title: New Tennessee Valley Authority board chair chosen after Bill Renick resigns
feed.summary: The Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) Board of Directors has appointed Mitch Graves as its new Chair, effective until April 1, 2027, with Jeff Hagood serving as Chair-elect afterward. This change follows the resignation of former chair Bill Renick on February 24, and the new chairs were confirmed by the Senate in December 2025. The board recently voted to continue operating all coal-fired power plants, reversing earlier plans.

feed.title: Insiders Are Buying These 3 Energy Stocks
feed.summary: Insiders have recently purchased shares in three energy companies: Texas Pacific Land (TPL), DT Midstream (DTM), and CMS Energy (CMS). These transactions are often seen by investors as a longer-term indicator, though they should not be relied upon for short-term performance given the differing holding periods and strict rules for insiders. All three companies have shown strong performance in 2026, with CMS and DTM recently raising dividends and TPL exploring data center opportunities.

feed.title: Via Renewables reports $463.5M revenue and $2.70 EPS in 2025 10-K
feed.summary: Via Renewables announced its 2025 fiscal year results, reporting $463.5 million in total revenues, a 16% increase, and a net income per share of $2.70. Despite revenue growth driven by higher volumes and customer expansion, net income and operating income saw a decline compared to 2024, primarily due to increased retail cost of revenues and lower segment margins. The company also highlighted significant customer expansion, with Retail Customer Equivalents (RCEs) reaching 421,000.

feed.title: W&T Offshore declares $0.01 quarterly dividend for Q1 2026
feed.summary: W&T Offshore (NYSE:WTI) has announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.01 per share for the first quarter of 2026, payable on March 26, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 19, 2026. This marks the company's ninth consecutive quarterly dividend since November 2023. The independent oil and natural gas producer operates in the Gulf of Mexico, holding interests in 50 fields.

feed.title: Trio Petroleum Expands At-The-Market Equity Offering Capacity
feed.summary: Trio Petroleum Corp. (TPET) announced an expansion of its at-the-market (ATM) equity offering capacity. Initially set up in January 2026 for $3.6 million, the program was progressively increased through amendments in March, reaching a maximum aggregate offering size of $17.377 million, with $4 million in new shares currently available for placement after approximately $13.377 million in sales. This expansion reflects the company's strategy for flexible, incremental access to equity capital.

feed.title: Scorpio Tankers Sells Three Product Tankers and Secures Long-Term Charters in Fleet Overhaul
feed.summary: Scorpio Tankers (STNG) is undergoing a significant fleet overhaul, selling three 2015-built product tankers for $130 million, with deals closing in Q1 or Q2 2026. Simultaneously, the company has secured long-term time charters for two LR2 tankers, enhancing cash flow visibility and reducing debt. This strategy aims to optimize its asset base, renew its fleet with newer, more efficient vessels, and strengthen its financial position in a dynamic product tanker market.

feed.title: Genesis Energy completes $750 million senior notes offering due 2034
feed.summary: Genesis Energy, L.P. (NYSE:GEL) has successfully closed a $750 million offering of 6.750% senior notes due 2034. The company plans to use the net proceeds to redeem its outstanding 7.75% senior notes due 2028 and for general partnership purposes, including potentially repaying part of its revolving credit facility. The new notes are unsecured obligations and will mature on May 15, 2034, with interest payable semi-annually.

feed.title: Is VinFast’s Three-Brand EV Strategy Reshaping the Investment Case For VinFast Auto (VFS)?
feed.summary: VinFast has restructured into three distinct automotive brands: VF mass-market EVs, Lac Hong ultra-luxury, and Green commercial mobility, with plans to launch Lac Hong models in 2027. This strategy aims to elevate VinFast's market position, particularly through the ultra-luxury segment, by combining cultural narratives with high-end technology. While the new structure clarifies portfolio roles, the immediate investment narrative for VinFast still centers on liquidity, significant cash burn, and the challenge of achieving sustainable gross margins, despite ambitious revenue and earnings projections for 2028.

feed.title: Greenfire Resources Hits New 52-Week High at USD 6.50
feed.summary: Greenfire Resources has achieved a new 52-week high of USD 6.50, significantly up from its low of USD 3.39, reflecting a year-over-year performance of 10.21%. The microcap company, with a market capitalization of USD 762 million in the oil industry, has a P/E ratio of 4.00 and a low debt-to-equity ratio of 0.24. Despite a negative return on equity, this recent price movement indicates increased market activity for the stock.

feed.title: Ranger Energy Services (NYSE:RNGR) Announces Earnings Results
feed.summary: Ranger Energy Services (NYSE:RNGR) shared its Q4 earnings, reporting an EPS of $0.14, missing consensus estimates by $0.06, but revenue slightly exceeded expectations at $142.2 million. The company highlighted smooth integration of American Well Services and progress with its Echo hybrid-electric rig program. While 2025 saw strong revenue, adjusted EBITDA, and free cash flow generation with significant share repurchases, management anticipates flat activity in 2026 due to factors like winter storms and working capital builds.

feed.title: Ranger Energy Services, Inc. to Issue Quarterly Dividend of $0.06 (NYSE:RNGR)
feed.summary: Ranger Energy Services, Inc. announced a quarterly dividend of $0.06 per share, payable on April 6th to shareholders of record as of March 20th. This translates to an annualized yield of 1.4% and a payout ratio of 23.1%, indicating the dividend is well-covered by earnings. Following the announcement, RNGR shares experienced a 3% decline.

feed.title: Moog, Comfort Systems, Viavi Solutions, nLIGHT, and Sterling Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Moog, Comfort Systems, Viavi Solutions, nLIGHT, and Sterling experienced significant drops due to escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. The conflict, particularly involving the U.S. and Iran, caused oil prices to surge and reignited inflation concerns, leading to a broad-based market sell-off. This situation puts pressure on the Federal Reserve and could delay potential interest rate cuts.

feed.title: Pembina approves new pipeline expansions in Western Canada
feed.summary: Pembina Pipeline Corporation has approved C$425 million (US$315 million) in new pipeline expansions in Western Canada, including a 95-km Birch-to-Taylor pipeline and upgrades to the Taylor-to-Gordondale Expansion. These projects aim to support growing energy production in British Columbia and Alberta. Additionally, Pembina has fully contracted its 1.5 million tonnes per year capacity share at the Cedar LNG project.

feed.title: Delek Logistics Partners, LP 2025 Form 10-K Available on Website
feed.summary: Delek Logistics Partners, LP announced that its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, was filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on February 27, 2026. This report, including audited financial statements, is accessible on the Partnership’s website, and limited partners can request a physical copy. Delek Logistics Partners, LP is a midstream energy master limited partnership involved in gathering, pipeline transportation, and other services for crude oil and refined products.

feed.title: Delek Logistics puts 2025 annual report and audited financials online
feed.summary: Delek Logistics Partners, LP (NYSE: DKL) announced the filing of its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on February 27, 2026. The report, which includes audited financial statements, is accessible on the Partnership’s website. Limited partners can also request a free printed copy.

feed.title: Nuclear regulator grants licence for Nexgen to build uranium mine in northern Sask.
feed.summary: The Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission has granted NexGen Energy Ltd. a licence to build its Rook I uranium mine in the southern Athabasca Basin, making it the second such approval in Saskatchewan this year. The project, located 150 kilometres north of La Loche, is expected to create 459 full-time jobs and produce up to 30 million pounds of uranium annually over its 24-year lifespan, with construction slated to begin this summer. NexGen has secured benefit agreements with several Indigenous communities, which its vice-president highlighted as crucial for the project's rapid approval and expected to bring significant economic opportunities to the region.

feed.title: Genesis Energy, L.P. Announces Early Results and Initial Settlement Date for Tender Offer for Any and All of Its 7.750% Senior Notes Due 2028
feed.summary: Genesis Energy, L.P. announced the early results and initial settlement date for its cash tender offer for its 7.750% senior notes due 2028. The company received tenders for an aggregate principal amount of $415,942,000 by the early tender deadline of March 3, 2026, with an initial settlement date set for March 5, 2026. The tender offer will remain open until March 18, 2026, and holders tendering after the early deadline will receive a lower consideration.

feed.title: Hunton Advises Underwriters on Genesis Energy, L.P.’s $750M Senior Notes Offering
feed.summary: Hunton Andrews Kurth LLP advised the underwriters for Genesis Energy, L.P.'s $750 million public offering of 6.750% Senior Notes due 2034. Genesis Energy is a diversified midstream energy master limited partnership based in Houston, Texas. The Hunton team included corporate, tax, and regulatory specialists.

feed.title: SandRidge Energy 2025: Revenue $156.4M, EPS $1.90
feed.summary: SandRidge Energy reported strong financial results for 2025, with revenues reaching $156.4 million and diluted EPS of $1.90. This growth was attributed to higher natural gas prices and increased volumes from their Cherokee development. The company also saw significant improvements in net income and operating income, supported by derivative gains and robust realized prices.

feed.title: Stabilis Solutions 10-K: Revenue $68.25M, EPS $(0.07)
feed.summary: Stabilis Solutions (SLNG) reported fiscal-year revenues of $68.245 million and a net loss of $(0.07) per share for the period ending March 5, 2026. This decline was attributed to the conclusion of multi-year contracts and reduced LNG volumes. Despite the financial downturn, the company is progressing with strategic projects including new liquefaction facilities and has secured significant long-term bunkering and data center LNG contracts.

feed.title: Carbon credits, jet-fuel plan power Gevo’s record ethanol year
feed.summary: Gevo (NASDAQ: GEVO) announced strong financial results for Q4 and full-year 2025, reporting a positive operating cash flow of $20 million in Q4 and $161 million in total revenue for the year. The company achieved record low-carbon ethanol production at Gevo North Dakota and successfully monetized carbon credits, while also advancing its Alcohol-to-Jet (ATJ) SAF project. These achievements were supported by significant production tax credit sales and debt consolidation efforts.

feed.title: BCX ETF Price at $12.41 — 5.34% NAV Discount, 6.55% Yield, Qatar's LNG Shutdown Hitting Every Position It Owns
feed.summary: The BCX ETF is trading at $12.41, representing a 5.34% discount to its NAV of $13.48 and offering a 6.55% monthly yield. The fund's performance is significantly influenced by current commodity shocks, particularly the Qatar LNG shutdown, which is driving up prices for crude oil, natural gas, and precious metals. BCX's exposure to major oil companies and gold miners, combined with its covered call strategy, positions it well to capitalize on the ongoing commodity bull market.

feed.title: VAALCO Schedules Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Earnings Release and Conference Call
feed.summary: VAALCO Energy (NYSE: EGY) announced it will release its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 earnings on Thursday, March 12, 2026, after the close of the NYSE. The company will host a conference call to discuss these results on Friday, March 13, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. Central Time. Dial-in details are provided for US, UK, and international participants, and the call will also be webcast on VAALCO's website.

feed.title: How Much Is the 2026 Lexus ES?
feed.summary: The 2026 Lexus ES offers both plug-in hybrid (PHEV) and pure electric vehicle (EV) powertrains in an all-new generation. Interestingly, the EV model has a lower starting price than the PHEV. Lexus has released preliminary drivetrain specifications, including horsepower and estimated ranges, but is holding some details until closer to the sales launch later in 2026.

feed.title: Aecom Technology Declares Quarterly Dividend, Signals Ongoing Confidence
feed.summary: AECOM (ACM) shareholders approved all board nominees, auditor ratification, and executive compensation at their annual meeting on March 3, 2026. The company's board subsequently declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.31 per share, payable on April 17, 2026, signaling confidence in its financial health and commitment to shareholder returns. Spark, TipRanks' AI Analyst, rates ACM as Neutral, noting strong financial performance offset by mixed technicals and a relatively high valuation.

feed.title: Cheniere Announces Pricing of $1 Billion Senior Notes due 2036 and $750 Million Senior Notes due 2056
feed.summary: Cheniere Energy Partners, L.P. (CQY) announced the pricing of two offerings of senior notes: $1 billion notes due 2036 and $750 million notes due 2056. These offerings are expected to close on March 14, 2026, and the proceeds will be used to prepay a portion of CQY’s existing term loan facility.

feed.title: Oil & Gas Commission approves Tetra Technologies additional compensation rate for lithium
feed.summary: The Arkansas Department of Energy and Environment, Oil and Gas Commission, has approved an additional lithium compensation rate of 2.5% for TETRA Technologies, Inc. This approval, consistent with previous decisions for other companies, applies to lithium production from TETRA's Evergreen Brine Production Unit and Evergreen Brine Expansion Unit in south Arkansas. The decision aims to further position Arkansas as a leader in lithium production, providing clear compensation rates and encouraging extraction methods with minimal environmental impact.

feed.title: Trinity Industries, Inc. Announces Quarterly Dividend of $0.31 (NYSE:TRN)
feed.summary: Trinity Industries, Inc. (NYSE:TRN) declared a quarterly dividend of $0.31 per share, offering a 3.7% yield, marking its 16th consecutive year of dividend increases. Despite a 2.9% year-over-year revenue decline to $611.2M, the company reported an EPS of $2.31, beating estimates, and set its FY2026 EPS guidance between $1.85 and $2.10. Shares dropped 3.2% to $33.15, resulting in a market cap of $2.65 billion and a P/E ratio of 10.76.

feed.title: Top Analyst Reports for Exxon Mobil, Palantir & AstraZeneca
feed.summary: This article features Zacks' top analyst reports for March 5, 2026, highlighting major stocks like Exxon Mobil, Palantir Technologies, and AstraZeneca, alongside micro-cap companies Gencor Industries and Hour Loop. The reports delve into each company's recent performance, strategic initiatives, financial health, and potential headwinds. Key insights include Exxon Mobil's production growth, Palantir's AI strategy, AstraZeneca's strong drug pipeline, Gencor's balance sheet, and Hour Loop's consumer traction despite liquidity concerns.

feed.title: Is Chevron Corporation (CVX) A Good Stock To Buy?
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on Chevron Corporation (CVX), highlighting its resilience despite volatile crude prices due to steady oil and gas demand. Key points include its strong cash flow, disciplined capital allocation, and the strategic acquisition of a stake in the Stabroek block, which is expected to boost production growth. The company has also demonstrated commitment to shareholder returns through increased dividends, positioning it as an appealing long-term investment.

feed.title: JB Hunt Announces Dual Listing on New Nasdaq Texas
feed.summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc. has secured a dual listing for its common stock on the new Nasdaq Texas exchange, with trading starting on March 6, 2026. This move allows JB Hunt to maintain its primary listing on the Nasdaq Global Select Market while leveraging Texas's economic growth and deepening its operational footprint in the state. The company aims to access additional liquidity and global investors without disrupting existing shareholders.

feed.title: F-150 Lightning Outsold Tesla's Cybertruck, But Ford CEO Says He 'Would've Done It Differently'
feed.summary: Ford CEO Jim Farley expressed regrets about the company's electric vehicle strategy, particularly with the F-150 Lightning, despite it outselling Tesla's Cybertruck in 2025. Farley acknowledged that Ford lost money on each electric pickup sold and plans to focus on profitability with hybrids and gas-powered vehicles, while later introducing a more affordable EV platform. He also highlighted China's rapid advancements in the automotive sector and Ford's new focus on vehicles as "entertainment space."

feed.title: ConocoPhillips close to settlement with state of Louisiana over coastal marsh damage
feed.summary: Louisiana Governor Jeff Landry announced that the state is nearing a settlement with ConocoPhillips regarding lawsuits alleging the company caused damage to coastal marshes and land loss. The proposed settlement would grant the state access to 150,000 acres of ConocoPhillips' land, unlock hundreds of millions in coastal investment, and facilitate a 94-mile coastal land project. This agreement is expected to accelerate crucial coastal restoration efforts and follows similar settlements with other energy companies.

feed.title: Gevo (NASDAQ:GEVO) Releases Quarterly Earnings Results, Beats Expectations By $0.01 EPS
feed.summary: Gevo (NASDAQ:GEVO) announced its quarterly earnings, reporting an EPS of ($0.02), which beat analysts' estimates by $0.01, and revenue of $45.35 million, surpassing the consensus of $43.52 million. Despite beating expectations, the company remains unprofitable with a negative net margin of 37.33% and negative return on equity. The stock traded down by 1% following the announcement, and recent insider selling, including by the CFO, has been noted, while analysts maintain a "Hold" consensus rating.

feed.title: Argus Sees Enbridge (ENB) Well Positioned, Raises Price Target to $59
feed.summary: Argus Research has raised its price target for Enbridge Inc. (ENB) to $59 from $54 while reiterating a Buy rating, citing the company's strong positioning with limited exposure to commodity prices and a robust network of pipelines and utility businesses. Enbridge reported another year of record performance in Q4 2025, exceeding financial guidance for the 20th consecutive year and increasing its dividend for the 31st straight year, maintaining its status as a dividend aristocrat. The company's stable debt-to-EBITDA ratio also supports its strong investment-grade credit profile and expansion capacity.

feed.title: Granite Ridge (NYSE: GRNT) grows 2025 output and issues 2026 production guidance
feed.summary: Granite Ridge Resources, Inc. (NYSE: GRNT) reported significant production growth in 2025, with total output increasing 28% to 31,984 Boe per day and proved reserves rising to 62,347 MBoe. Despite a Q4 net loss, the company achieved a full-year net income of $24.4 million and Adjusted EBITDAX of $315.0 million, maintaining solid liquidity with a Net Debt to Adjusted EBITDAX ratio of 1.2x. For 2026, Granite Ridge forecasts average production of 34,000–36,000 Boe per day and expects capital expenditures between $320–$360 million, reflecting a continued focus on disciplined capital allocation and controlled development through Operated Partnerships.

feed.title: Scotiabank Raises Dominion Energy (D) Price Target Following Solid Execution
feed.summary: Scotiabank has increased its price target for Dominion Energy (NYSE: D) to $67 from $63, while reiterating a Sector Perform rating, after the company demonstrated strong execution and commitment to management. Dominion Energy is expanding its capital spending to meet rising electricity demand, particularly from data centers, and now projects $64.7 billion in investments between 2026 and 2030. The company, which provides regulated electricity and natural gas services across several states, has contracted significant data center capacity from major tech firms.

feed.title: Mercury Systems, Martin Marietta Materials, Quanex, Keysight, and Fortune Brands Shares Plummet, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Mercury Systems, Martin Marietta Materials, Quanex, Keysight, and Fortune Brands plummeted following escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which caused oil prices to soar and reignited inflation concerns. The Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped significantly as the conflict in the Middle East disrupted global energy markets. The broad market sell-off affected multiple sectors, leading to sharp declines for these specific companies.

feed.title: Gevo Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results and Provides Business Update
feed.summary: Gevo, Inc. announced strong financial results for Q4 and full year 2025, achieving positive cash flow from operations and a significant increase in cash, cash equivalents, and restricted cash. The company reported $45 million in revenue for Q4 and $161 million for the full year, along with positive non-GAAP Adjusted EBITDA for the third consecutive quarter. Gevo also highlighted record production at its North Dakota facility, monetization of carbon credits, and progress on its Alcohol-to-Jet (ATJ) Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) project.

feed.title: J.B. Hunt Announces Anticipated Dual Listing on Nasdaq Texas Stock Exchange
feed.summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc. (Nasdaq: JBHT) announced its approval for a dual listing on Nasdaq Texas, LLC. This move provides J.B. Hunt with access to additional liquidity and global investors, aligning with Texas's economic growth. The company expects trading on Nasdaq Texas to begin on March 6, 2026, under the ticker “JBHT.”

feed.title: PSEG (PEG) and Southern (SO) Upgraded, Duke Energy (DUK) Downgra
feed.summary: Evercore ISI has upgraded Public Service Enterprise Group (PEG) and Southern Company (SO) to "Outperform" with price targets of $96 and $111, respectively, citing PSEG's regulated earnings mix and favorable New Jersey regulatory environment, and Southern's robust capital plan. Conversely, Duke Energy (DUK) was downgraded to "In Line" with a $139 price target following a significant stock appreciation. PSEG's financial health shows a strong operating margin but concerns with a low current ratio and high debt-to-equity, placing it in the Altman Z-Score distress zone.

feed.title: ESS Tech (NYSE: GWH) trims 2025 losses but leans on new financings
feed.summary: ESS Tech, Inc. (NYSE: GWH) reported a net loss of $63.4 million for 2025, which is an improvement from the $86.2 million loss in 2024, alongside a 38% improvement in Adjusted EBITDA loss to $44.3 million. Despite reduced operating expenses and early-stage commercial revenue of $1.6 million, the company experienced a significant decline in working capital to $1.0 million but bolstered its liquidity through new financings, including a $40 million deal with Yorkville and a $15 million registered direct offering in early 2026. ESS Tech is focusing on strategic projects and intellectual property acquisition to scale its long-duration energy storage systems, emphasizing execution and commercial positioning in key markets.

feed.title: Faraday Future director Chui Tin Mok to step down from board, remain executive
feed.summary: Chui Tin Mok, a director on Faraday Future Intelligent Electric Inc.'s board, will resign to focus on business in the Middle East. He will continue as an executive officer and Head of FF Middle East. This decision follows recent company developments, including an increase in authorized shares, a strategic investment from Aixcrypto Holdings Inc., and the issuance of new preferred stock.

feed.title: Why American Airlines (AAL) Shares Are Sliding Today
feed.summary: American Airlines (AAL) shares fell 5.4% due to an analyst downgrade from Rothschild Redburn, citing surging jet fuel prices caused by escalating Middle East conflict. The firm lowered its rating to Neutral from Buy and cut the price target. Concerns arose from the airline's lack of fuel hedging, large debt, and rising domestic capacity, all of which could impact future profitability.

feed.title: Colombian billionaire buys GeoPark stake, eyes Venezuela
feed.summary: Colombian billionaire Jaime Gilinski, through his affiliate Colden Investments, has acquired a 20% stake in South America-focused oil and gas producer GeoPark for approximately US$107 million, making Colden the largest shareholder. This investment aims to fund acquisitions, support development in Colombia's and Argentina's Vaca Muerta shale formation, and maintain financial flexibility. GeoPark is also considering future opportunities in Venezuela if market conditions become favorable.

feed.title: Primoris, Corning, Plug Power, Construction Partners, and Dycom Shares Plummet, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Several stocks, including Primoris, Corning, Plug Power, Construction Partners, and Dycom, experienced a significant drop in value due to escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which sent oil prices soaring and rekindled inflation fears. The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell over 1,000 points as the conflict disrupted global energy markets. This broad-based sell-off impacted multiple sectors and raised concerns about future monetary policy and consumer spending.

feed.title: Titan International, Astronics, Installed Building Products, SolarEdge, and SPX Technologies Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Titan International, Astronics, Installed Building Products, SolarEdge, and SPX Technologies fell significantly in response to escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which sent oil prices soaring. The conflict, particularly involving the U.S. and Iran and its impact on shipping routes, has reignited inflation concerns and caused the Dow Jones Industrial Average to drop over 1,000 points. SolarEdge, an example of a volatile stock, saw an 8% decline, and its shares have been highly reactive to similar news in the past.

feed.title: NexGen Energy Ltd. (TSE:NXE) Receives Consensus Rating of "Buy" from Analysts
feed.summary: NexGen Energy (TSE:NXE) has received a consensus "Buy" rating from five brokerages, with an average one-year price target of C$17.56. Several analysts recently raised their price targets, including Stifel Nicolaus to C$22 and TD Securities and UBS Group to C$20. The company, focused on its Rook I uranium project, reported negative EPS of C($0.06) last quarter and has a market capitalization of C$10.98 billion.

feed.title: Seadrill raised from Sell rating at Citi on improved visibility into 2026-27 (SDRL:NYSE)
feed.summary: Citi upgraded Seadrill (SDRL) to Neutral/High Risk from Sell/High Risk, increasing its price target from $32 to $46. The upgrade reflects improving end-market demand for offshore drilling, strengthening backlog coverage, and a more constructive medium-term outlook for the company. Seadrill's stock rose 2.5% following the announcement.

feed.title: KLX Energy Services Announces 2025 Full Year and Fourth Quarter Earnings Release and Conference Call Schedule
feed.summary: KLX Energy Services Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: KLXE) has announced it will report its 2025 full-year and fourth-quarter financial results on Thursday, March 12, 2026. The company will host a conference call at 10:00 a.m. Eastern Time for investors and the public. Details for accessing the live call or webcast, and replay information, are provided.

feed.title: Why AAR (AIR) Shares Are Plunging Today
feed.summary: Shares of AAR CORP (NYSE:AIR) fell 7.3% following reports of rising oil prices due to geopolitical conflict, which is expected to negatively impact the aviation industry. Higher fuel costs directly pressure airline finances and, consequently, companies providing maintenance and aftermarket services. Despite today's drop, AAR has seen a significant increase of 31.2% this year and is trading near its 52-week high.

feed.title: KLX Energy to detail 2025 results in March 12 earnings call
feed.summary: KLX Energy Services (NASDAQ: KLXE) has announced it will report its 2025 full year and fourth-quarter financial results on Thursday, March 12, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. ET. The results will be released before a live conference call, which investors can access via dial-in or webcast. A replay will be available until March 26, 2026, and questions for management can be submitted via email in advance.

feed.title: Dorchester Minerals: Price Recovery Makes Sense, Valuation Is Still An Entry Position
feed.summary: Dorchester Minerals LP (DMLP) remains a buy due to its robust liquidity, high 11.3% yield, and resilient low-cost business model. The company's Q4 2025 revenue saw a 5.4% year-over-year rebound, supported by increased lease bonuses and stable operating expenses. Despite geopolitical risks, DMLP's diversified mineral rights and non-capital-intensive approach mitigate volatility, presenting an attractive valuation with a new target price of $38.88.

feed.title: Why Alto Ingredients Stock Is Surging 50% Today
feed.summary: Alto Ingredients Inc (NASDAQ:ALTO) shares surged over 50% on Thursday following a strong fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 earnings report. The renewable fuels producer reported a significant profit turnaround, with net income swinging to $21.5 million in Q4 2025 from a loss the previous year. This positive financial performance reinforces the company's turnaround and has led to a new 52-week high for its stock.

feed.title: Get Up to $2,500 Back on 2025 Lexus ES as New 2026 Model Looms
feed.summary: Lexus is offering up to $2,500 in dealer cash for the 2025 Lexus ES models as a redesigned 2026 model with new powertrain options is anticipated. This incentive is available until March 31 and applies to all three 2025 ES powertrains: the ES 250, ES 350, and ES 300h. Shoppers should check with their local dealerships to determine how much of this unadvertised dealer cash will be passed on to them.

feed.title: Titan International, Astronics, Installed Building Products, SolarEdge, and SPX Technologies Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Titan International, Astronics, Installed Building Products, SolarEdge, and SPX Technologies fell significantly following an escalation of geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which sent oil prices soaring. This rise in energy costs has reignited inflation concerns and prompted a broad market sell-off, impacting various sectors and putting pressure on the Federal Reserve's monetary policy decisions. SolarEdge, in particular, saw an 8% drop, highlighting market volatility in response to the news.

feed.title: Titan International, Astronics, Installed Building Products, SolarEdge, and SPX Technologies Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Titan International, Astronics, Installed Building Products, SolarEdge, and SPX Technologies experienced declines following an escalation of geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which sent oil prices soaring. The rise in Brent crude towards $85 sparked renewed inflation fears and impacted various sectors, particularly those sensitive to fuel costs and consumer spending. This market reaction put pressure on the Federal Reserve regarding future monetary policy and potential interest rate cuts.

feed.title: SM Energy Co. Hits Day High with 9.47% Surge in Stock Price
feed.summary: SM Energy Co. recently experienced a 9.47% surge in its stock price, reaching an intraday high of USD 23.24 on February 27, 2026. While the company has shown strong short-term growth with a 19.97% increase over the past month and 23.69% year-to-date, it faces longer-term challenges, including significant declines over the past one and three years. Despite these fluctuating performances, SM Energy Co. exhibits efficient management with a high return on capital employed and a stable financial position characterized by a low debt-to-equity ratio and healthy long-term operating profit growth.

feed.title: Assessing XPO (XPO) Valuation After Tonnage Growth Returns And Efficiency Gains
feed.summary: XPO (XPO) has seen a return to positive year-over-year tonnage growth in its North American LTL segment and significant share price momentum. Despite this, the most popular narrative suggests XPO's fair value is $156.08, below its current trading price of $214.46, indicating it may be overvalued as the market has already factored in future growth. Analysts have a consensus price target of $138.375, with a range from $84.0 to $156.0, and there are execution risks if freight demand remains soft or labor costs increase.

feed.title: J.B. Hunt Announces Anticipated Dual Listing on Nasdaq Texas Stock Exchange
feed.summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc. announced its common stock has been approved for a dual listing on Nasdaq Texas, LLC, commencing March 6, 2026, under the symbol "JBHT." This move aligns with Texas's economic growth and provides access to additional liquidity and global investors, without affecting existing trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market. President and CEO Shelley Simpson highlighted the opportunity for future growth and continued investment in the region.

feed.title: American Airlines, StubHub among stocks hit by analyst downgrades
feed.summary: Several US-listed stocks, including American Airlines, Grocery Outlet, StubHub, Edison International, and Tencent Music, experienced analyst downgrades and price target cuts. American Airlines was downgraded to Neutral with a reduced price target due to concerns about its higher per-unit costs and greater leverage compared to its peers. The downgrades have placed pressure on these companies across various sectors, with multiple price targets now close to recent trading levels.
